content_sections.1 Introduction: Pseudo-scalar meson distribution amplitudes (DAs) П•MвҖӢ(x)subscriptitalic-П•рқ‘Җрқ‘ҘphiM(x) of a meson Mрқ‘ҖM with valence quark qвҖӢqВҜвҖІрқ‘һsuperscriptВҜрқ‘һвҖІqbarqprime describe the probability amplitude of finding the meson moving on the light-cone in its minimal Fock state, a quark-antiquark pair |qвҖӢqВҜвҖІвҹ©ketрқ‘һsuperscriptВҜрқ‘һвҖІ|qbarqprime each carrying a momentum fraction of xрқ‘Ҙx and 1вҲ’x1рқ‘Ҙ1-x, respectively. They are important universal inputs to hard exclusive processes and form factorscitebib.bib1, bib.bib2, bib.bib3, bib.bib4 at large momentum transfer Q2вү«ОӣQCD2much-greater-thansuperscriptрқ‘„2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2Q2ggLambdarm QCD2. Especially, the kaon DA gains particular interest because of its relevance to CP violating processes in heavy meson decays, such as BвҶ’ПҖвҖӢKвҶ’рқҗөрқңӢрқҗҫBtopi K and DвҶ’KвҖӢKВҜвҶ’рқҗ·рқҗҫВҜрқҗҫDto KbarK citebib.bib5, bib.bib6, bib.bib7, which are important probes to new physics beyond Standard Model. So far, the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of meson DAs are only weakly constrained by experimental measurements of form factorscitebib.bib4, bib.bib8, bib.bib9, bib.bib10, thus many model-dependent theoretical calculations are suggested, providing very different shapes with different model assumptionscitebib.bib11, bib.bib12, bib.bib13. A direct xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence calculation from first principle methods, such as lattice QCD, could improve our understanding of the meson structures. The meson DAs can be defined as light-like correlations, iвҖӢfMвҖӢП•MвҖӢ(x,Ој)=вҲ«dвҖӢО·вҲ’2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢP+вҖӢО·вҲ’вҖӢвҹЁ0|qВҜвҖІвҖӢвҖӢОі5вҖӢОі+вҖӢWвҖӢ(0,О·вҲ’)вҖӢqвҖӢ(О·вҲ’)|MвҖӢ(P)вҹ©,рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘“рқ‘Җsubscriptitalic-П•рқ‘Җрқ‘ҘрқңҮрқ‘‘superscriptрқңӮ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқңӮquantum-operator-product0superscriptВҜрқ‘һвҖІ0subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫрқ‘Ҡ0superscriptрқңӮрқ‘һsuperscriptрқңӮрқ‘Җрқ‘ғdisplaystyleifMphiM(x,mu)=intfracdeta-2pieixP+eta- 0|overlineqprimegamma5gamma+W(0,eta-)q(eta-)|M(P), where fMsubscriptрқ‘“рқ‘ҖfM is the mesonвҖҷs decay constant, WвҖӢ(0,О·вҲ’)=рқ’«вҖӢexpвҒЎ[вҲ’iвҖӢgвҖӢвҲ«0О·вҲ’рқ‘‘sвҖӢnОјвҖӢAОјвҖӢ(nвҖӢs)]рқ‘Ҡ0superscriptрқңӮрқ’«рқ‘–рқ‘”subscriptsuperscriptsuperscriptрқңӮ0differential-dрқ‘ subscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮsuperscriptрқҗҙрқңҮрқ‘ӣрқ‘ W(0,eta-)=hatPexpleft[-iginteta-0!!ds,nmuAmu(ns)right] is the Wilson line between the two light-cone coordinates 00 and О·вҲ’=(О·0+О·3)/2superscriptрқңӮsuperscriptрқңӮ0superscriptрқңӮ32eta-=(eta0+eta3)/sqrt2 with рқ’«рқ’«hatP denoting the path-ordering operator. The real-time dependence in the light-cone DA definition makes it difficult to simulate directly on a Euclidean lattice. Thus different approaches have been proposed to extract partial information of meson DAs with lattice calculations, including the calculation of their lowest moments вҹЁ(2вҖӢxвҲ’1)nвҹ©delimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscript2рқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘ӣ(2x-1)n from local twist-2 operatorscitebib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib16, bib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib19 through the operator product expansion (OPE) of the pion correlators, the calculation of non-local correlations that can be related to the meson DAs through OPE or short distance factorization (SDF)citebib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib22, bib.bib23, bib.bib24, bib.bib25, bib.bib26, and the large momentum effective theory (LaMET)citebib.bib27, bib.bib28, bib.bib29, bib.bib30, bib.bib31, bib.bib32, bib.bib33, bib.bib34, bib.bib35, bib.bib36 that directly calculates the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence from a momentum-space factorization. The local twist-2 operator calculation and the SDF approach in principle allows us to obtain a few lowest-order meson DA moments, but the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence could only be obtained by fitting with some model assumptions of the shape. On the other hand, LaMET relates a class of observables at finite hadron momentum, named quasi-distributions, to the light-cone distribution through a factorization in momentum space, with power corrections suppressed by the power of parton momentum xвҖӢPzрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§xPz or (1вҲ’x)вҖӢPz1рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§(1-x)Pz, thus provides an approach to calculate the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence in a moderate range of xрқ‘Ҙx with well-controlled systematics. So far, various lattice artifacts and theoretical systematics have been studied in the LaMET calculation of the meson DAs since the first attemptscitebib.bib30, bib.bib31. The theoretical efforts include the lattice renormalization, improvement of the power accuracy, and the inclusion of higher-order effects in perturbation theory. The renormalization of the linear divergence in the quasi-DA correlatorscitebib.bib37, bib.bib38, bib.bib39, bib.bib40 using regularization-independent momentum subtraction (RI/MOM) citebib.bib41, bib.bib42, bib.bib43, bib.bib44 scheme or the ratio citebib.bib45, bib.bib46 scheme in the early stage, which was later improved with the hybrid renormalization schemecitebib.bib47, bib.bib48, bib.bib49. The power corrections resulting from the linear renormalon ambiguity of order ОӣQCDsubscriptОӣQCDLambdatextQCD in renormalization and perturbative matching is resolved with the leading-renormalon resummation (LRR) methodcitebib.bib35, bib.bib50, thus the power accuracy is improved to sub-leading order. The high-order effects are examined by including the calculation of next-to-leading order (NLO) matching kernel for quasi-DAcitebib.bib30, bib.bib31, bib.bib51, bib.bib52, bib.bib53, and the renormalization group resummation (RGR)citebib.bib54, bib.bib55 and threshold resummationcitebib.bib54, bib.bib56, bib.bib57, bib.bib36 that resum the logarithm to further include higher-order logarithm contributions. Since the power expansion parameter is related to the physical scale xвҖӢPzрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§xPz or (1вҲ’x)вҖӢPz1рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§(1-x)Pz, the LaMET expansion cannot be applied to endpoint regions xвҶ’0,1вҶ’рқ‘Ҙ01xto0,1. Beyond the range that LaMET calculates, it has also been proposed to constrain the endpoint regions with the global information of DA, such as the lower moments or short distance correlations, extracted from SDFcitebib.bib58, bib.bib35. Numerically, the first continuum extrapolation of the DA was carried out in Ref. citebib.bib32 with the RI/MOM scheme at heavier-than-physical pion masses, succeeded by a continuum extrapolation at physical pion mass with NLO hybrid-scheme renormalization and matching, but without LRRcitebib.bib34. The LRR method was first implemented in Ref. citebib.bib35 with NLO matching and RGR. Meanwhile, the meson DA moments have also been calculated using the SDF approach at NLO accuracycitebib.bib22, bib.bib25. Recently, there is new progress to develop the threshold resummation for the LaMET calculation of pion-DA on a domain-wall ensemble that preserves chiral symmetrycitebib.bib36. In this work, we present the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence calculation of pion and kaon DAs on a lattice ensemble with physical pion mass and lattice spacing a=0.076рқ‘Һ0.076a=0.076 fm, by boosting the pion momentum up to 1.81.81.8GeV and the kaon momentum up to 2.32.32.3GeV. We renormalize the bare meson quasi-DA matrix elements with the hybrid scheme, and then match them to the continuum MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme with LRR. After Fourier transforming to the xрқ‘Ҙx-space, we match the quasi-DA to the light-cone with next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) threshold resummation and NLO matching, which provides a reliable calculation in the range xвҲҲ[0.25,0.75]рқ‘Ҙ0.250.75xin[0.25,0.75] for pion and xвҲҲ[0.2,0.8]рқ‘Ҙ0.20.8xin[0.2,0.8] for kaon. Finally, we utilize the short distance correlations along with the already determined mid-xрқ‘Ҙx distribution, to better constrain the distribution in the endpoint region, thus provide us a rough estimate for higher moments of DA. This work is organized as follows. In Sec. , we present our lattice setup of the calculation, and show the extraction of the bare matrix elements. In Sec. , we renormalize the bare matrix elements in the hybrid scheme with LRR and obtain xрқ‘Ҙx-dependent quasi-DAs. In Sec. , we apply the threshold-resummed perturbative inverse matching to extract the light-cone DA in a range of xвҲҲ[x0,1вҲ’x0]рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ01subscriptрқ‘Ҙ0xin[x0,1-x0], and provide a rough estimate for the higher moments of pion and kaon DAs by modeling the endpoint regions using complemenarity with SDF. Finally, we conclude in Sec. .
content_sections.2 Numerical setup: We use two 2+1 flavor Highly Improved Staggered Quark (HISQ)citebib.bib59 gauge ensembles generated by the HotQCD collaborationcitebib.bib60 with lattice size LsГ—Lt=643Г—64subscriptрқҗҝрқ‘ subscriptрқҗҝрқ‘Ўsuperscript64364Lstimes Lt=643times 64 and lattice spacing of a = 0.076 fm. The quark mass in the sea are both at the physical point. We use the Wilson-Clover action for the valence sector, and the clover coefficients are set to be csвҖӢwsubscriptрқ‘җрқ‘ рқ‘Өcsw = 1.0372citebib.bib61 using the averaged plaquette after 1-step HYP smearingcitebib.bib62. The Wilson-Clover quark mass are tuned so that the pion and kaon mass are 140 MeV and 498 MeV, respectively. The calculations used QUDA multigrid algorithmcitebib.bib63, bib.bib64, bib.bib65, bib.bib66 for the Wilson-Dirac operator inversions to get the quark propagators. All Mode Averaging (AMA) techniquecitebib.bib67 is applied to increase the statistics. The pion correlators on this ensemble have already been generated and analyzed in a previous workcitebib.bib25, so we only present the analysis of kaon raw data in this section. In order to derive the bare matrix elements of ground state, we need to compute the two-point functions to extract the energy spectrum and get the overlap amplitudes, CKKвҖӢ(рқҗҸ,ts)=вҹЁ[KSвҖӢ(рқҗҸ,ts)]вҖӢ[KSвҖӢ(рқҗҸ,0)]вҖ вҹ©. subscriptрқҗ¶KKрқҗҸsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ delimited-вҹЁвҹ©delimited-subscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҶрқҗҸsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ superscriptdelimited-subscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҶрқҗҸ0вҖ displaystyle Crm KK(P,ts)=left[KS(P,ts)][KS(P,0)]daggerright. where KSsubscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҶKS are the kaon interpolators, namely, KSвҖӢ(рқҗҸ,t)=вҲ‘рқҗұsВҜSвҖӢ(рқҗұ,t)вҖӢОі5вҖӢuSвҖӢ(рқҗұ,t)вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢрқҗҸвӢ…рқҗұ,subscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҶрқҗҸрқ‘ЎsubscriptрқҗұsubscriptВҜрқ‘ рқ‘Ҷрқҗұрқ‘Ўsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘Ҷрқҗұрқ‘Ўsuperscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–рқҗҸрқҗұdisplaystylebeginsplitKS(P,t)=sumxbarsS(x,t)gamma5uS(x,t)e-iPcdotx, endsplit in which boosted smearingcitebib.bib68, bib.bib69 (denoted by Sрқ‘ҶS) is applied to have better overlap with ground states and improve the signal of high-momenta states. The Gaussian radius of the light and strange quarks used in this work are rlGsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҹрқ‘ҷрқҗәrlG = 0.59 fm and rsGsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҹрқ‘ рқҗәrsG = 0.83 fm. With periodic boundary condition, the hadron momentum in physical unit are Pz=2вҖӢПҖвҖӢnz/(LsвҖӢa)subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2рқңӢsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§subscriptрқҗҝрқ‘ рқ‘ҺPz=2pi nz/(Lsa) with nzsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§nz being an integer. Similar to Ref. citebib.bib25, the quasi-DA matrix elements of kaon can be extracted from the equal-time correlators, CDAО“вҖӢ(z;P,ts)=вҹЁ[OО“вҖӢ(z;рқҗҸ,ts)]вҖӢ[KSвҖӢ(рқҗҸ,0)]вҖ вҹ©,superscriptsubscriptрқҗ¶DAО“рқ‘§Psubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ delimited-вҹЁвҹ©delimited-subscriptрқ‘ӮО“рқ‘§рқҗҸsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ superscriptdelimited-subscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҶрқҗҸ0вҖ displaystylebeginsplitCrmDAGamma(z;P,ts)=left[OGamma(z;P,ts)][KS(P,0)]daggerright,endsplit with, OО“вҖӢ(z;рқҗҸ,ts)=вҲ‘рқҗұuВҜвҖӢ(рқҗұ,ts)вҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(рқҗұ,ts;рқҗұ+рқҗі,ts)вҖӢsвҖӢ(рқҗұ+рқҗі,ts)вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢрқҗҸвӢ…рқҗұ. subscriptрқ‘ӮО“рқ‘§рқҗҸsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ subscriptрқҗұВҜрқ‘ўрқҗұsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ О“рқ‘Ҡрқҗұsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқҗұрқҗіsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘ рқҗұрқҗіsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ superscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–рқҗҸрқҗұdisplaystylebeginsplitOGamma(z;P,ts)=sumxbaru(x,ts)Gamma W(x,ts;x+z,ts)s(x+z,ts)e-iPcdotx. endsplit where the quark fields are separated by рқҗі=(0,0,z)рқҗі00рқ‘§z=(0,0,z) and connected by the Wilson line WвҖӢ(рқҗұ,ts;рқҗұ+рқҗі,ts)рқ‘Ҡрқҗұsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқҗұрқҗіsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ W(x,ts;x+z,ts) to keep the gauge invariance. The direction of momentum is chosen to be along with the Wilson line рқҗҸ=(0,0,Pz)рқҗҸ00subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§P=(0,0,Pz). The quasi-DA operator with both О“=Оі5вҖӢОі3О“subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3Gamma=gamma5gamma3 and Оі5вҖӢОі0subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ0gamma5gamma0 can approach the light-cone DAs under large momentum boost. However, the Оі5вҖӢОі0subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ0gamma5gamma0 is not favored due to its mixing under renormalization with the ПғxвҖӢysubscriptрқңҺрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsigmaxy operator from the explicit chiral symmetry breaking of Wilson-type actioncitebib.bib41, bib.bib70. The Оі5вҖӢОі3subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3gamma5gamma3 is free of such mixing as we will focus in the following analysis.
content_sections.2 Numerical setup 2.1 Energy spectrum fr: The two-point functions of kaon can be decomposed as, CKKвҖӢ(рқҗҸ,ts)=вҲ‘n=0NstateвҲ’1|вҹЁО©|KS|n;рқҗҸвҹ©|2вҖӢ(eвҲ’EnвҖӢts+eвҲ’EnвҖӢ(aвҖӢLtвҲ’ts)),=вҲ‘n=0NstateвҲ’1|Zn|22вҖӢEnвҖӢ(eвҲ’EnвҖӢts+eвҲ’EnвҖӢ(aвҖӢLtвҲ’ts)),displaystylebeginsplitCrm KK(P,ts)=sumn=0Nrm state-1|Omega|KS|n;P|2(e-Ents+e-En(aLt-ts)), =sumn=0Nrm state-1frac|Zn|22En(e-Ents+e-En(aLt-ts)),endsplit with Ensubscriptрқҗёрқ‘ӣEn representing the energy levels and An=Zn/2вҖӢEnsubscriptрқҗҙрқ‘ӣsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘ӣ2subscriptрқҗёрқ‘ӣAn=Zn/sqrt2En being the kaon overlap amplitude вҹЁО©|KS|n;рқҗҸвҹ©quantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқҗҫрқ‘Ҷрқ‘ӣрқҗҸOmega|KS|n;P. By truncating at Nstate=1subscriptрқ‘Ғstate1Nrm state=1, one can define the effective mass of kaon using adjacent time slices as shown in Fig. . As one can see, the effective mass reach the plateaus around tsвүі8вҖӢagreater-than-or-equivalent-tosubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 8рқ‘Һtsgtrsim 8a, in which the plateau of Pz=0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§0Pz=0 around 497 MeV is the physical kaon mass. The plateaus of non-zero momentum all agree with the solid lines evaluated from the dispersion relation E0вҖӢ(Pz)=Pz2+mK2subscriptрқҗё0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқҗҫ2E0(Pz)=sqrtPz2+mK2, suggesting the energy spectrum is dominated by the kaon ground state after tsвҲј8вҖӢasimilar-tosubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 8рқ‘Һtssim 8a. We then perform the Nрқ‘ҒN-state fit for the two-point functions of tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts in range [tmin,32вҖӢa]subscriptрқ‘Ўmin32рқ‘Һ[ttextupmin,32a]. The results are shown in Fig. . The E0subscriptрқҗё0E0 from one-state fit (left panels) show similar behavior like Fig. but more stable because of combining multiple tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts. It is clear that the ground state energy E0subscriptрқҗё0E0 following the dispersion relation E0вҖӢ(Pz)=Pz2+mK2subscriptрқҗё0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқҗҫ2E0(Pz)=sqrtPz2+mK2 well as also observed in Ref. citebib.bib71. To further extract the first excited state E1subscriptрқҗё1E1, we use two-state fit and fix E0subscriptрқҗё0E0 with the dispersion relation. The results are shown in the right panels of Fig. . Since the SS correlators are tuned to have better overlap with ground state, the signal of E1subscriptрқҗё1E1 is much worse. Notably, the E1subscriptрқҗё1E1 determined from two-state fit reaches the plateau around tminвҲј4вҖӢasimilar-tosubscriptрқ‘Ўmin4рқ‘Һttextupminsim 4a, suggesting the energy spectrum can be well described by the two-state model from there. We will use the Ensubscriptрқҗёрқ‘ӣEn and Ansubscriptрқҗҙрқ‘ӣAn in this section for the extraction of bare matrix elements of quasi-DA.
content_sections.2 Numerical setup 2.2 Bare matrix elemen: The bare quasi-DA matrix elements has spectral decomposition, CDAОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(z;рқҗҸ,ts)=вҲ‘n=0NstateвҲ’1вҹЁО©|OОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(z)|n;рқҗҸвҹ©вҖӢвҹЁn;рқҗҸ|KS|О©вҹ©вҖӢ(eвҲ’EnвҖӢts+eвҲ’EnвҖӢ(aвҖӢLtвҲ’ts)),=вҲ‘n=0NstateвҲ’1Zn2вҖӢEnвҖӢвҹЁО©|OОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(z)|n;рқҗҸвҹ©вҖӢ(eвҲ’EnвҖӢts+eвҲ’EnвҖӢ(aвҖӢLtвҲ’ts)),displaystylebeginsplitCrm DAgamma5gamma3(z;P,ts)=sumn=0Nrm state-1Omega|Ogamma5gamma3(z)|n;P n;P|KS|Omega(e-Ents+e-En(aLt-ts)), =sumn=0Nrm state-1fracZn2EnOmega|Ogamma5gamma3(z)|n;P(e-Ents+e-En(aLt-ts)),endsplit where both the Znsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘ӣZn and Ensubscriptрқҗёрқ‘ӣEn are the same as the two-point functions defined in Eq. and analyzed in Sec. . Taking the advantage of correlations between CKKsubscriptрқҗ¶KKCrm KK and CDAsubscriptрқҗ¶DACrm DA, we construct the ratio, RОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(ts)=CDAОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(z;P,ts)CKKвҖӢ(P,ts). superscriptрқ‘…subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ superscriptsubscriptрқҗ¶DAsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3рқ‘§Psubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ subscriptрқҗ¶KKPsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ displaystyle Rgamma5gamma3(ts)=fracCrmDAgamma5gamma3(z;P,ts)Crm KK(P,ts). In Fig. we show the ratios RОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(ts)superscriptрқ‘…subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ Rgamma5gamma3(ts) for momentum nz=1,9subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§19nz=1,9 at z=1вҖӢa,2вҖӢa,4вҖӢaрқ‘§1рқ‘Һ2рқ‘Һ4рқ‘Һz=1a,2a,4a as a function of tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts. At large tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts, plateaus can be clearly observed. In the tsвҶ’вҲһвҶ’subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ tsrightarrowinfty limit, the quasi-DA matrix elements of kaon ground state can be derived from RОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(ts)вҶ’вҹЁО©|OОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(z)|0;рқҗҸвҹ©/Z0вҶ’superscriptрқ‘…subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ quantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3рқ‘§0рқҗҸsubscriptрқ‘Қ0Rgamma5gamma3(ts)rightarrowOmega|Ogamma5gamma3(z)|0;P/Z0. As has been observed in Sec. that a two-state spectral decomposition can approximate the tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts dependence of two-point function beyond tmin=4вҖӢasubscriptрқ‘Ўmin4рқ‘Һtrm min=4a, we apply two-state fit to extract the ground state matrix elements, using the ratios RОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(ts)superscriptрқ‘…subscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ Rgamma5gamma3(ts) started from tsвүі4вҖӢagreater-than-or-equivalent-tosubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 4рқ‘Һtsgtrsim 4a. The fit results are shown as the bands in Fig. , which nicely described the data. The extracted bare matrix elements вҹЁО©|OОі5вҖӢОі3вҖӢ(z)|0;рқҗҸвҹ©quantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3рқ‘§0рқҗҸOmega|Ogamma5gamma3(z)|0;P for nzвҲҲ[0,9]subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§09nzin[0,9] are summarized in Fig. . Considering the Lorentz covariance, the matrix elements can be parameterized ascitebib.bib72, вҹЁО©|OОі5вҖӢОіОјвҖӢ(z)|0;рқҗҸвҹ©=PОјвҖӢHвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…P)+zОјвҖӢmK2вҖӢkвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…P). quantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫрқңҮрқ‘§0рқҗҸsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқңҮрқҗ»superscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§рқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘§рқңҮsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқҗҫ2рқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§рқ‘ғdisplaystylebeginsplitOmega|Ogamma5gammamu(z)|0;P=PmutildeH(z2,zcdot P)+zmumK2tildek(z2,zcdot P). endsplit The 2nd term of the right hand proportional to zОјsuperscriptрқ‘§рқңҮzmu contributes to the matrix elements of OОі5вҖӢОі3subscriptрқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3Ogamma5gamma3 with finite spacial separation zрқ‘§z, while it is zero for the case of using OОі5вҖӢОі0subscriptрқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ0Ogamma5gamma0. In the infinite-momentum limit, the first term HвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…Pz)рқҗ»superscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§tildeH(z2,zcdot Pz) is approaching the light-cone DAs, thus is identified as the actual matrix element HвҖӢ(z,Pz)вүЎHвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…Pz)рқҗ»рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқҗ»superscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§tildeH(z,Pz)equivtildeH(z2,zcdot Pz) in our notation, while the kвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…P)рқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§рқ‘ғtildek(z2,zcdot P) term is suppressed to zero as an power correction. It is worth to mention that, PzвҖӢHвҖӢ(z,Pz)superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқҗ»рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§PztildeH(z,Pz) is zero when Pz=0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§0Pz=0 for the case of OОі5вҖӢОі3subscriptрқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқӣҫ3Ogamma5gamma3. When z=0рқ‘§0z=0, the kaon decay constant can be extracted from the matrix elements by HвҖӢ(0,Pz)=fK/ZAрқҗ»0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘“рқҗҫsubscriptрқ‘ҚрқҗҙtildeH(0,Pz)=fK/ZA with ZA=0.969вҖӢsubscriptрқ‘Қрқҗҙ0.9691ZA=0.969 the finite renormalization constant for the axial current operator that has been determined in Ref. citebib.bib25. The fKsubscriptрқ‘“рқҗҫfK we got are summarized in Fig. as a function of momenta Pz=2вҖӢПҖвҖӢnz/(NsвҖӢa)subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2рқңӢsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ рқ‘ҺPz=2pi nz/(Nsa). As one can observe, the momentum dependence is mild, suggesting the goodness of our extraction. Our determination at nz=1subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§1nz=1 and 2 are 153.3 and 152.7 MeV respectively, which are close to the FLAG average for 2+1 flavor QCD fKsubscriptрқ‘“рқҗҫfK = 155.7 MeVcitebib.bib73. The small deviation could considerably come from the lattice artifacts as only one ensemble is used in this calculation. When zвү 0рқ‘§0zneq 0, considering the parity and time-reversal symmetry (see appendix in Ref. citebib.bib72), the zвҖӢmK2вҖӢkвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…P)рқ‘§superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқҗҫ2рқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§рқ‘ғzmK2tildek(z2,zcdot P) term can only contribute to the imaginary part of matrix elements and leave the real part unaffected. As shown in Fig. , the real parts of matrix elements at Pz=0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§0Pz=0 are zero while the imaginary parts are non-zero as expected. In the latter case, we fit the moments at an initial scale вҹЁОҫ2вҖӢ(Ојi=2вҖӢeвҲ’ОіEвҖӢzвҲ’1)вҹ©delimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscriptрқңү2subscriptрқңҮрқ‘–2superscriptрқ‘’subscriptрқӣҫрқҗёsuperscriptрқ‘§1xi2(mui=2e-gammaEz-1), where the log terms in CnвҖӢmвҖӢ(z,Ојi)subscriptрқҗ¶рқ‘ӣрқ‘ҡрқ‘§subscriptрқңҮрқ‘–Cnm(z,mui) vanish, then evolve to Ој=2рқңҮ2mu=2GeV by solving Eq. . The scale variation is examined by choosing Ојi=2вҖӢcвҖӢeвҲ’ОіEвҖӢzвҲ’1subscriptрқңҮрқ‘–2рқ‘җsuperscriptрқ‘’subscriptрқӣҫрқҗёsuperscriptрқ‘§1mui=2ce-gammaEz-1 with c=2,1,1/2рқ‘җ2112c=sqrt2,1,1/sqrt2 to estimate the uncertainties from higher-order perturbation theory. The fitted ratio and the extracted moments are shown in Fig. . We show the fitted moments with both statistical and scale variation error bars. Note that the scale variation becomes large when zрқ‘§z increases, because the scale zвҲ’1/2вҲјОӣQCDsimilar-tosuperscriptрқ‘§12subscriptОӣQCDz-1/sqrt2simLambdarm QCD becomes non-perturbative. The second moment of kaon DA is slightly lower than the moment of pion DA, indicating the kaon DA to be narrower, similar to what has been observed in previous xрқ‘Ҙx-dependent calculationscitebib.bib31, bib.bib32, bib.bib34. In principle, the odd moments can also be extracted from the imaginary part of the matrix elements. In our data, we found that the imaginary part are non-vanishing for smaller momenta data, indicating a non-zero first moment of kaon DA. However, for large momenta, the imaginary parts decreases with momentum, and for the largest momentum Im[HвҖӢ(z,nz=9)]рқҗ»рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§9imaginary[H(z,nz=9)] are consistent with zero at short distance. Thus in principle we cannot describe all data with a universal first moment. Since we focus on the large momentum expansion approach in this work, we show the fit results for the ratio of imaginary part at the largest momentum nz=9subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§9nz=9 to the real part of nz=1subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§1nz=1 in the range of zвҲҲ[a,zmax]рқ‘§рқ‘Һsubscriptрқ‘§maxzin[a,zrm max] at different zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max. This results in a first Mellin moment of kaon вҹЁОҫвҹ©KвҖӢ(Ој)вүҲ0.0013вҖӢsubscriptdelimited-вҹЁвҹ©рқңүрқҗҫрқңҮ0.001360xiK(mu)approx 0.0013, corresponding to the first Gegenbauer coefficient a1=0.002вҖӢsubscriptрқ‘Һ10.00210a1=0.002, which is consistent with zero, as shown in Fig. . The suppression of the kaon skewness at large momentum may suggest that such skewness originating from the mass difference between light and strange quark is no longer important in the infinite momentum limit, where both partons act just as massless particles. However, considering that our subtracted imaginary part is still potentially contaminated by the Pzsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz dependence in zОјвҖӢkвҖӢ(z2,zвӢ…P)superscriptрқ‘§рқңҮрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘§2вӢ…рқ‘§рқ‘ғzmuk(z2,zcdot P) term in Eq. , and since previous lattice calculations of local twist-2 opeartors suggest a non-zero kaon first momentcitebib.bib16, bib.bib77, bib.bib17, bib.bib19, the suppressed imaginary part at large momentum in our data may just be a lattice artifact, and needs further investigation.

The bare matrix elements of meson quasi-DAs contain UV divergences. Especially, besides logarithmic divergences, the spatial Wilson line WвҖӢ(0,z)рқ‘Ҡ0рқ‘§W(0,z) in the operator has a linearly divergent self energy in the lattice regulator aвҲ’1superscriptрқ‘Һ1a-1citebib.bib37, bib.bib38, bib.bib39, bib.bib40. It can be renormalized multiplicatively ascitebib.bib38, bib.bib39, bib.bib40 HRвҖӢ(z,Pz)=HBвҖӢ(z,Pz,a)/ZRвҖӢ(z,a)superscriptрқҗ»рқ‘…рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptрқҗ»рқҗөрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘…рқ‘§рқ‘Һdisplaystyle HR(z,Pz)=HB(z,Pz,a)/ZR(z,a) with a renormalization constant ZRвҖӢ(z,a)вҲјeвҲ’ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)вҖӢ|z|similar-tosuperscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘…рқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘Һрқ‘§ZR(z,a)sim e-delta m(a)|z| containing the same linear divergence in the mass counterterm ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)вҲјaвҲ’1similar-toрқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘Һ1delta m(a)sim a-1. In this work, we used the hybrid renormalization schemecitebib.bib47 that can be perturbatively matched to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme in all regions of zрқ‘§z. The renormalization constant in the hybrid scheme is defined as Zhybrid(z,a)=ZRвҖӢ(z,a)|z|вүӨzsZRвҖӢ(zs,a)вҖӢeвҲ’ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)вҖӢ(|z|вҲ’zs)|z|>zsdisplaystyle Zrm hybrid(z,a)=leftbeginmatrixZR(z,a)hfill|z|leq zs ZR(zs,a)e-delta m(a)(|z|-zs)|z|>zsendmatrixright. At short distance, since the correlator of ОіzвҖӢОі5subscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫ5gammazgamma5 vanishes at Pz=0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§0Pz=0, we cannot use the ratio scheme ZR(z,a)=HB(z,P=0,a)ZR(z,a)=HB(z,P=0,a). Instead, we determine it from the self-renormalization approachcitebib.bib48, bib.bib35, which uses the perturbatively calculated Wilson coefficient ZRвҖӢ(z,a)=eвҲ’ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)вҖӢ|z|вҖӢC0вҖӢ(z,Ој)superscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘…рқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘Һрқ‘§subscriptрқҗ¶0рқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyle ZR(z,a)=e-delta m(a)|z|C0(z,mu) for |z|вүӨzsрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘§рқ‘ |z|leq zs. And ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘Һdelta m(a) is obtained by requiring that the matrix elements after removing the linear divergence eОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)вҖӢ|z|вҖӢHBвҖӢ(z,Pz,a)superscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘Һрқ‘§superscriptрқҗ»рқҗөрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқ‘Һedelta m(a)|z|HB(z,Pz,a) agree with OPE reconstruction of HRвҖӢ(z,Pz,Ој)superscriptрқҗ»рқ‘…рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮHR(z,Pz,mu) in Eq. using the moments fitted from the RG-invariant ratio as inputs, up to a constant conversion factor that converts the lattice scheme to MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MScitebib.bib50, eОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ(a)вҖӢ|z|вҖӢHBвҖӢ(z,Pz,a)=HRвҖӢ(z,Pz,Ој)вҖӢeв„җlatвҖӢ(aвҲ’1)вҲ’в„җвҖӢ(Ој),superscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘Һрқ‘§superscriptрқҗ»рқҗөрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқҗ»рқ‘…рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘’superscriptв„җlatsuperscriptрқ‘Һ1в„җрқңҮdisplaystyle edelta m(a)|z|HB(z,Pz,a)=HR(z,Pz,mu)eIrm lat(a-1)-I(mu), where в„җвҖӢ(Ој)=вҲ«ОіОІвҖӢрқ‘‘Оұ|Оұ=ОұsвҖӢ(Ој)в„җрқңҮevaluated-atрқӣҫрқӣҪdifferential-dрқӣјрқӣјsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ рқңҮI(mu)=inttfracgammabetadalpha|alpha=alphas(mu) are the RG-evolution factors that cancels the UV-regulator dependence ОјрқңҮmu in the matrix elements. To ensure the linear power accuracy of the factorization, we applied the LRR and RGR-improved Wilson coefficientcitebib.bib50. CmвҖӢnLRRsuperscriptsubscriptрқҗ¶рқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣLRRdisplaystyle Cmnrm LRR (z,zвҲ’1)=CmвҖӢnвҖӢ(z,zвҲ’1)+ОҙnвҖӢmвҖӢNвҖӢ[вҲ’ОұsвҖӢ(1+c1)+4вҖӢПҖОІ0вҖӢвҲ«0,PVвҲһрқ‘‘uвҖӢeвҲ’4вҖӢПҖвҖӢuОұsвҖӢ(Ој)вҖӢОІ0вҖӢ1(1вҲ’2вҖӢu)1+bвҖӢ(1+c1вҖӢ(1вҲ’2вҖӢu)+вҖҰ)],рқ‘§superscriptрқ‘§1subscriptрқҗ¶рқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘§1subscriptрқӣҝрқ‘ӣрқ‘ҡрқ‘Ғdelimited-subscriptрқӣјрқ‘ 1subscriptрқ‘җ14рқңӢsubscriptрқӣҪ0superscriptsubscript0PVdifferential-dрқ‘ўsuperscriptрқ‘’4рқңӢрқ‘ўsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ рқңҮsubscriptрқӣҪ01superscript12рқ‘ў1рқ‘Ҹ1subscriptрқ‘җ112рқ‘ўвҖҰdisplaystyle(z,z-1)=Cmn(z,z-1)+deltanmNleft[-alphas(1+c1)+frac4pibeta0intrm 0,PVinftydue-frac4pi ualphas(mu)beta0frac1(1-2u)1+bbig(1+c1(1-2u)+. . . big)right], where b=ОІ1/2вҖӢОІ02рқ‘ҸsubscriptрқӣҪ12superscriptsubscriptрқӣҪ02b=beta1/2beta02 and c1=(ОІ12вҲ’ОІ0вҖӢОІ2)/(4вҖӢbвҖӢОІ04)subscriptрқ‘җ1superscriptsubscriptрқӣҪ12subscriptрқӣҪ0subscriptрқӣҪ24рқ‘ҸsubscriptsuperscriptрқӣҪ40c1=(beta12-beta0beta2)/(4bbeta40) are from higher orders in the QCD beta function, NвҖӢ(nf=3)=0.575рқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘“30.575N(nf=3)=0.575 is the overall strength of the linear renormalon estimated from the quark pole mass correctioncitebib.bib78, bib.bib79. Taking the moments we fitted from the RG-invariant ratios and tune the value of ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m, we find the matrix elements eОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ|z|вҖӢHBвҖӢ(z,Pz,O)superscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘§superscriptрқҗ»рқҗөрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқ‘Ӯedelta m|z|HB(z,Pz,O) agree well with OPE reconstruction Eq. when ОҙвҖӢmвүҲ0.6рқӣҝрқ‘ҡ0.6delta mapprox 0.6GeV for kaon and ОҙвҖӢmвүҲ0.57рқӣҝрқ‘ҡ0.57delta mapprox 0.57GeV for pion, as shown in Fig. . The consistency with OPE reconstruction suggests that the short-distance data can be well-described by perturbation theory. This justifies our choice of using C0вҖӢ(z,Ој)subscriptрқҗ¶0рқ‘§рқңҮC0(z,mu) calculated from perturbation theory in Eq. to renormalize the short distance matrix elements. Then we apply it to the hybrid scheme with zs=3вҖӢasubscriptрқ‘§рқ‘ 3рқ‘Һzs=3a in Fig. . We find the largest three momenta converge to a universal shape, and the imaginary part for kaon is non-vanishing but close to zero, indicating the existence of small skewness.

The LaMET factorization is naturally defined in momentum space, which requires the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of quasi-DA to obtain the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependent light-cone DA. The extraction of xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence requires the Fourier transform of the above renormalized correlation functions to the momentum space. Thus in principle we need to know the distribution to arbitrarily large zвҖӢPzрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§zPz. On the other hand, the noise increases exponentially at large zрқ‘§z, limiting our calculations to a certain range of zрқ‘§z values. An exact Fourier transformation is thus impossible. However, the contribution of the unknown long-tail distribution turns out to be bounded by physical constraints. For example, the Euclidean correlation functions must have a finite correlation length О»0вҲқPzproportional-tosubscriptрқңҶ0subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§lambda0propto Pz in the coordinate space, which results in an exponential decay expвҒЎ([вҲ’О»/О»0])delimited-рқңҶsubscriptрқңҶ0exp[-lambda/lambda0] of the correlation functions at large О»=zвҖӢPzрқңҶрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§lambda=zPz. We are thus able to make an extrapolation of the long-tail distribution with these constraints to reduce uncertainty from it. One example of the long-tail modeling iscitebib.bib47, HвҖӢ(О»)вҶ’О»вҶ’вҲһ(c1вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢО»/2(вҲ’iвҖӢО»)d1+c2вҖӢeiвҖӢО»/2(iвҖӢО»)d2)вҖӢeвҲ’О»/О»0,вҶ’рқңҶвҶ’рқҗ»рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘җ1superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶ2superscriptрқ‘–рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘‘1subscriptрқ‘җ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶ2superscriptрқ‘–рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘‘2superscriptрқ‘’рқңҶsubscriptрқңҶ0displaystyle H(lambda)xrightarrowlambdatoinftyleft(fracc1e-ilambda/2(-ilambda)d1+fracc2eilambda/2(ilambda)d2right)e-lambda/lambda0, where the parameterization inside the round brackets is motivated from the Regge behaviorcitebib.bib80 of the light-cone distribution near the endpoint regions. For symmetric pion DA, c1=c2subscriptрқ‘җ1subscriptрқ‘җ2c1=c2 and d1=d2subscriptрқ‘‘1subscriptрқ‘‘2d1=d2. For kaon DA, since the imaginary part is too small to be extrapolated, we model only the real part, and directly discrete Fourier transform the imaginary part truncated at the point when it starts to be consistent with zero. The model dependence of the extrapolation is estimated from three different fits of the long tail, by including or excluding the exponential decay eвҲ’О»/О»0superscriptрқ‘’рқңҶsubscriptрқңҶ0e-lambda/lambda0 (labeled вҖңexponentialвҖқ and вҖңalgebraicвҖқ) in Eq. or by extrapolating from a larger zрқ‘§z (labeled вҖңlarge О»рқңҶlambdaвҖқ). The results are shown in Fig. . The long-tail extrapolation will eventually introduce a small systematic uncertainties to the light-cone DA near x=0.5рқ‘Ҙ0.5x=0.5 in our case. Compared to the pion quasi-DA, the more precise long-range correlation in kaon quasi-DA helps reduce such a systematic uncertainty.

Several improvements on the NLO matching kernel have been proposed to increase the accuracy of the perturbative matching in LaMET, including the LRRcitebib.bib50, bib.bib35 that eliminate the linear power correction, and the RGRcitebib.bib55 and threshold resummationcitebib.bib56 that resums the small-momentum logarithms. Our other recent work has derived the formalism for DA to resum all the small-momentum logarithms in the threshold limit |xвҲ’y|вҶ’0вҶ’рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұ0|x-y|to 0citebib.bib36, where the matching kernel can be factorized into the Sudakov factor HвҖӢ(xвҖӢPz,xВҜвҖӢPz,Ој)рқҗ»рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§ВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮH(xPz,barxPz,mu) and jet function JвҖӢ(|xвҲ’y|вҖӢPz,Ој)рқҗҪрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮJ(|x-y|Pz,mu)citebib.bib56, рқ’һвҖӢ(x,y,Ој,Pz)вҶ’xвҶ’yвҶ’рқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰвҶ’рқ’һрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§absentdisplaystyleC(x,y,mu,Pz)xrightarrowxto y HвҖӢ(xвҖӢPz,xВҜвҖӢPz,Ој)вҠ—JвҖӢ(|xвҲ’y|вҖӢPz,Ој)+рқ’ӘвҖӢ((yвҲ’x)0). tensor-productрқҗ»рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§ВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮрқҗҪрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮрқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙ0displaystyle H(xPz,barxPz,mu)otimes J(|x-y|Pz,mu)+O((y-x)0). In the threshold limit, the Sudakov factor HвҖӢ(xвҖӢPz,xВҜвҖӢPz,Ој)=в„ұвҖӢ[CвҲ’sgnвҖӢ(z)вҖӢ(xвҖӢPz,Ој)вҖӢCsgnвҖӢ(z)вҖӢ(xВҜвҖӢPz,Ој)]вҖӢ(xвҲ’y)рқҗ»рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§ВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮв„ұdelimited-superscriptрқҗ¶sgnрқ‘§рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮsuperscriptрқҗ¶sgnрқ‘§ВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰH(xPz,barxPz,mu)=F[C-rm sgn(z)(xPz,mu)Crm sgn(z)(barxPz,mu)](x-y) incorporates the hard-collinear gluon modes connected to the external quark (anti-quark) lines with momentum xвҖӢPzрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§xPz (xВҜвҖӢPzВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§barxPz), and the jet function JвҖӢ(|xвҲ’y|вҖӢPz,Ој)=в„ұвҖӢ[JвҖӢ(z,Ој)]вҖӢ(xвҲ’y)рқҗҪрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮв„ұdelimited-рқҗҪрқ‘§рқңҮрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰJ(|x-y|Pz,mu)=F[tildeJ(z,mu)](x-y) absorbs the soft gluon modes with momentum |xвҲ’y|вҖӢPzрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§|x-y|Pzcitebib.bib56, bib.bib81. They each follows individual RG equations вҲӮlnвҒЎCВұвҖӢ(pz,Ој)вҲӮlnвҒЎОј=12вҖӢО“cuspвҖӢ(Оұs)вҖӢ(lnвҒЎ4вҖӢpz2Ој2ВұiвҖӢПҖ)+ОіcвҖӢ(Оұs),superscriptрқҗ¶plus-or-minussubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§рқңҮрқңҮ12subscriptО“cuspsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ plus-or-minus4superscriptsubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2рқ‘–рқңӢsubscriptрқӣҫрқ‘җsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ displaystylefracpartialln Cpm(pz,mu)partiallnmu=frac12Gammarm cusp(alphas)left(lnfrac4pz2mu2pm ipiright)+gammac(alphas), вҲӮlnвҒЎJвҖӢ(z,Ој)вҲӮlnвҒЎОј=О“cuspвҖӢ(Оұs)вҖӢlnвҒЎz2вҖӢОј2вҖӢe2вҖӢОіE4вҲ’ОіJвҖӢ(Оұs),рқҗҪрқ‘§рқңҮрқңҮsubscriptО“cuspsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘’2subscriptрқӣҫрқҗё4subscriptрқӣҫрқҗҪsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ displaystylefracpartiallntildeJ(z,mu)partiallnmu=Gammarm cusp(alphas)lnfracz2mu2e2gammaE4-gammaJ(alphas), where О“cusp,Оіc,ОіJsubscriptО“cuspsubscriptрқӣҫрқ‘җsubscriptрқӣҫрқҗҪGammarm cusp,gammac,gammaJ are the corresponding anomalous dimensionscitebib.bib55, bib.bib82, bib.bib36. Solving these RG equations, we can obtain the resummed threshold components in the matching kernel. Here the convolution order of Hрқҗ»H and JрқҗҪJ does not affect the threshold limit because theyвҖҷre multiplicative in coordinate space. So we average them and consider the difference between the two choices as a systematic error in our final results. The initial scales of the resummation are suggested to becitebib.bib36, Ојi=2вҖӢminвҒЎ[x,xВҜ]вҖӢPz,subscriptрқңҮрқ‘–2рқ‘ҘВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§displaystylemui=2min[x,barx]Pz,quad Ојh1=2вҖӢxвҖӢPz,Ојh2=2вҖӢxВҜвҖӢPz. formulae-sequencesubscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ12рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ22ВҜрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§displaystylemuh1=2xPz,quadmuh2=2barxPz. The full matching kernel is then resummed by first subtracting the threshold components JвҖӢHNLOрқҗҪsubscriptрқҗ»NLOJHrm NLO at fixed order, then add back the resummed threshold terms JвҖӢHTRрқҗҪsubscriptрқҗ»TRJHrm TR, рқ’һTRвҖӢ(Ој)=JвҖӢHTRвҖӢ(Ојi,Ојh1,Ојh2,Ој)вҠ—JвҖӢHNLOвҲ’1вҖӢ(Ој)вҠ—рқ’һNLOвҖӢ(Ој),subscriptрқ’һTRрқңҮtensor-producttensor-productрқҗҪsubscriptрқҗ»TRsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘–subscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ1subscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ2рқңҮрқҗҪsubscriptsuperscriptрқҗ»1NLOрқңҮsubscriptрқ’һNLOрқңҮdisplaystyleCrm TR(mu)=JHrm TR(mui,muh1,muh2,mu)otimes JH-1rm NLO(mu)otimesCrm NLO(mu), and the resummed inverse matching kernel can be obtained as рқ’һTRвҲ’1вҖӢ(Ој)=рқ’һNLOвҲ’1вҖӢ(Ој)вҠ—JвҖӢHNLOвҖӢ(Ој)вҠ—JвҖӢHTRвҲ’1вҖӢ(Ојi,Ојh1,Ојh2,Ој). subscriptsuperscriptрқ’һ1TRрқңҮtensor-producttensor-productsubscriptsuperscriptрқ’һ1NLOрқңҮрқҗҪsubscriptрқҗ»NLOрқңҮрқҗҪsubscriptsuperscriptрқҗ»1TRsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘–subscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ1subscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ2рқңҮdisplaystyleC-1rm TR(mu)=C-1rm NLO(mu)otimes JHrm NLO(mu)otimes JH-1rm TR(mui,muh1,muh2,mu). Setting Ојi=ОјsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘–рқңҮmui=mu or Ојh1=Ојh2=ОјsubscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ1subscriptрқңҮsubscriptв„Һ2рқңҮmuh1=muh2=mu, we can examine the effects of resumming the Sudakov factors or the jet function separately, as shown in Fig. . We find that the resummation of Sudakov factor and jet function have opposite effects on the matching. To estimate the uncertainty from higher-order perturbation theory, we vary the initial scale of resummation by a factor of 2вҲ’1,2superscript212sqrt2-1,sqrt2. When the contribution from large logarithmic terms at higher orders of perturbation theory are significant, the resummed results will become sensitive to the choice of initial scales, indicating that the perturbation theory does not work or converge, so the matched DA is no longer reliable. We show the scale variation for the hard scale ОјhsubscriptрқңҮв„Һmuh and semi-hard scale ОјisubscriptрқңҮрқ‘–mui in Fig. . The scale dependence are smaller near x=0.5рқ‘Ҙ0.5x=0.5, but when approaching the endpoints, it becomes very large, indicating that the perturbation theory breaks down. We find that the scale variation for kaon DA from Pz=2.3subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2.3Pz=2.3GeV data is still controllable at xвүҲ0.2рқ‘Ҙ0.2xapprox 0.2. For pion with lower momentum Pz=1.8subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§1.8Pz=1.8GeV, the uncertainty grows much faster, thus the reliable range shrinks. Calculating the same observable with larger hadron momentum helps extend the range of reliability for our calculation. We consider the systematic error from the choice of zsвҲҲ[2вҖӢa,3вҖӢa]subscriptрқ‘§рқ‘ 2рқ‘Һ3рқ‘Һzsin[2a,3a] in the hybrid scheme, the model-dependence in the long-tail extrapolation, and the scale variation cвҲҲ2вҲ’1,1,2рқ‘җsuperscript2112cinsqrt2-1,1,sqrt2 in the hard and semi-hard scales with the replacement Ојi,hвҶ’cвҖӢОјi,hвҶ’subscriptрқңҮрқ‘–в„Һрқ‘җsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘–в„Һmui,hto cmui,h. For each of these choices, we obtain П•iвҖӢ(x)subscriptitalic-П•рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙphii(x) with a statistical error band. The systematic error band is obtained by requiring the total error band to cover all П•iвҖӢ(x)subscriptitalic-П•рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙphii(x) results. Figure shows the relative uncertainties as a function of xрқ‘Ҙx. Truncating at 10%percent1010% overall uncertainty, we find that the reliable range of our calculation is roughly xвҲҲ[0.25,0.75]рқ‘Ҙ0.250.75xin[0.25,0.75] for pion, and xвҲҲ[0.2,0.8]рқ‘Ҙ0.20.8xin[0.2,0.8] for kaon. Combining these results, we show the final results of pion DA and kaon DA and their comparison in Fig. .
content_sections.4 Matching to the light-cone DA 4.2 High: With the LaMET method, we have calculated the pion DA for a range of xвҲҲ[0.25,0.75]рқ‘Ҙ0.250.75xin[0.25,0.75] for pion and xвҲҲ[0.2,0.8]рқ‘Ҙ0.20.8xin[0.2,0.8] for kaon. Beyond this range, the perturbation theory no longer converges, and the power corrections in Eq. are increasingly important. Both effects prevent us from extracting the distribution closer to the endpoint with current hadron momentum PzвҲј2similar-tosubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2Pzsim 2GeV. Although the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence is not directly calculable in this region, it has been proposed to constrain the endpoint regions with the global information of DA, such as the lower moments or short distance correlations, extracted from SDFcitebib.bib58, bib.bib35. In coordinate space, the perturbation theory still works at zвүӘОӣQCDвҲ’1much-less-thanрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD1zllLambdarm QCD-1, and the power corrections рқ’ӘвҖӢ(znвҖӢОӣQCDn)рқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘§рқ‘ӣsubscriptsuperscriptОӣрқ‘ӣQCDO(znLambdanrm QCD) are also suppressed at short range. Thus the short distance correlations, along with the already determined mid-xрқ‘Ҙx distribution, can be utilized to better constrain the distribution in the endpoint regions, thus allowing us to roughly estimate the higher moments of DA. Here we use a simple power-law model for the distribution near the endpoint, П•вҖӢ(xвҶ’0,1)вҲқAвҖӢxmвҖӢ(1вҲ’x)nproportional-toitalic-П•вҶ’рқ‘Ҙ01рқҗҙsuperscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҡsuperscript1рқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣphi(xto 0,1)propto Axm(1-x)n. Then we get a full П•вҖӢ(x,Ој,m,n)italic-П•рқ‘ҘрқңҮрқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣphi(x,mu,m,n) in [0,1]01[0,1] depending on the unknown parameters mрқ‘ҡm and nрқ‘ӣn. For the pion we set m=nрқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣm=n since its DA is symmetric around x=0.5рқ‘Ҙ0.5x=0.5. By Fourier transforming П•вҖӢ(x,Ој,m,n)italic-П•рқ‘ҘрқңҮрқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣphi(x,mu,m,n) to coordinate space, and implementing the RG-improved matching using SDF, we obtain the quasi-DA correlations HвҖӢ(z,m,n)рқҗ»рқ‘§рқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣH(z,m,n). HвҖӢ(z,m,n)рқҗ»рқ‘§рқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣdisplaystyle H(z,m,n) =вҲ«рқ‘‘ОҪвҖӢрқ’өвҖӢ(ОҪ,z2,Ој,zвҖӢPz)вҖӢвҲ«PzвҖӢрқ‘‘zвҖӢeiвҖӢ(12вҲ’x)вҖӢОҪвҖӢzвҖӢPzвҖӢП•вҖӢ(x,Ој,m,n),absentdifferential-dрқңҲрқ’өрқңҲsuperscriptрқ‘§2рқңҮрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§differential-dрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–12рқ‘ҘрқңҲрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§italic-П•рқ‘ҘрқңҮрқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣdisplaystyle=int dnuZ(nu,z2,mu,zPz)int Pzdz ei(frac12-x)nu zPzphi(x,mu,m,n), where рқ’өвҖӢ(ОҪ,z2,Ој,zвҖӢPz)рқ’өрқңҲsuperscriptрқ‘§2рқңҮрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Z(nu,z2,mu,zPz) is the matching kernel for coordinate space correlationscitebib.bib74, bib.bib25, bib.bib35. Then the parameters m,nрқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣm,n can be obtained by fitting the lattice matrix elements HRвҖӢ(z)superscriptрқҗ»рқ‘…рқ‘§HR(z) to HвҖӢ(z,m,n)рқҗ»рқ‘§рқ‘ҡрқ‘ӣH(z,m,n). The parameters of pion DA are fitted using the real part of the lattice matrix elements because we have enforced the iso-spin symmetry, while the kaon DA model are constrained by both real and imaginary parts of the lattice matrix elements. After determining the parameters mрқ‘ҡm and nрқ‘ӣn, we can estimate the higher moments of DA. The Mellin moments вҹЁОҫiвҹ©delimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscriptрқңүрқ‘–xii and Gegenbauer moments aisubscriptрқ‘Һрқ‘–ai are calculated by вҹЁОҫiвҹ©=вҲ«01рқ‘‘xвҖӢП•вҖӢ(x)вҖӢ(2вҖӢxвҲ’1)i,delimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscriptрқңүрқ‘–superscriptsubscript01differential-dрқ‘Ҙitalic-П•рқ‘Ҙsuperscript2рқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘–displaystylexii=int01dxphi(x)(2x-1)i, ai=вҲ«01рқ‘‘xвҖӢП•вҖӢ(x)вҖӢCi3/2вҖӢ[2вҖӢxвҲ’1]вҖӢ4вҖӢ(i+3/2)3вҖӢ(i+1)вҖӢ(i+2),subscriptрқ‘Һрқ‘–superscriptsubscript01differential-dрқ‘Ҙitalic-П•рқ‘Ҙsubscriptsuperscriptрқҗ¶32рқ‘–delimited-2рқ‘Ҙ14рқ‘–323рқ‘–1рқ‘–2displaystyle ai=int01dxphi(x)C3/2i[2x-1]frac4(i+3/2)3(i+1)(i+2), where Ci3/2вҖӢ[2вҖӢxвҲ’1]subscriptsuperscriptрқҗ¶32рқ‘–delimited-2рқ‘Ҙ1C3/2i[2x-1] are the Gegenbauer Polynomials. The results are summarized in Table. The second moment of our kaon DA is consistent with the local twist-2 operators by RQCD collaborationcitebib.bib19, but the second moment of our pion DA is much larger than their calculation. In a previous work on the moment analysis of the same pion DA datacitebib.bib25 with NLO matching, it is suggested that the power-law parameter mрқ‘ҡm lies between 0.20.20.2 and 0.320.320.32 with different analysis choices, consistent with our estimate m=0.31вҖӢрқ‘ҡ0.316m=0.31 in this calculation. Our second moment and fourth moment are also in 2ПғрқңҺsigma agreement with their analysis of вҹЁОҫ2вҹ©=0.287вҖӢвҖӢdelimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscriptрқңү20.28766xi2=0.287 and вҹЁОҫ4вҹ©=0.14вҖӢвҖӢdelimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscriptрқңү40.1433xi4=0.14. Note that the fitted second Mellin moments вҹЁОҫ2вҹ©delimited-вҹЁвҹ©superscriptрқңү2xi2 for the pion and kaon data in this approach agree with those fitted from RG-invariant ratios in Fig. . Such consistency in the lowest moments provide us more confidence about our estimate of higher moments. From both moments and the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence, the pion DA is flatter than kaon, showing the non-negligible quark mass dependence of the meson DAs. The kaon is only slightly skewed as mрқ‘ҡm and nрқ‘ӣn are very close, and the first Mellin moment is consistent with zero. Higher moments estimated from this approach can be used as inputs to the pion and kaon phenomenology. An example is to calculate the pion transition form factor FПҖвҖӢОівҖӢОівҲ—вҖӢ(Q2)subscriptрқҗ№рқңӢрқӣҫsuperscriptрқӣҫsuperscriptрқ‘„2Fpigammagamma*(Q2) at large Q2superscriptрқ‘„2Q2, which can be factorized as, FвҖӢ(Q2)=2вҖӢfПҖ6вҖӢQ2вҖӢвҲ«01П•вҖӢ(x,Ој)вҖӢTвҖӢ(x,Ој,Q2),рқҗ№superscriptрқ‘„22subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢ6superscriptрқ‘„2superscriptsubscript01italic-П•рқ‘ҘрқңҮрқ‘Үрқ‘ҘрқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘„2displaystyle F(Q2)=fracsqrt2fpi6Q2int01phi(x,mu)T(x,mu,Q2), where the hard kernel TвҖӢ(x,Ој,Q2)рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘрқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘„2T(x,mu,Q2) has been calculated up to 2-loop order citebib.bib83. The convolution has also been formulated in terms of Gagenbauer coefficients with the hard coefficients cnsubscriptрқ‘җрқ‘ӣcn provided up to 2-loopcitebib.bib83, Q2вҖӢFвҖӢ(Q2)=2вҖӢfПҖвҖӢвҲ‘n=0,2,вҖҰanвҖӢ(Ој)вҖӢcnвҖӢ(Ој,Q). superscriptрқ‘„2рқҗ№superscriptрқ‘„22subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢsubscriptрқ‘ӣ02вҖҰsubscriptрқ‘Һрқ‘ӣрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘җрқ‘ӣрқңҮрқ‘„displaystyle Q2F(Q2)=sqrt2fpisumn=0,2,dotsan(mu)cn(mu,Q). We truncate the above equation at n=6рқ‘ӣ6n=6 and show the result from our estimate of moments in Fig. . The scale variation is examined by choosing ОјвҲҲQ/2,Q,2вҖӢQрқңҮрқ‘„2рқ‘„2рқ‘„muinQ/2,Q,2Q, with the corresponding anвҖӢ(Ој)subscriptрқ‘Һрқ‘ӣрқңҮan(mu) evolved from 222GeV to ОјрқңҮmu using their anomalous dimensions up to 3 loopscitebib.bib76. Our band is in good agreement with data from Bellecitebib.bib10 from Q2>4вҖӢGeV2superscriptрқ‘„24superscriptGeV2Q2>4rmGeV2. Note that our estimation of higher moments are model-dependent. For our pion momentum PzвүҲ1.8subscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§1.8Pzapprox 1.8GeV, LaMET only provides reliable prediction in a range of xвҲҲ[0.25,0.75]рқ‘Ҙ0.250.75xin[0.25,0.75]. In principle, this range is not reaching close enough to the endpoint for us to model the DA as a simple power-law function. Moreover, since the lattice spacing is not fine enough, we only have three data points of spatial correlation function at zвүӨ3вҖӢaрқ‘§3рқ‘Һzleq 3a that stays within the perturbative region, thus a complicated model could hardly be determined by this approach. To provide a more reliable estimate for the overall shape and higher moments of DA, we should include more calculations on finer lattice spacings and with higher hadron momenta.
content_sections.5 Conclusion: In this work, we present a lattice QCD calculation of the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependent pion and kaon DAs in the framework of LaMET. This calculation is performed on a fine lattice of a=0.076рқ‘Һ0.076a=0.076fm at physical pion mass, and has used the state-of-the-art analysis methods, including the recently developed resummation methods of small-momentum logarithms. With the pion boosted to 1.81.81.8GeV and kaon boosted to 2.32.32.3GeV, we are able to calculate a range of xвҲҲ[x0,1вҲ’x0]рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ01subscriptрқ‘Ҙ0xin[x0,1-x0] with x0=0.25subscriptрқ‘Ҙ00.25x0=0.25 for pion and x0=0.2subscriptрқ‘Ҙ00.2x0=0.2 for kaon with systematics under control. Beyond this range, the perturbation theory and the power-expansion of the factorization theorem are no longer reliable, thus the DA cannot be directly accessed. Our results suggest the pion DA is flatter than the kaon DA, and the asymmetry in the kaon DA is small. Using complementarity, we estimate higher moments of the pion and kaon DAs by combining our calculation with short-distance factorization. The second moments are consistent with the OPE fits to the RG-invariant ratios of the matrix elements.

sorted_papers.title:
Flavor decomposition of the nucleon unpolarized, helicity and
  transversity parton distribution functions from lattice QCD simulations
sorted_papers.abstract:
We present results on the quark unpolarized, helicity and transversity parton
distributions functions of the nucleon. We use the quasi-parton distribution
approach within the lattice QCD framework and perform the computation using an
ensemble of twisted mass fermions with the strange and charm quark masses tuned
to approximately their physical values and light quark masses giving pion mass
of 260 MeV. We use hierarchical probing to evaluate the disconnected quark
loops. We discuss identification of ground state dominance, the Fourier
transform procedure and convergence with the momentum boost. We find non-zero
results for the disconnected isoscalar and strange quark distributions. The
determination of the quark parton distribution and in particular the strange
quark contributions that are poorly known provide valuable input to the
structure of the nucleon.

sections.28: Iwasaki,В Renormalization Group Analysis of Lattice Theories and Improved Lattice Action: Two-Dimensional Nonlinear O(N) Sigma Model,В Nucl
sections.37: Wilcox,В Noise methods for flavor singlet quantities,В inВ Interdisciplinary Workshop on Numerical Challenges in Lattice QCDВ (1999)В arXiv:hep-lat/9911013 .

sections.48: MГӨrkisch etВ al.,В Measurement of the Weak Axial-Vector Coupling Constant in the Decay of Free Neutrons Using a Pulsed Cold Neutron Beam,В Phys

qualitative: The Fourier transform from coordinate space to momentum fraction x is a key step in reconstructing parton distribution functions (PDFs) from lattice QCD simulations using the quasi-PDF approach.

The quasi-PDF method involves computing matrix elements of non-local quark operators in coordinate space with a spatial Wilson line, which are then Fourier transformed to obtain the x-dependence of the PDFs.

The Fourier transform procedure is applied to the spatially non-local matrix elements of the quark bilinear operators, with the momentum boost used to access different values of the momentum fraction x.
Convergence of the Fourier transform with respect to the momentum boost and the spatial extent of the Wilson line is essential for reliable extraction of the PDFs.
The use of hierarchical probing enables the evaluation of disconnected quark loops, which are necessary for flavor decomposition, including strange and isoscalar contributions.
The reconstruction of the PDFs from lattice data requires careful treatment of ground state dominance and the matching between quasi-PDFs and light-cone PDFs.
quantitative: The momentum fraction x is obtained via the Fourier transform: x = p / P, where p is the momentum boost and P is the total momentum of the nucleon in the lattice frame.
The spatial extent of the Wilson line used in the non-local operator is typically L вүҲ 2.5 fm (in physical units), and the momentum boost is varied in steps of О”p вүҲ 2ПҖ / L.
The pion mass in the simulation ensemble is 260 MeV, and the strange and charm quark masses are tuned to their physical values.
The disconnected contributions to the isoscalar and strange quark distributions are found to be non-zero, indicating non-trivial flavor structure in the nucleon.
The renormalization and matching procedures for the singlet case involve corrections due to mixing with the gluon PDF, with recent progress in factorization and renormalization prescriptions.

Constantia Alexandrou (Univ. of Cyprus and The Cyprus Inst.), Martha
  Constantinou (Temple Univ.), Kyriakos Hadjiyiannakou (Univ. of Cyprus and The

content_sections.I Introduction: Parton distribution functions (PDFs) are the foundation for understanding the structure of hadrons in terms of their partonic content. Together with the generalized parton distributions (GPDs) and the transverse-momentum dependent distributions (TMDs) form a set of quantities that are needed for the mapping of hadrons, in the coordinate and momentum space. The most well-constraint distribution functions are the PDFs, which depend only on the momentum fraction carried by the partons. These can be obtained from a number of scattering processes and have a wide kinematical coverage (see, e. g. , Ref. citebib.bib1, bib.bib2). Furthermore, the individual-flavor contributions are also studied in phenomenological fits for the colinear PDFs. The present calculation is motivated by the fact that not all PDFs are well-constrained from global analyses. The number of available experimental data sets in the case of the transversity is less by рқ’ӘвҖӢрқ’Ә10cal O compared to the helicity, and рқ’ӘвҖӢрқ’Ә100cal O compared to the unpolarized PDFs. In addition, isolating the strange-quark PDF from the down-quark PDFs can be challenging, as most of the high-energy processes cannot differentiate between the two flavors. For example, there is a disagreement on the sign of the strange-quark helicity PDF, О”вҖӢsвҖӢ(x)+О”вҖӢsВҜвҖӢ(x)О”рқ‘ рқ‘ҘО”ВҜрқ‘ рқ‘ҘDelta s(x)+Deltabars(x), from analysis of polarized inclusive deep inelastic scatteringcitebib.bib3, bib.bib4 and global analyses of inclusive and semi-inclusive deep inelastic scattering data setscitebib.bib5, bib.bib6, bib.bib7, bib.bib8. The large uncertainties in the strange PDFs have an effect on other quantities, such as the Wрқ‘ҠW-boson mass and the determination of the CKM matrix element VcвҖӢssubscriptрқ‘үрқ‘җрқ‘ Vcscitebib.bib9, bib.bib10. Therefore, calculations of the individual-quark PDFs from lattice QCD can, eventually, be used as input in analysis requiring knowledge of PDFs. The flavor decomposition of proton charges and form factors had been under investigation in the last few years with calculations of disconnected diagrams using ensembles at or near the physical values for the quark masses(physical point)citebib.bib11, bib.bib12, bib.bib13, bib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib16, bib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib22, bib.bib23, bib.bib24. Such a success in lattice calculations of hadron structure is partly due to available computational resources, but also due to novel methodologies to extract the individual quark Mellin moments and form factors. A notable example is the hierarchical probingcitebib.bib25, which improves the signal significantly. In this work, we extend hierarchical probing to non-local operators. Such an approach was shown to be successful in our first calculation on the helicity PDFcitebib.bib26. Matrix elements of non-local operators are of great interest in recent years. These can be related to light-cone PDFs through a factorization and matching procedure. Methods to access the xрқ‘Ҙx dependence of PDFs, such as the quasi-PDFscitebib.bib27, bib.bib28, pseudo-ITDscitebib.bib29, and current-current correlatorscitebib.bib30, bib.bib31 are now well established. Progress in terms of the renormalizability, renormalization prescription, and factorization of light-cone PDFs has been made, alleviating major sources of systematic uncertainties. For their application in lattice QCD see the recent reviews of Ref. citebib.bib32, bib.bib33, bib.bib34. Results from lattice QCD simulations on the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of PDFs are very promising, and therefore, their flavor decomposition is the extension of these investigations. The matching for the singlet case, as well as the mixing with the gluon PDFs have been recently addressedcitebib.bib35, bib.bib36. In Ref. citebib.bib26 we presented the first calculation for the helicity PDFs including disconnected contributions and the flavor decomposition for the up-, down- and strange-quark PDFs. Here we extend the calculation to the three types of collinear PDFs, that is the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs. While such calculations are becoming feasible, there are a number of computational challenges before taming the statistical uncertainties. To date, calculations of disconnected contributions for matrix elements of non-local operators at the physical point do not exist. The paper is organized as follows: Section presents the methodology of extracting the nucleon matrix elements, the renormalization and matching. Section focuses on the details of the calculation and the techniques employed. The results for the disconnected and connected matrix elements are presented in Sections and , respectively. In Section, we extract the vector, axial and tensor charges using the data with zero length for the Wilson line. The PDF reconstruction and flavor decomposition is given in Section. Finally, we give out conclusions in Section.
content_sections.II Methodology II.1 Nucleon bare matrix: The main component of this study is the calculation of the nucleon matrix elements of non-local operators, that is hО“ОҘвҖӢ(z,P3)=вҹЁNвҖӢ(P3)|ПҲВҜвҖӢ(z)вҖӢОҘвҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(z)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ|NвҖӢ(P3)вҹ©,superscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3quantum-operator-productрқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ғ3ВҜрқң“рқ‘§ОҘО“рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§рқң“0рқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ғ3hGammaUpupsilon(z,P3)=braketN(P3)overlinepsi(z)Upupsilon,Gamma,W(z)psiN(P3), where |NвҖӢ(P3)вҹ©ketрқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ғ3|N(P3) is the nucleon state with momentum boost along the zрқ‘§z-direction, i. e. PвҶ’=(0,0,P3)вҶ’рқ‘ғ00subscriptрқ‘ғ3vecP=(0,0,P3). The fermionic field ПҲвҖӢ(x)вүЎПҲвҖӢ(xвҶ’,t)рқң“рқ‘Ҙрқң“вҶ’рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ўpsi(x)equivpsi(vecx,t) can be either the light quark doublet ПҲвүЎ(u,d)Tрқң“superscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқ‘Үpsiequiv(u,d)T or the strange quark field ПҲвҖӢ(x)вүЎsвҖӢ(x)рқң“рқ‘Ҙрқ‘ рқ‘Ҙpsi(x)equiv s(x). The Wilson line WвҖӢ(z)рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§W(z) is constructed in the direction parallel to the nucleon boost PвҶ’вҶ’рқ‘ғvecP and extends from zero length to up to half of the lattice, L/2рқҗҝ2L/2, in both positive and negative directions. The Dirac structure of the operator, О“О“Gamma, acts in spin space and depends on the type of the collinear PDF under study. Without loss of generality, one can take that the momentum boost is in the zрқ‘§z-direction (kрқ‘ҳk). Based on this, we employ the following О“О“Gamma matrices: вҖў О“=Оі0О“superscriptрқӣҫ0Gamma=gamma0 for the unpolarized distribution qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙq(x); вҖў О“=Оі5вҖӢОі3О“superscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқӣҫ3Gamma=gamma5gamma3 for the helicity distribution О”вҖӢqвҖӢ(x)О”рқ‘һрқ‘ҘDelta q(x); вҖў О“=Пғ3вҖӢjО“subscriptрқңҺ3рқ‘—Gamma=sigma3j with jвү 3рқ‘—3jneq 3 for the transversity distribution ОҙвҖӢqвҖӢ(x)рқӣҝрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdelta q(x). We calculate both the isovector and isoscalar quark contributions to Eq. , and, therefore, we introduce the superscript ОҘОҘUpupsilon for the matrix elements. The matrix ОҘОҘUpupsilon acts on the light quark sector and takes the value П„3вҖӢsuperscriptрқңҸ3double-struck-рқҹҷtau3, for the isovector (isoscalar) distribution, where П„3=diagвҖӢ(1,вҲ’1)superscriptрқңҸ3diag11tau3=rm diag(1,-1), is the third Pauli matrix. The matrix elements are computed from the ratio of three- and two-point functions defined as C2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;ts,0)=рқ’«ОұвҖӢОІвҖӢвҲ‘xвҶ’eвҲ’iвҖӢPвҶ’вӢ…xвҶ’вҖӢвҹЁО©|NОұвҖӢ(xвҶ’,ts)вҖӢNВҜОІвҖӢ(0вҶ’,0)|О©вҹ©C3вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;tins;ts,0)=рқ’«ОұвҖӢОІвҖӢвҲ‘xвҶ’,yвҶ’eвҲ’iвҖӢPвҶ’вӢ…xвҶ’вҖӢвҹЁО©|NОұвҖӢ(xвҶ’,ts)вҖӢрқ’ӘвҖӢ(yвҶ’,tins;z)вҖӢNВҜОІвҖӢ(0вҶ’,0)|О©вҹ©,subscriptрқҗ¶2рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0subscriptрқ’«рқӣјрқӣҪsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–вҶ’рқ‘ғвҶ’рқ‘Ҙquantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘ҒрқӣјвҶ’рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ subscriptВҜрқ‘ҒрқӣҪвҶ’00О©subscriptрқҗ¶3рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўinssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0subscriptрқ’«рқӣјрқӣҪsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҘвҶ’рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–вҶ’рқ‘ғвҶ’рқ‘Ҙquantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘ҒрқӣјвҶ’рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ’ӘвҶ’рқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘Ўinsрқ‘§subscriptВҜрқ‘ҒрқӣҪвҶ’00О©beginsplitC2pt(vecP;ts,0)=Palphabetasumlimitsvecxe-ivecPcdotvecxbraketOmegaNalpha(vecx,ts)overlineNbeta(vec0,0)Omega C3pt(vecP;trm ins;ts,0)=tildePalphabetasumlimitsvecx,vecye-ivecPcdotvecxbraketOmegaNalpha(vecx,ts)O(vecy,trm ins;z)overlineNbeta(vec0,0)Omega,endsplit where tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts is the source-sink separation, and tinssubscriptрқ‘Ўinstrm ins the insertion time of the three-point function. We use the proton interpolating field NОұ=ОөaвҖӢbвҖӢcвҖӢuОұaвҖӢ(x)вҖӢ(dbвҖӢTвҖӢ(x)вҖӢрқ’һвҖӢОі5вҖӢucвҖӢ(x))subscriptрқ‘ҒрқӣјsuperscriptрқңҖрқ‘Һрқ‘Ҹрқ‘җsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ўрқӣјрқ‘Һрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘‘рқ‘Ҹрқ‘Үрқ‘Ҙрқ’һsuperscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘җрқ‘ҘNalpha=varepsilonabcualphaa(x)left(dbT(x)Cgamma5uc(x)right) with рқ’һ=Оі0вҖӢОі2рқ’һsuperscriptрқӣҫ0superscriptрқӣҫ2C=gamma0gamma2. The three-point function projector depends on the operator under study and can be found in Table and for the two-point function we use рқ’«=(1ВұОі0)/2рқ’«plus-or-minus1subscriptрқӣҫ02cal P=(1pmgamma0)/2. To increase the number of measurements for the disconnected diagrams, we average the three- and two- point functions over plus and minus parity projectors. The operator рқ’Әрқ’ӘO is defined as рқ’ӘвҖӢ(yвҶ’,tins;z)=ПҲВҜвҖӢ(yвҶ’+zвҖӢz,tins)вҖӢОҘвҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(yвҶ’+zвҖӢz,yвҶ’)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(yвҶ’,П„),рқ’ӘвҶ’рқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘Ўinsрқ‘§ВҜрқң“вҶ’рқ‘Ұрқ‘§рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ЎinsОҘО“рқ‘ҠвҶ’рқ‘Ұрқ‘§рқ‘§вҶ’рқ‘Ұрқң“вҶ’рқ‘ҰрқңҸO(vecy,trm ins;z)=barpsi(vecy+zhatz,trm ins)Upupsilon,Gamma,W(vecy+zhatz,vecy)psi(vecy,tau),, and is inserted in the three-point function of Eq. . The Wick contractions lead to two topologically different diagrams, as shown in Fig. and . In Fig. , we also show pictorially the two-point function of Eq. . For the case that the fermionic field in Eq. is ПҲ=(u,d)Tрқң“superscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқ‘Үpsi=(u,d)T and ОҘ=П„3ОҘsuperscriptрқңҸ3Upupsilon=tau3, we obtain the matrix elements for the isovector distribution uвҲ’dрқ‘ўрқ‘‘u-d, which receive contribution from the connected diagram only (Fig. ). However, in the case where ОҘ=рқҹҷОҘdouble-struck-рқҹҷUpupsilon=1, the three-point function takes contributions from both connected and disconnected diagrams. For the nucleon, the strange-quark contribution comes exclusively from the disconnected diagram. We emphasize that, disconnected contributions have a considerably smaller signal-to-noise ratio compared to the connected ones and their evaluation requires the use of stochastic and gauge noise reduction techniques described in detail in Sec. . The ratio of three- over two-point functions becomes, вҹЁC3вҖӢpвҖӢt(P3;tins;ts,0вҹ©вҹЁC2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(P3;ts,0)вҹ©=0вүӘtinsвүӘts(hО“ОҘ)bareвҖӢ(z,P3),fracbraketC3pt(P3;trm ins;ts,0braketC2pt(P3;ts,0)stackrelscriptstyle 0ll trm insll ts=left(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare(z,P3),,, and is used to obtain the matrix elements of Eq. . This relation is meaningful for the ground-state contribution. To isolate the latter, we apply constant (plateau) fits in a range where the operator insertion time is large enough, and away from the source and sink. Besides the plateau fit method, we employ different techniques allowing the extraction of the nucleon matrix element, as described in Sec. .
content_sections.II Methodology II.2 Non-perturbative ren: The bare matrix elements of Eq. must be renormalized in coordinate space prior to obtaining the quasi-PDFs, which are defined in the momentum space (see Sec. ). The renormalization of both the non-singlet and singlet quantities is multiplicative. In this work, we use the non-singlet renormalization function, as the difference with the singlet is expected to be smallcitebib.bib37, which was demonstrated numerically for local operatorscitebib.bib20, bib.bib18. The mixing between the unpolarized and helicity singlet-quark PDFs with the gluon PDFs arises at the matching level because there is no additional non-local ultraviolet divergence in the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib38, bib.bib35, bib.bib36. To renormalize the matrix elements we apply the regularization independent (RI) scheme, and use the momentum source methodcitebib.bib39 that offers high statistical accuracy. More details on the setup can be found in Refs. citebib.bib40, bib.bib41. In Refs. citebib.bib42, bib.bib43, we proposed an extension of the renormalization prescription to include non-local operators, which we also follow in this work. The conditions for the renormalization functions of the non-local operator, ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma, and the quark field, Zqsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һZq, are (ZqimprвҖӢ(Ој0))вҲ’1вҖӢZО“вҖӢ(z,Ој0)вҖӢTrвҖӢ[рқ’ұО“вҖӢ(p,z)вҖӢpМё]|p2=Ој02=TrвҖӢ[рқ’ұО“BornвҖӢ(p,z)вҖӢpМё]|p2=Ој02,evaluated-atsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һimprsubscriptрқңҮ01subscriptрқ‘ҚО“рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Trdelimited-subscriptрқ’ұО“рқ‘қрқ‘§italic-pМёsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮ02evaluated-atTrdelimited-superscriptsubscriptрқ’ұО“Bornрқ‘қрқ‘§italic-pМёsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮ02displaystyleleft(Zqrm impr(mu0)right)-1,ZGamma(z,mu0),rm Trleft[cal VGamma(p,z),notpright]Bigr|p2=mu02=rm Trleft[cal VGammarm Born(p,z),notpright]Bigr|p2=mu02,, ZqimprвҖӢ(Ој0)=(112вҖӢTrвҖӢ[(SвҖӢ(p))вҲ’1вҖӢSBornвҖӢ(p)]вҲ’dвҖӢZqвҲһвҖӢ(p))|p2=Ој02. subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қimprрқ‘һsubscriptрқңҮ0evaluated-at112Trdelimited-superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘қ1superscriptрқ‘ҶBornрқ‘қрқ‘‘subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һрқ‘қsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮ02displaystyle Zrm imprq(mu0)=left(frac112rm Trleft[(S(p))-1,Srm Born(p)right]-dZinftyq(p)right)Bigr|p2=mu02,. qquad рқ’ұвҖӢ(p,z)рқ’ұрқ‘қрқ‘§cal V(p,z) (SвҖӢ(p)рқ‘Ҷрқ‘қS(p)) is the amputated vertex function of the operator (fermion propagator) and SBornвҖӢ(p)superscriptрқ‘ҶBornрқ‘қSrm Born(p) is the tree-level of the propagator. These conditions are applied at each value of zрқ‘§z separately. We improve Zqsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һZq, and consequently ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma, by subtracting lattice artifacts calculated to one-loop level in perturbation theory and to all orders in the lattice spacing, dвҖӢZqвҲһвҖӢ(p)рқ‘‘subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һрқ‘қdZinftyq(p). The details of the calculation can be found in Ref. citebib.bib40. The RI-type schemes are mass-independent, and therefore ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma is calculated at several ensembles with different values for the quark masses. Eventually, a chiral extrapolation is applied to remove residual artifacts related to the quark mass. Here we use five ensembles that are generated at different pion masses in the range of 350 MeV - 520MeV with a lattice volume of 243Г—48superscript24348243times 48. For the chiral extrapolation to be meaningful, all quark masses should be degenerate. Therefore, we use Nf=4subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘“4Nf=4 ensembles generated by the Extended Twisted Mass collaboration (ETMC) that are dedicated to the renormalization program. These ensembles have the same lattice spacing and action parameters as the Nf=2+1+1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘“211Nf=2+1+1 ensemble used for the production of the nucleon matrix elements. ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma depends on the RI renormalization scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, and it will be converted to MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS and evolved at a scale of choice. To reliably perform this procedure we use several values of Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, and the conversion and evolution formulas is applied on ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma obtained at each scale. We choose the initial scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0 such that discretization effects are smallcitebib.bib40. In particular, the 4-vector momentum pрқ‘қp, which is set equal to Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, has the same spatial components: p=(p0,p1,p1,p1)рқ‘қsubscriptрқ‘қ0subscriptрқ‘қ1subscriptрқ‘қ1subscriptрқ‘қ1p=(p0,p1,p1,p1). The values of p0subscriptрқ‘қ0p0 and p1subscriptрқ‘қ1p1 are chosen such that the ratio p4(p2)2superscriptрқ‘қ4superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘қ22fracp4(p2)2 is less than 0.28, as suggested in Ref. citebib.bib44. The values of aвҖӢОј0рқ‘ҺsubscriptрқңҮ0a,mu0 cover the range [1,5]15[1,5]. For each Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0 value, we apply a chiral extrapolation using the fit ZО“RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0,mПҖ)=ZО“,0RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0)+mПҖ2вҖӢZО“,1RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0),subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0subscriptрқ‘ҡрқңӢsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“0рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқңӢ2subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“1рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Zrm RIGamma(z,mu0,mpi)=Zrm RIGamma,0(z,mu0)+mpi2,Zrm RIGamma,1(z,mu0),, to extract the mass-independent ZО“,0RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“0рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Zrm RIGamma,0(z,mu0) at each value of the initial scale. ZО“,0RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“0рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Zrm RIGamma,0(z,mu0) is converted to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme and evolved to Ој=2рқңҮ2mu=2 GeV (Ој=2рқңҮ2mu=sqrt2 GeV) using the results of Ref. citebib.bib42 for the unpolarized and helicity (transversity) PDFs. The conversion and evolution depends on both the initial scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0 and the final scale in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS. The appropriate expressions have been obtained to one-loop perturbation theory in dimensional regularization. Therefore, there is residual dependence on the initial scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, which is eliminated by taking the limit (aвҖӢОј0)2вҶ’0вҶ’superscriptрқ‘ҺsubscriptрқңҮ020(a,mu0)2to 0 using a linear fit on the data in the region (aвҖӢОј0)2вҲҲ[1вҲ’2.6]superscriptрқ‘ҺsubscriptрқңҮ02delimited-12.6(a,mu0)2in[1-2.6]. The last step of the renormalization program is the conversion to a modified MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scheme (MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS), developed in Ref. citebib.bib41, and is given by рқ’өО“,0MвҖӢMSВҜвҖӢ(z,ОјВҜ)=рқ’өО“,0MSВҜвҖӢ(z,ОјВҜ)вҖӢрқ’һMSВҜ,MвҖӢMSВҜ,subscriptsuperscriptрқ’өMВҜMSО“0рқ‘§ВҜрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ’өВҜMSО“0рқ‘§ВҜрқңҮsuperscriptрқ’һВҜMSMВҜMSdisplaystylecal ZtextMoverlinetextMSGamma,0(z,barmu)=cal ZoverlinetextMSGamma,0(z,barmu),cal CoverlinetextMS,rm MoverlineMS,, where рқ’һО“MSВҜ,MвҖӢMSВҜ=1+CFвҖӢg216вҖӢПҖ2[displaystylecal CGammaoverlinerm MS,rm MoverlineMS=1+fracCFg216pi2Bigg[ eО“+eО“вҖӢlnвҒЎ(ОјВҜ24вҖӢОјF2)+eО“вҖӢ(iвҖӢПҖвҖӢ|ОјFвҖӢz|2вҖӢОјFвҖӢzвҲ’lnвҒЎ(|ОјFвҖӢz|)вҲ’CiвҖӢ(ОјFвҖӢz)вҲ’iвҖӢSiвҖӢ(ОјFвҖӢz)+lnвҒЎ(ОјFвҖӢz))subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’1О“subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’2О“superscriptВҜрқңҮ24superscriptsubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№2subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’3О“рқ‘–рқңӢsubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§2subscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§CisubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§рқ‘–SisubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§displaystyle eGamma+eGammalnleft(fracbarmu24muF2right)+eGammaleft(fracipileft|muFzright|2muFz-ln(left|muFzright|)-textCi(muFz)-itextSi(muFz)+ln(muFz)right) This scheme was introduced to satisfy particle number conservation. In Ref. citebib.bib41 we showed that the difference between MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS and MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS is numerically very small, but brings the PDFs closer to the phenomenological ones. In Eq. ОјFsubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№muF is the factorization scale set equal to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scale. CiCirm Ci, SiSirm Si, EiEirm Ei and sgnsgnrm sgn are the special functions cosine integral, sine integral, exponential integral, and sign function, respectively. The coefficients eО“(i)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–О“e(i)Gamma depend on the operator: eО“,eО“,eО“,eО“superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“1superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“3superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“4eGamma,,eGamma,,eGamma,,eGamma is вҲ’5,вҲ’3,+3,вҲ’3/253332-5,,-3,,+3,,-3/2, вҲ’7,вҲ’3,+3,вҲ’3/273332-7,,-3,,+3,,-3/2, вҲ’4,вҲ’4,+4,вҲ’4/244442-4,,-4,,+4,,-4/2, for the vector, axial and tensor operator, respectively. Due to the presence of the Wilson line, both the matrix elements and renormalization functions are complex functions. As a consequence, a complex multiplication is required to extract the renormalized matrix element, that is hО“ОҘ=ZО“MвҖӢMSВҜвӢ…(hО“ОҘ)baresuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘвӢ…subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚMВҜMSО“superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘbaredisplaystyle hGammaUpupsilon=ZtextMoverlinetextMSGammacdotleft(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare =displaystyle= (ReвҖӢ[ZО“MвҖӢMSВҜ]вҖӢReвҖӢ[(hО“ОҘ)bare]вҲ’ImвҖӢ[ZО“MвҖӢMSВҜ]вҖӢImвҖӢ[(hО“ОҘ)bare])Redelimited-subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚMВҜMSО“Redelimited-superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘbareImdelimited-subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚMВҜMSО“Imdelimited-superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘbaredisplaystyle,left(rm Re[ZtextMoverlinetextMSGamma],rm Re[left(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare]-rm Im[ZtextMoverlinetextMSGamma],rm Im[left(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare]right) For simplicity in the notation, the dependence on zрқ‘§z, P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3, scheme and scale is implied. As can be seen, the real (imaginary) part of the renormalized matrix elements are not simple multiples of the real (imaginary) part of the bare matrix element. Therefore, controlling systematic uncertainties in the renormalization is an important aspect of the calculation.
content_sections.II Methodology II.3 Quasi-PDFs and match: Quasi-PDFs are defined as the Fourier transform of the renormalized nucleon matrix elements in Eq. with respect to the Wilson line length zрқ‘§z qвҖӢ(x,P3)=вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢz4вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢzвҖӢP3вҖӢhО“ОҘвҖӢ(z,P3). рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқ‘§4рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3tildeq(x,P3)=intlimits-inftyinftyfracdz4pie-ixzP3,hGammaUpupsilon(z,P3),. Note that the renormalized matrix elements, hО“ОҘsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘhGammaUpupsilon, depend on the scheme and scale, which also propagates to qвҖӢ(x,P3)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3tildeq(x,P3). For simplicity in the notation, this dependence is implied. As mentioned in the previous paragraph, the matrix elements are renormalized in the MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS scheme and evolved to 2GeV (22sqrt2 GeV) for the unpolarized and helicity (transversity) PDFs. On the lattice, we can only evaluate the matrix elements for discrete and finite values of zрқ‘§z. Therefore, the integral of Eq. is replaced by a discrete sum over a finite number of Wilson line lengths qвҖӢ(x,P3)=вҲ‘вҲ’zmaxzmaxdвҖӢz4вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢzвҖӢP3вҖӢhО“ОҘвҖӢ(z,P3),рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptsubscriptрқ‘§maxsubscriptрқ‘§maxрқ‘‘рқ‘§4рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3tildeq(x,P3)=sumlimits-zrm maxzrm maxfracdz4pie-ixzP3,hGammaUpupsilon(z,P3),, where dвҖӢz/a=1рқ‘‘рқ‘§рқ‘Һ1dz/a=1. For the summation in Eq. to accurately reproduce Eq. , both the real and imaginary parts of the matrix element should be zero beyond zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max. Practically, this is not always possible due to the finite momentum boost and limited volume in the lattice formulation. The choice of the cutoff zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max, which is anyway limited up to L/2рқҗҝ2L/2, requires an extensive study. We note that systematic effects related to the reconstruction of the PDFs is operator dependent, as each matrix element may have different large-zрқ‘§z behavior. We will show the results of such analysis in Sec. . From the finite-momentum quasi-PDF, it is possible to obtain the light-cone parton distribution function (infinite momentum) through the so-called matching procedure. This is accomplished through a convolution of the quasi distribution with a kernel evaluated in continuum perturbation theory within the large momentum effective theory (LaMET)citebib.bib45, bib.bib33. The matching formula reads qвҖӢ(x,Ој)=вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢОҫ|Оҫ|вҖӢCвҖӢ(Оҫ,ОјxвҖӢP3)вҖӢqвҖӢ(xОҫ,Ој,P3),рқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқңүрқңүрқҗ¶рқңүрқңҮрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3рқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңүрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғ3q(x,mu)=int-inftyinftyfracdxi|xi|,Cleft(xi,fracmuxP3right),widetildeqleft(fracxxi,mu,P3right),, and the factorization scale ОјрқңҮmu is chosen to be the same as the renormalization scale. The matching kernel Cрқҗ¶C contains information on P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 which, in principle, is eliminated in qвҖӢ(x,Ој)рқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮq(x,mu). However, there is residual P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 due to the limitations in accessing large values of momentum from lattice QCD (see, e. g. , Ref. citebib.bib41) and the matching kernel being available to limited order in perturbation theory. Most of the calculations of the matching kernel have been performed to one-loop level (see, e. g. , Refs. citebib.bib46, bib.bib30, bib.bib47, bib.bib48, bib.bib49, bib.bib50, bib.bib51, bib.bib52). Recently, the computation of the kernel Cрқҗ¶C was extended to two loopscitebib.bib52, bib.bib53, bib.bib54, bib.bib55. In this study, we employ the kernel in the MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS-scheme which is known at one-loop levelcitebib.bib41. This matching kernel relates the quasi-PDFs defined in the MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS scheme at some scale, to the light-cone PDFs in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS at the same scale. For the unpolarized and helicity we choose a scale of 2 GeV, while for the transversity we choose 22sqrt2 GeV. To calculate the anti-quark distributions from qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙq(x), we exploit the crossing relationscitebib.bib56, that is qВҜfвҖӢ(x)=вҲ’qfвҖӢ(вҲ’x),О”вҖӢqВҜfвҖӢ(x)=О”вҖӢqfвҖӢ(вҲ’x),ОҙвҖӢqВҜfвҖӢ(x)=вҲ’ОҙвҖӢqfвҖӢ(вҲ’x). formulae-sequencesuperscriptВҜрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙformulae-sequenceО”superscriptВҜрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘ҘО”superscriptрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘ҘрқӣҝsuperscriptВҜрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙрқӣҝsuperscriptрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙbarqf(x)=-qf(-x),;;;Deltabarqf(x)=Delta qf(-x),;;;deltabarqf(x)=-delta qf(-x),.
content_sections.III Lattice setup: The computation is performed using one gauge ensemble of Nf=2+1+1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘“211Nf=2+1+1 clover-improved twisted mass fermions and the Iwasaki improved gluonic actioncitebib.bib57 generated by ETMCcitebib.bib58. Here, ПҮlTвҖӢ(x)=(u,d)subscriptsuperscriptрқң’рқ‘Үрқ‘ҷрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ўрқ‘‘chiTl(x)=(u,d) is the light quark doublet in the twisted basis, DWвҖӢ[U]subscriptрқҗ·рқ‘Ҡdelimited-рқ‘ҲDW[U] is the massless Wilson-Dirac operator and FОјвҖӢОҪвҖӢ[U]subscriptрқҗ№рқңҮрқңҲdelimited-рқ‘ҲFmunu[U] is the field strength tensor. The last term is weighted by cSWsubscriptрқ‘җSWcrm SW, the Sheikoleslami-Wohlertcitebib.bib59 clover coefficient. The heavy quark twisted mass action is similar to the light quark action in Eq. . However, it contains an additional term, proportional to the parameter ОјОҙsubscriptрқңҮрқӣҝmudelta, due to the non-degeneracy of the heavy quarks and reads StmhвҖӢ(ПҮh,ПҮВҜh,U)=a4вҖӢвҲ‘xПҮВҜhвҖӢ(x)вҖӢ[DWвҖӢ[U]+iвҖӢОјПғвҖӢОі5вҖӢП„3+mhвҲ’ОјОҙвҖӢП„1+i4вҖӢcSWвҖӢПғОјвҖӢОҪвҖӢFОјвҖӢОҪвҖӢ[U]]вҖӢПҮhвҖӢ(x),superscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ҷtmв„Һsubscriptрқң’в„ҺsubscriptВҜрқң’в„Һрқ‘Ҳsuperscriptрқ‘Һ4subscriptрқ‘ҘsubscriptВҜрқң’в„Һрқ‘Ҙdelimited-subscriptрқҗ·рқ‘Ҡdelimited-рқ‘Ҳрқ‘–subscriptрқңҮрқңҺsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқңҸ3subscriptрқ‘ҡв„ҺsubscriptрқңҮрқӣҝsubscriptрқңҸ1рқ‘–4subscriptрқ‘җSWsuperscriptрқңҺрқңҮрқңҲsuperscriptрқҗ№рқңҮрқңҲdelimited-рқ‘Ҳsubscriptрқң’в„Һрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle Srm tmh(chih,overlinechih,U)=a4sumxoverlinechih(x)left[DW[U]+imusigmagamma5tau3+mh-mudeltatau1+fraci4crm SWsigmamunuFmunu[U]right]chih(x),, where ПҮhTвҖӢ(x)=(s,c)subscriptsuperscriptрқң’рқ‘Үв„Һрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ рқ‘җchiTh(x)=(s,c). Moreover, ОјlsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘ҷmul and ОјПғsubscriptрқңҮрқңҺmusigma are the twisted mass parameter. The mass terms mlsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқ‘ҷml and mhsubscriptрқ‘ҡв„Һmh are the (untwisted) Wilson quark masses tuned to the critical value mcritsubscriptрқ‘ҡcritmrm crit (i. e. at maximal twist), which ensures automatic рқ’ӘвҖӢ(a)рқ’Әрқ‘ҺO(a) improvementcitebib.bib60 for parity even operators. The equivalent discussion for non-local operators can be found in Refs. citebib.bib38, bib.bib61, bib.bib62, bib.bib63. From now on we will use fields in the physical basis. The ensemble we use has lattice volume V=323Г—64рқ‘үsuperscript32364V=323times 64, with a lattice spacing of a=0.0938рқ‘Һ0.0938a=0.0938 fm.
