Due to QCD confinement, hвҖӢ(z,Pmaxz)в„Һрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§maxtildeh(z,Pzrm max) has a correlation length вҲј1/ОӣQCDsimilar-toabsent1subscriptОӣQCDsim 1/Lambdarm QCD, leading to an exponential decay at large zрқ‘§zcitebib.bib275. Therefore, if zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max is sufficiently large (e. g. , the proton size вҲј1similar-toabsent1sim 1 fm) for hвҖӢ(z,Pmaxz)в„Һрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§maxtildeh(z,Pzrm max) to fall to almost zero, then the truncated Fourier transform of hвҖӢ(z,Pmaxz)в„Һрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§maxtildeh(z,Pzrm max) should converge quickly, and the truncation effects mainly affect results at small xвүІ1/О»maxless-than-or-similar-toрқ‘Ҙ1subscriptрқңҶmaxxlesssim 1/lambdarm max. If hвҖӢ(z,Pmaxz)в„Һрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§maxtildeh(z,Pzrm max) exhibits exponential decay but still has a nonzero value at zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max, then one can perform a physically motivated extrapolation beyond zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm maxcitebib.bib275 to do the Fourier transform, which removes the unphysical oscillation from truncation and only affects the small-xрқ‘Ҙx region. In the momentum space, one can use LaMET expansion to calculate the PDF point by point in xрқ‘Ҙx with systematic error controlled by ОӣQCD2/(xвҖӢPmaxz)2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2superscriptрқ‘Ҙsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§max2Lambdarm QCD2/(xPzrm max)2 and ОӣQCD2/((1вҲ’x)вҖӢPmaxz)2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2superscript1рқ‘Ҙsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§max2Lambdarm QCD2/((1-x)Pzrm max)2, which gives the prediction for a cetain region of xрқ‘Ҙx, [xmin,xmax]subscriptрқ‘Ҙminsubscriptрқ‘Ҙmax[xrm min,xrm max], with a target error. In coordinate-space factorization, one expands hвҖӢ(О»,z2)в„ҺрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2tildeh(lambda,z2) in z2вҖӢОӣQCD2superscriptрқ‘§2subscriptsuperscriptОӣ2QCDz2Lambda2rm QCD. For the factorization formula to be valid, zрқ‘§z must remain in the perturbative region. For example, an estimate incitebib.bib275 gives zmaxвҲј0.3similar-tosubscriptрқ‘§max0.3zrm maxsim 0.3вҖ“0.40.40.4 fm. Although there have been observations that forming ratios of hвҖӢ(О»,z2)в„ҺрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2tildeh(lambda,z2) may cancel the higher-twist contributions at z>0.4рқ‘§0.4z>0.4 fmcitebib.bib381, this cancellation needs be quantified for precision calculations. With a finite range of quasi-LF correlations, the PDFs can be extracted through modelling the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence or more advanced techniques such as Bayesian analysiscitebib.bib89 or neural networkcitebib.bib299, bib.bib128, bib.bib160, which is similar to extracting the PDFs from experimental datacitebib.bib349, although quantifying the systematic error from fitting can be challenging. The coordinate-space factorization can also provide the extraction of the lowest moments of PDFscitebib.bib300, bib.bib414, bib.bib296, bib.bib206, where the main systematic error is controlled by z2вҖӢОӣQCD2superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2z2Lambdarm QCD2. So far, there have been very limited studies about the comparison between quasi- and pseudo-PDF analysis citebib.bib61, bib.bib16. It remains to be seen how systematic errors in the two strategies are compared and contrasted.

The multiplicative renormalizability of the nonlocal Wilson-line operators for quasi-PDFs allows a nonperturbative renormalization on the lattice, after which the continuum limit can be taken. This is an important step in the application of LaMET. One way of doing so is to perform a mass subtraction of the Wilson line firstcitebib.bib375, bib.bib245, bib.bib117, bib.bib450, bib.bib214, bib.bib215, and then renormalize the remnant UV divergences with lattice perturbation theory or other nonperturbative schemes. Another scheme which has gained more popularity in recent years is the regularization-independent momentum subtraction (RI/MOM) schemecitebib.bib149, bib.bib420, bib.bib17, bib.bib115, bib.bib343. In the coordinate space approach where |z|вүӘОӣQCDвҲ’1much-less-thanрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD1|z|llLambdarm QCD-1, the ratios of quasi-LF correlations in different statescitebib.bib390, bib.bib381, bib.bib84, bib.bib326 have also been proposed as a renormalization scheme. At large zрқ‘§z, the RI/MOM and ratio schemes introduce extra nonperturbative effects at different levels, which may distort the IR property of the original quasi-LF correlations. Due to the suppression of long-range contributions by large Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz in the Fourier transform, this nonperturbative contamination mainly affects the end-point region in xрқ‘Ҙx-space, while the existing LaMET calculations with RI/MOM scheme at moderate xрқ‘Ҙx, for example incitebib.bib21, bib.bib329, suffers less from such systematics. Nevertheless, the above complication can be avoided by switching to the hybrid schemecitebib.bib275 where one utilizes the advantages of different schemes at short and large distances. In the following, we discuss the above schemes in order, with a particular focus on the hybrid renormalization scheme. Before we proceed, it should be noted that the current-current correlators incitebib.bib165, bib.bib76, bib.bib349 do not need or have simple renormalization on the lattice, though it might be more costly to simulate them. Besides, there is another distinct method based on a redefinition of the quasi-PDF with smeared fermion and gauge fields via the gradient flowcitebib.bib367. The smeared quasi-PDF is free from UV divergences and remains finite in the continuum limit, which can be perturbatively matched onto the PDFcitebib.bib369. Nevertheless, this method awaits to be implemented on the lattice.Since the mass correction ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m includes all the linear UV divergences, it is highly favored to nonperturbatively subtract it from the quasi-PDFs. It is well known that the Wilson line renormalization is related to the additive renormalization of the static quark-antiquark potential, i. e. , ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m, especially in the context of finite temperature field theory. For a rectangle-shaped Wilson loop of dimension LГ—Tрқҗҝрқ‘ҮLtimes T in the spatial and temporal directions, its vacuum expectation value for large Tрқ‘ҮT scales as limTвҶ’вҲһWвҖӢ(L,T)=cвҖӢ(L)вҖӢeвҲ’VвҖӢ(L)вҖӢT. subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Үрқ‘Ҡрқҗҝрқ‘Үрқ‘җрқҗҝsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘үрқҗҝрқ‘ҮdisplaystylelimTtoinftyW(L,T)=c(L)e-V(L)T,. The renormalized static potential is VRвҖӢ(L)=VвҖӢ(L)+2вҖӢОҙвҖӢm,superscriptрқ‘үрқ‘…рқҗҝрқ‘үрқҗҝ2рқӣҝрқ‘ҡdisplaystyle VR(L)=V(L)+2delta m,, and ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m can be fixed by imposing the condition VRвҖӢ(L0)=0superscriptрқ‘үрқ‘…subscriptрқҗҝ00VR(L0)=0 for a particular value of L0subscriptрқҗҝ0L0. Alternatively, one can also fit ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m from the famous string potential model, VвҖӢ(L)=ПғвҖӢLвҲ’ПҖ12вҖӢLвҲ’2вҖӢОҙвҖӢm. рқ‘үрқҗҝрқңҺрқҗҝрқңӢ12рқҗҝ2рқӣҝрқ‘ҡdisplaystyle V(L)=sigma L-piover 12L-2delta m,. Apart from using the static potential to determine ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m, it was also proposed to calculate this quantity in the auxiliary вҖңheavy quarkвҖқ field theory with the following conditioncitebib.bib214, ОҙвҖӢm=ddвҖӢzвҖӢlnвҒЎTrвҖӢвҹЁQвҖӢ(x+zвҖӢnz)вҖӢQВҜвҖӢ(x)вҹ©QCD+Q|z=z0. рқӣҝрқ‘ҡevaluated-atрқ‘‘рқ‘‘рқ‘§Trsubscriptdelimited-вҹЁвҹ©рқ‘„рқ‘Ҙрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§ВҜрқ‘„рқ‘ҘQCDрқ‘„рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘§0displaystyledelta m=dover dzlnrm TrbigQ(x+znz)barQ(x)bigrm QCD+QBig|z=z0,. Other suggestions have also been made for a nonperturbative calculation of ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta mcitebib.bib275. For example, one can investigate the asymptotic large-zрқ‘§z behavior of the hadron matrix element or the single quark GreenвҖҷs function, of the vacuum expectation value of OО“вҖӢ(z,a)subscriptрқ‘ӮО“рқ‘§рқ‘ҺOGamma(z,a) in a fixed gauge. The ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m calculated from all these matrix elements will have the following dependence on the lattice spacing aрқ‘Һa, ОҙвҖӢm=mвҲ’1вҖӢ(a)/a+m0,рқӣҝрқ‘ҡsubscriptрқ‘ҡ1рқ‘Һрқ‘Һsubscriptрқ‘ҡ0delta m=m-1(a)/a+m0 , where mвҲ’1subscriptрқ‘ҡ1m-1(a) is the coefficient of the power divergence which is independent of the specific matrix element, while m0вҲјOвҖӢ(ОӣQCD)similar-tosubscriptрқ‘ҡ0рқ‘ӮsubscriptОӣQCDm0sim O(Lambdarm QCD) is finite and depends on the external state. The determination of m0subscriptрқ‘ҡ0m0 can be rather nontrivial, and in practical calculations one could adopt a fine-tuning method, such as that for the Wilson-fermion mass, to find the critical value of m0subscriptрқ‘ҡ0m0 at which the final result converges fastest in the large Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz limit. After the Wilson-line mass subtraction, there are still logarithmic UV divergences in OО“вҖӢ(z,a)subscriptрқ‘ӮО“рқ‘§рқ‘ҺOGamma(z,a). One can use lattice perturbation theory to match ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m-subtracted OО“вҖӢ(z,a)subscriptрқ‘ӮО“рқ‘§рқ‘ҺOGamma(z,a) to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS schemecitebib.bib245, bib.bib444, bib.bib149, but the convergence still needs to be examined at higher orders. Incitebib.bib214, bib.bib215, the logarithmic divergences were nonperturbatively renormalized with RI/MOM-like schemes. The Wilson-line mass-subtraction has been implemented on the lattice incitebib.bib375, bib.bib450, bib.bib214, bib.bib454, bib.bib16.The RI/MOM scheme has been widely used in lattice QCD for the renormalization of local composite quark operators that are free from power-divergent mixings citebib.bib359. It is essentially a momentum subtraction scheme in QFT and can be nonperturbatively implemented on the lattice. For an arbitrary composite quark bilinear operator OBsuperscriptрқ‘ӮрқҗөOB that is multiplicatively renormalized as OB=ZOвҖӢORsuperscriptрқ‘Ӯрқҗөsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқ‘Ӯрқ‘…OB=ZOOR, the RI/MOM scheme is defined by imposing the following condition on its off-shell quark matrix element at a subtraction scale ОјRsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘…mutiny R, ZOвҲ’1вҖӢвҹЁp|OB|pвҹ©|p2=вҲ’ОјR2=вҹЁp|O|pвҹ©tree. evaluated-atsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қ1рқ‘Ӯquantum-operator-productрқ‘қsuperscriptрқ‘Ӯрқҗөрқ‘қsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘…2subscriptquantum-operator-productрқ‘қрқ‘Ӯрқ‘қtreedisplaystyle Z-1O p|OB|pBig|p2=-mutiny R2= p|O|prm tree,. where the subscript вҖңtreeвҖқ means the tree-level matrix element in perturbation theory. If ОјRвү«ОӣQCDmuch-greater-thansubscriptрқңҮрқ‘…subscriptОӣQCDmutiny RggLambdarm QCD, ZOsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘ӮZO defined in Eq. is in the perturbative region, and we can convert it to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme order by order in perturbation theory. In this sense, ZOsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘ӮZO is not literally nonperturbative, but an all-order calculable quantity. Since the nonlocal quark bilinear operator OО“вҖӢ(z)subscriptрқ‘ӮО“рқ‘§OGamma(z) has been proven to be multiplicatively renormalizable in the coordinate space, one can also renormalize it in the RI/MOM scheme and then match the result to PDF in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS schemecitebib.bib149, bib.bib420. On the lattice, the off-shell matrix element of an operator is defined from its amputated GreenвҖҷs function, or vertex function, with off-shell quarks. For the nonlocal Wilson-line operator, the latter is Оӣ0О“вҖӢ(z,a,p)вүЎ[S0вҲ’1вҖӢ(p,a)]вҖ вҖӢвҲ‘x,yeiвҖӢpвӢ…(xвҲ’y)subscriptsuperscriptОӣО“0рқ‘§рқ‘Һрқ‘қsuperscriptdelimited-subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Ҷ10рқ‘қрқ‘ҺвҖ subscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–рқ‘қрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰdisplaystyleLambdaGamma0(z,a,p)equivleft[S-10(p,a)right]daggersumx,yeipcdot(x-y) Г—вҹЁ0|TвҖӢ[ПҲ0вҖӢ(x,a)вҖӢOО“BвҖӢ(z,a)вҖӢПҲВҜ0вҖӢ(y,a)]|0вҹ©вҖӢS0вҲ’1вҖӢ(p,a),absentquantum-operator-product0рқ‘Үdelimited-subscriptрқң“0рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Һsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ӮрқҗөО“рқ‘§рқ‘ҺsubscriptВҜрқң“0рқ‘Ұрқ‘Һ0subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Ҷ10рқ‘қрқ‘Һdisplaystyle times!big0big|Tbigl[psi0(x,a)OBGamma(z,a)barpsi0(y,a)bigr]big|0bigS-10(p,a),, where S0вҖӢ(p,a)subscriptрқ‘Ҷ0рқ‘қрқ‘ҺS0(p,a) is the bare quark propagator, and the external momentum pрқ‘қp is Euclidean on the lattice. Since GreenвҖҷs functions are not gauge invariant, one needs to fix a gauge (usually Landau gauge вҲӮвӢ…A=0вӢ…рқҗҙ0partialcdot A=0 is chosen), and the gauge dependence is expected to be canceled by the matching or scheme conversion order by order in perturbation theory. After including the quark wavefunction renormalization Zqsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һZq, which can be determined independently on the latticecitebib.bib359, Eq. is revised as ZqвҖӢZOО“вҲ’1вҖӢОӣ0О“вҖӢ(z,a,p)|p=pR=ОӣtreeО“вҖӢ(z,a,p)=О“вҖӢeiвҖӢpRвӢ…z. evaluated-atsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қ1subscriptрқ‘ӮО“subscriptsuperscriptОӣО“0рқ‘§рқ‘Һрқ‘қрқ‘қsubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘…subscriptsuperscriptОӣО“treeрқ‘§рқ‘Һрқ‘қО“superscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘қрқ‘…рқ‘§displaystyle ZqZ-1OGammaLambdaGamma0(z,a,p)Big|p=ptiny R=LambdaGammarm tree(z,a,p)=Gamma eiptiny Rcdot z,. Due to finite lattice volume and deteriorating signal-to-noise ratios at large zрқ‘§z, the available lattice data have to be truncated at zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L. As we have discussed in Sec. , the quasi-LF correlation has a correlation length ОҫzвҲјОӣQCDвҲ’1similar-tosubscriptрқңүрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD1xizsimLambdarm QCD-1 and exhibits an exponential decay at large zрқ‘§z (вҲј1similar-toabsent1sim 1 fm). If zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L is not sufficiently large and the quasi-LF correlation still has a considerable nonzero value, then a direct Fourier transform truncated at zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L will lead to unphysical oscillations and other systematics in the quasi-PDF. To improve this situation, it is suggested in the hybrid scheme to perform an extrapolation to zвҶ’вҲһвҶ’рқ‘§ztoinftycitebib.bib275. When Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz is not very large and the lattice matrix elements exhibit the exponential behavior near zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L, one can use the form вҲјeвҲ’z/Оҫzsimilar-toabsentsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘§subscriptрқңүрқ‘§sim e-z/xiz to do the extrapolation, although some algebraic behavior can be added on top to better reflect the zрқ‘§z-dependence. If Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz is very large, then the signal-to-noise ratio gets worse, so zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L is smaller. In this case, the quasi-LF correlation is yet to show exponential decay and dominated by the leading-twist contributions, so one can use the algebraic decay form to do the extrapolation. To summarize, the hybrid scheme provides a proper renormalization of the quasi-LF correlations at all zрқ‘§z, which allows for a controlled calculation of the PDF for xвҲҲ[xmin,xmax]рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ҙminsubscriptрқ‘Ҙmaxxin[xrm min,xrm max] through LaMET expansion in momentum space. Therefore, we expect it to play a dominant role in the LaMET calculation of PDFs in the future.

The collinear divergence is regulated by the finite quark mass mрқ‘ҡm. If we follow the procedure incitebib.bib438 and take PzвҶ’вҲһвҶ’superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pztoinfty limit before UV regularizationcitebib.bib286, then О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(вҲһ,Ој)вҖӢ(2вҖӢSz)О”рқҗәрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyleDeltawidetildeG(infty,mu)(2Sz) =вҹЁPвҖӢS|ПөвҹӮiвҖӢjвҖӢFiвҖӢ0вҖӢAj|PвҖӢSвҹ©q|вҲҮвҶ’вӢ…AвҶ’=0absentevaluated-atsubscriptquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—perpendicular-tosuperscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–0superscriptрқҗҙрқ‘—рқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷрқ‘һвӢ…вҶ’вҲҮвҶ’рқҗҙ0displaystyle=left. PS|epsilonijperpFi0Aj|PSqrightarrowvertvecnablacdotvecA=0 =ОұsвҖӢCF4вҖӢПҖвҖӢ(3вҖӢlnвҒЎОј2m2+7)вҖӢ(2вҖӢSz),absentsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ subscriptрқҗ¶рқҗ№4рқңӢ3superscriptрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘ҡ272superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyle=fracalphasCF4pileft(3lnmu2over m2+7right)(2Sz),, which is exactly the same as the light-cone gluon helicity О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)О”рқҗәрқңҮDelta G(mu)citebib.bib239. Therefore, despite the difference in the UV divergence, the collinear divergences of О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu) and О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)О”рқҗәрқңҮDelta G(mu) are exactly the same, which allows for a perturbative matching between them. The complete factorization formula that relates О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu) to О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G and О”вҖӢОЈО”ОЈDeltaSigma is О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyleDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu) =ZgвҖӢgвҖӢ(Pz/Ој)вҖӢО”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)absentsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘”superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮО”рқҗәрқңҮdisplaystyle=Zgg(Pz/mu)Delta G(mu) +ZgвҖӢqвҖӢ(Pz/Ој)вҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+вҖҰ,subscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮО”ОЈрқңҮвҖҰdisplaystyleqquadqquad+Zgq(Pz/mu)DeltaSigma(mu)+. . . ,, where вҖҰвҖҰ. . . are power corrections suppressed by 1/Pz1superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§1/Pz, and the matching coefficients ZgвҖӢgsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘”Zgg and ZgвҖӢgsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘”Zgg have been calculated for the Coulomb gauge at one-loopcitebib.bib287. Besides, one can also calculate the gluon helicity PDF О”вҖӢgвҖӢ(x)О”рқ‘”рқ‘ҘDelta g(x) according to the factorization formula in Sec. , and then integrate it over xрқ‘Ҙx to obtain О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G. At leading-twist, apart from the unpolarized and helicity PDFs that we have discussed before, there is also the transversity PDF defined ascitebib.bib249, bib.bib250 h1вҖӢ(x)=12вҖӢP+вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢО»вҖӢxвҖӢвҹЁPвҖӢSвҹӮ|ПҲВҜвҖӢвҖӢОі+вҖӢОівҹӮвҖӢОі5вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹӮвҹ©. subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙ12superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶрқ‘Ҙquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toВҜрқң“0superscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫperpendicular-tosubscriptрқӣҫ5рқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toh1(x)!=!frac12P+!intfracdlambda2pie-ilambda x PSperp|overlinepsigamma+gammaperpgamma5psi(lambda n)|PSperp,. The h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) simply counts the number of transversely polarized quarks carrying the momentum fraction xрқ‘Ҙx in a transversely polarized proton. The first moment of this distribution corresponds to the so-called tensor charge ОҙвҖӢqрқӣҝрқ‘һdelta q, which is the matrix element of a chiral-odd operator. h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) can be accessed through the transverse-transverse spin asymmetry in Drell-Yan processescitebib.bib400, bib.bib249, bib.bib250 or the Collins single-spin asymmetry in SIDIS where the transversity TMDPDF couples to a chiral-odd TMD fragmentation functioncitebib.bib136. At present, experimental results on the transversity PDF are very limitedcitebib.bib45, bib.bib297, bib.bib330, bib.bib389, bib.bib101, especially for the sea quark contributionscitebib.bib113, so this is one scenario where lattice QCD calculation can make an important difference. The LaMET calculation of h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) is straightforward as the nonlocal operator has the same renormalization as the unpolarized case, and its one-loop matching has been calculated in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS and RI/MOM schemes at one-loop ordercitebib.bib21, bib.bib340. First lattice calculations of h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) have been done incitebib.bib114, bib.bib21, bib.bib340, which will be discussed with more details in Sec. .
content_sections.IV Generalized Collinear Parton Observab: In the previous section, we have extensively discussed the leading-twist collinear PDFs that characterize the 1D structure of the proton in longitudinal momentum space. There exist various other parton observables that provide complementary information. In this section, we focus on observables defined by collinear parton correlators, in the sense that only the collinear quark and gluon mode contribute, corresponding to the so-called collinear expansion in QCD factorizationscitebib.bib419, bib.bib132. We call them вҖңgeneralized collinear parton observablesвҖқ (GCPOs), and discuss their calculations through LaMET framework. For observables defined by parton correlators involving transverse separations, in particular, the TMDPDFs, Wigner functions, and LFWFs, we will consider them in the following sections. One of the important GCPOs is the GPDs introduced incitebib.bib365, and rediscoveredcitebib.bib258 from their connection to the spin structure of the proton. They describe the correlation between the transverse position and longitudinal momentum of partons inside the proton, and thus provide important information for 3D imaging of the proton. A proton spin sum rule was derived in terms of the moments of the GPDs, which has stimulated considerable general interest in the GPDs. It was also found that in the so-called zero skewness limit or when the longitudinal momentum transfer vanishes, the GPD has a probability interpretation in the impact parameter spacecitebib.bib99. In general case, it is related to the quantum phase-space distributions or Wigner functionscitebib.bib260, bib.bib54. Experimentally, the GPDs can be measured through hard exclusive processes such as deeply virtual Compton scattering (DVCS) or meson production (DVMP) that were first proposed incitebib.bib258, bib.bib257. Much effort has been devoted to measuring such processes at completed and ongoing experiments, including HERA, COMPASS and JLab. For a more comprehensive discussion on the GPDs, we refer the readers to the review articlescitebib.bib259, bib.bib253, bib.bib167, bib.bib52. Despite that the GPDs have more complicated kinematic dependence and relation to experimental observables, various fitting methods have been proposed in the literature to fit available DVCS and DVMP datacitebib.bib310, bib.bib198. In parallel, one can also extract certain information on the GPDs from lattice calculations of their momentscitebib.bib223, bib.bib211, bib.bib15, which, however, is again very limited due to the same difficulties existing in lattice calculations of the PDF moments. For JLab 12 GeV program and future EIC, it is critically important to have first-principle calculations of GPDs with much better understanding of the physical landscape in different kinematic variables. A simpler but closely related GCPO is the parton distribution amplitudes (DAs), which are collinear matrix elements of light-cone operators between a hadron state and the QCD vacuum, representing the probability amplitude of finding a given Fock state in the hadron. They can be probed in certain exclusive processescitebib.bib91, and are crucial inputs for processes relevant to measuring fundamental parameters of the Standard Model and probing new physics. There exists a vast amount of literature on this subject, particularly about the pion DA. For a review see e. g. citebib.bib92, bib.bib219, bib.bib77. Another type of GCPO is the higher-twist parton distributions. They are defined by multi-parton correlation functions, and quantify the proton structure in terms of longitudinal momentum correlationscitebib.bib252, bib.bib188, bib.bib250. Although physically interesting, they are hard to separate theoretically due to mixing with the leading-twist onescitebib.bib370, bib.bib256, and difficult to extract experimentally because they are power-suppressedcitebib.bib255. Higher-twist effects can become important in kinematic regions where the suppression is relaxed. Moreover, some twist-three distributions, gTsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘ҮgT and hLsubscriptв„ҺрқҗҝhL, are different; they have no leading-twist to mix with and are dominant in spin-related observablescitebib.bib250. Twist-three GPDs are also relevant for studying parton OAM in the protoncitebib.bib232, bib.bib281, bib.bib152 and can be accessed through DVCS processcitebib.bib383, bib.bib301. In principle, all the GCPOs discussed above can be computed within LaMET. In addition, an accurate LaMET expansion for the leading-twist PDFs requires calculations of quasi higher-twist matrix elements. In the following, we begin with the flavor non-singlet quark GPDs and hadronic DAs for which the computational procedure has been well established, and then give some generic discussions on higher-twist distributions, followed by the discussion on power-suppressed contributions required to extract the leading-twist quark PDFs, which have been investigated using different approaches though not yet implemented in numerical computations.

The operators defining the GPDs are the same as those defining the PDFs. Thus, the LaMET calculation of PDFs can be rather straightforwardly generalized to the GPDs by taking into account the non-forward kinematicscitebib.bib342. To illustrate how it works, let us take the nonsinglet unpolarized quark GPDs in the nucleon as an example. The unpolarized quark GPDs are defined through the following matrix elementcitebib.bib253 Fрқҗ№displaystyle F =12вҖӢPВҜ+вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢО»вҖӢвҹЁPвҖІвҖӢSвҖІ|OОі+вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹ©absent12superscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶquantum-operator-productsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІsuperscriptрқ‘ҶвҖІsubscriptрқ‘ӮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷdisplaystyle=frac12barP+intfracdlambda2pie-ixlambda PprimeSprime|Ogamma+(lambda n)|PS =12вҖӢPВҜ+вҖӢuВҜвҖӢ(PвҖІвҖӢSвҖІ)вҖӢ[HвҖӢОі++EвҖӢiвҖӢПғ+ОјвҖӢО”Ој2вҖӢM]вҖӢuвҖӢ(PвҖӢS),absent12superscriptВҜрқ‘ғВҜрқ‘ўsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІsuperscriptрқ‘ҶвҖІdelimited-рқҗ»superscriptрқӣҫрқҗёрқ‘–superscriptрқңҺрқңҮsubscriptО”рқңҮ2рқ‘Җрқ‘ўрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷdisplaystyle=frac12barP+baru(PprimeSprime)bigg[Hgamma++Efracisigma+muDeltamu2Mbigg]u(PS),, where we have suppressed the arguments (x,Оҫ,t,Ој)рқ‘Ҙрқңүрқ‘ЎрқңҮ(x,xi,t,mu) of Fрқҗ№F, Hрқҗ»H and EрқҗёE for simplicity. The operator OОі+вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)=ПҲВҜвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn2)вҖӢОі+вҖӢWвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn2,вҲ’О»вҖӢn2)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(вҲ’О»вҖӢn2)subscriptрқ‘ӮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҶрқ‘ӣВҜрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2superscriptрқӣҫрқ‘ҠрқңҶрқ‘ӣ2рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2рқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2displaystyle Ogamma+(lambda n)=barpsi(fraclambda n2)gamma+W(fraclambda n2,-fraclambda n2)psi(-fraclambda n2) with nОј=1/2вҖӢ(1/PВҜ+,0,0,вҲ’1/PВҜ+)superscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮ121superscriptВҜрқ‘ғ001superscriptВҜрқ‘ғnmu=1/sqrt2(1/barP+,0,0,-1/barP+) is the same operator used to define the unpolarized quark PDF, Mрқ‘ҖM is the nucleon mass. The momentum fraction xвҲҲ[вҲ’1,1]рқ‘Ҙ11xin[-1,1], and О”вүЎPвҖІвҲ’P,tвүЎО”2,ОҫвүЎвҲ’PвҖІвҒЈ+вҲ’P+PвҖІвҒЈ++P+=вҲ’О”+2вҖӢPВҜ+,formulae-sequenceО”superscriptрқ‘ғвҖІрқ‘ғformulae-sequenceрқ‘ЎsuperscriptО”2рқңүsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІsuperscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІsuperscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptО”2superscriptВҜрқ‘ғdisplaystyleDeltaequiv Pprime-P,;;tequivDelta2,;;xiequiv-fracPprime+-P+Pprime++P+=-fracDelta+2barP+,, where without loss of generality we have chosen a Lorentz frame in which the average momentum takes the following form PВҜОјвүЎPвҖІвҒЈОј+PОј2=(PВҜ0,0,0,PВҜz). superscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІрқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқңҮ2superscriptВҜрқ‘ғ000superscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§displaystylebarPmuequivfracPprimemu+Pmu2=(barP0,0,0,barPz),. The skewness parameter ОҫвҲҲ[вҲ’1,1]рқңү11xiin[-1,1] since P+,PвҖІвҒЈ+вүҘ0superscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІ0P+,Pprime+geq 0. Besides, there exists another kinematic constraint on Оҫрқңүxi, which follows from О”вҶ’вҹӮ2вүҘ0subscriptsuperscriptвҶ’О”2perpendicular-to0vecDelta2perpgeq 0, ОҫвүӨОҫmaxвҖӢ(t)=вҲ’tвҲ’t+4вҖӢM2. рқңүsubscriptрқңүmaxрқ‘Ўрқ‘Ўрқ‘Ў4superscriptрқ‘Җ2displaystylexileqxirm max(t)=sqrtfrac-t-t+4M2,. In the following, we will also assume Оҫ>0рқңү0xi>0 without loss of generality. With these kinematic constraints, the GPDs can be divided into several kinematic regions that have different physical interpretations. As shown in Fig. , in the region Оҫ<x<1вҖӢ(вҲ’1<x<вҲ’Оҫ)рқңүрқ‘Ҙ11рқ‘Ҙрқңүxi<x<1,(-1<x<-xi) the distribution describes the emission and reabsorption of a quark (antiquark), while in the region вҲ’Оҫ<x<Оҫрқңүрқ‘Ҙрқңү-xi<x<xi it represents the creation of a quark and antiquark pair. The first region is similar to that present in usual PDFs and referred to as the DGLAP region, whereas the second is similar to that in a meson DA, which will be discussed later in this section, and referred to as the Efremov-Radyushkin-Brodsky-Lepage (ERBL) region. The easiest way to see this is in light-cone quantization and light-cone gauge where the matrix element defining the GPDs can be rewritten in terms of parton creation and annihilation operators, for details see e. g. citebib.bib253. The quark GPDs defined above have a number of remarkable properties, see, e. g. ,citebib.bib259, bib.bib253, bib.bib167, bib.bib52 , which either hold or have similar counterparts for the quark quasi-GPDs to be defined below. Apart from their physical significance, these properties also serve as useful checks on calculations related to GPDs. According to LaMET, the unpolarized quark GPDs defined above can be determined by calculating the following quasi-GPDs Fрқҗ№displaystyletildeF =12вҖӢPВҜ0вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢyвҖӢО»вҖӢвҹЁPвҖІвҖӢSвҖІ|OОі0вҖӢ(z)|PвҖӢSвҹ©absent12superscriptВҜрқ‘ғ0рқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҰрқңҶquantum-operator-productsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІsuperscriptрқ‘ҶвҖІsubscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫ0рқ‘§рқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷdisplaystyle=frac12barP0intfracdlambda2pieiylambda PprimeSprime|Ogamma0(z)|PS =12вҖӢPВҜ0вҖӢuВҜвҖӢ(PвҖІвҖӢSвҖІ)вҖӢHвҖӢОі0+EвҖӢiвҖӢПғ0вҖӢОјвҖӢО”Ој2вҖӢMвҖӢuвҖӢ(PвҖӢS),absent12superscriptВҜрқ‘ғ0ВҜрқ‘ўsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІsuperscriptрқ‘ҶвҖІрқҗ»superscriptрқӣҫ0рқҗёрқ‘–superscriptрқңҺ0рқңҮsubscriptО”рқңҮ2рқ‘Җрқ‘ўрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷdisplaystyle=frac12barP0baru(PprimeSprime)biggtildeHgamma0+tildeEfracisigma0muDeltamu2Mbiggu(PS),, where we have again suppressed the arguments (y,Оҫ,t,PВҜz,Ој)рқ‘Ұрқңүрқ‘ЎsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮ(y,tildexi,t,barPz,mu) of Fрқҗ№tildeF, Hрқҗ»tildeH, and EрқҗёtildeE. The operator OОі0вҖӢ(z)=ПҲВҜвҖӢ(z2)вҖӢОі0вҖӢWвҖӢ(z2,вҲ’z2)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(вҲ’z2)subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫ0рқ‘§ВҜрқң“рқ‘§2superscriptрқӣҫ0рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§2рқ‘§2рқң“рқ‘§2Ogamma0(z)=barpsi(fracz2)gamma0W(fracz2,-fracz2)psi(-fracz2) is the same operator defining the unpolarized quark quasi-PDF, and О»=zвҖӢPВҜzрқңҶрқ‘§superscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§lambda=zbarPz. As in the quasi-PDF case, the momentum fraction yрқ‘Ұy extends from вҲ’вҲһ-infty to вҲһinfty. The skewness parameter for the quasi-GPD Оҫ=вҲ’PвҖІвҒЈzвҲ’PzPвҖІвҒЈz+Pz=вҲ’О”z2вҖӢPВҜz=Оҫ+рқ’ӘвҖӢ(M2(PВҜz)2,t(PВҜz)2)рқңүsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘ғвҖІрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptО”рқ‘§2superscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңүрқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§2рқ‘ЎsuperscriptsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§2displaystyletildexi=-fracPprime z-PzPprime z+Pz=-fracDeltaz2barPz=xi+Oleft(M2over(barPz)2,tover(barPz)2right) differs from the light-cone skewness Оҫрқңүxi by power suppressed corrections. Moreover, the constraint from О”вҶ’вҹӮ2вүҘ0superscriptsubscriptвҶ’О”perpendicular-to20vecDeltaperp2geq 0 becomescitebib.bib277 ОҫвүӨ12вҖӢPВҜzвҖӢвҲ’tвҖӢ[(PВҜz)2+M2вҲ’t/4]M2вҲ’t/4,рқңү12superscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқ‘Ўdelimited-superscriptsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§2superscriptрқ‘Җ2рқ‘Ў4superscriptрқ‘Җ2рқ‘Ў4displaystyletildexileqfrac12barPzsqrtfrac-tleft[(barPz)2+M2-t/4right]M2-t/4, which differs from the constraint in Eq. by corrections of рқ’ӘвҖӢ(M2/(PВҜz)2,t/(PВҜz)2)рқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§2рқ‘ЎsuperscriptsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§2O(M2/(barPz)2,t/(barPz)2). We can replace Оҫрқңүtildexi with Оҫрқңүxi and attribute the difference to generic power suppressed contributions. The quasi-GPDs defined above can be renormalized by observing that their UV divergence depends only on the operators defining them, but not on the external states. Since OОі0вҖӢ(z)subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫ0рқ‘§Ogamma0(z) is multiplicatively renormalized, we can choose the same renormalization factor as that for the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib420, bib.bib343 to renormalize the quasi-GPD. After renormalization, the quasi-GPD can then be matched to the usual GPD through a factorization formula. The factorization of quasi-GPDs was first proposed and verified at one-loop order incitebib.bib277, bib.bib445, where a transverse momentum cutoff and a quark mass were used as the UV and IR regulator, respectively. Later on, a detailed derivation based on OPE was given incitebib.bib341. In contrast with the OPE for the quasi-PDF, a crucial difference here is that the total derivative of operators can come into play, as it simply gives momentum transfer factors when sandwiched between non-forward external states, and therefore is non-vanishing. In other words, the local twist-two operators as those in Eq. will mix under renormalization with operators with total derivatives. The RGE that governs the mixing readscitebib.bib79, Ој2вҖӢddвҖӢОј2вҖӢOОј0вҖӢОј1вҖӢвҖҰвҖӢОјnвҖӢ(Ој)=вҲ‘m=0[n/2]О“nвҖӢmsuperscriptрқңҮ2рқ‘‘рқ‘‘superscriptрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘ӮsubscriptрқңҮ0subscriptрқңҮ1вҖҰsubscriptрқңҮрқ‘ӣрқңҮsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡ0delimited-рқ‘ӣ2subscriptО“рқ‘ӣрқ‘ҡdisplaystylemu2dover dmu2Omu0mu1ldotsmun(mu)=summ=0[n/2]Gammanm Г—[iвҖӢвҲӮ(Ој1вӢҜвҖӢiвҖӢвҲӮОј2вҖӢmПҲВҜвҖӢОіОј0вҖӢiвҖӢDвҶ”Ој2вҖӢm+1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢiвҖӢDвҶ”Ојn)вҖӢПҲвҲ’trace],displaystyletimesleft[ipartial(mu1cdots ipartialmu2mbarpsigammamu0ioverleftrightarrowDmu2m+1cdots ioverleftrightarrowDmun)psi-rm traceright],, where О“nвҖӢmsubscriptО“рқ‘ӣрқ‘ҡGammanm is the anomalous dimension of the associated operators, DвҶ”=(DвҶ’вҲ’DвҶҗ)/2вҶ”рқҗ·вҶ’рқҗ·вҶҗрқҗ·2overleftrightarrowD=(overrightarrowD-overleftarrowD)/2 with DвҶ’вҖӢ(DвҶҗ)вҶ’рқҗ·вҶҗрқҗ·overrightarrowD(overleftarrowD) denoting the covariant derivative acting to the right (left). For the helicity and transversity quark quasi-GPDs, the factorization formula has the same form as Eq. citebib.bib341. The matching coefficient can be obtained by replacing the hadron states in Eqs. and with the quark states carrying momentum p+О”/2рқ‘қО”2p+Delta/2 and pвҲ’О”/2рқ‘қО”2p-Delta/2 with pОј=(p0,0,0,pz)superscriptрқ‘қрқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘қ000superscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§pmu=(p0,0,0,pz), and calculating the quark matrix element in perturbation theory. The explicit expression for the рқ’ӘвҖӢ(Оұs)рқ’Әsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ O(alphas) matching coefficients can be found incitebib.bib341. An important feature of the result is: The quasi-GPDs do not vanish in all yрқ‘Ұy range, but the collinear singularities only show up in DGLAP and ERBL regions at one-loop. They are exactly the same as those in light-cone GPDs, and thus cancel in the matching coefficient. Moreover, one can derive momentum RGEs for the quasi-GPDs, which are turned into RGE for the scale dependence of the GPDs by the matching procedure. To conclude this subsection, let us make some remarks on the EFT formula for the quark GPD above. First, at zero skewness Оҫ=0рқңү0xi=0, we have FвҖӢ(x,0,t,Ој)рқҗ№рқ‘Ҙ0рқ‘ЎрқңҮdisplaystyle F(x,0,t,mu) =вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢy|y|вҖӢCвҖӢ(xy,0,ОјyвҖӢPz)вҖӢFвҖӢ(y,0,t,Pz,Ој)absentsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұрқҗ¶рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұ0рқңҮрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқҗ№рқ‘Ұ0рқ‘Ўsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyle=!!int-inftyinfty!dyover|y|C!left(xover y,0,muover yPzright)!tildeF(y,0,t,Pz,mu) +вҖҰ,вҖҰdisplaystyleqquad+ldots,, where the matching kernel CвҖӢ(x/y,0,Ој/(yвҖӢPz))рқҗ¶рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұ0рқңҮрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§C(x/y,0,mu/(yPz)) is exactly the same as the matching coefficient for the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib247, even when tвү 0рқ‘Ў0tneq 0. This can be understood as follows: At zero skewness, both the longitudinal momentum transfer and the energy transfer vanish, the momentum transfer is purely transverse and thus is not affected by Lorentz boost along the longitudinal zрқ‘§z direction. As a result, no extra matching related to tрқ‘Ўt is required in the large Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz limit, and the matching remains the same as in the quasi-PDF case. If we take the forward limit О”вҶ’0вҶ’О”0Deltato 0, then Eq. reduces exactly to the EFT expansion formula for the PDFcitebib.bib277, bib.bib247. Second, in the limit ОҫвҶ’1вҶ’рқңү1xito 1 and tвҶ’0вҶ’рқ‘Ў0tto 0, the quasi-GPD reduces to the quasi-DA that will be discussed in the next subsection, and the corresponding matching kernel also reduces to that for the quasi-DA.

Within LaMET, the DAs of protons as well as other hadrons can also be extracted from lattice simulations of appropriately chosen quasi-DAs. In this subsection, we show how this can be done in practice. For illustration, we take the leading-twist pion DA as an example. The application to other hadronscitebib.bib454, bib.bib434 is analogous. The leading-twist DA of the pion is the simplest and most extensively studied hadronic DA. It represents the probability amplitude of finding the valence qвҖӢqВҜрқ‘һВҜрқ‘һqbarq Fock state in the pion with the quark carrying a fraction xрқ‘Ҙx of the total pion momentum, and is defined as П•ПҖвҖӢ(x)subscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘Ҙdisplaystylephipi(x) =1iвҖӢfПҖвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢP+вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢ(xвҲ’12)вҖӢО»вҖӢвҹЁ0|OОі+вҖӢОі5вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|ПҖвҖӢ(P)вҹ©,absent1рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙ12рқңҶquantum-operator-product0subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқңӢрқ‘ғdisplaystyle=frac1ifpiintfracdlambda2pi P+e-i(x-frac12)lambda 0|Ogamma+gamma5(lambda n)|pi(P),, with normalization вҲ«01рқ‘‘xвҖӢП•ПҖвҖӢ(x)=1superscriptsubscript01differential-dрқ‘Ҙsubscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘Ҙ1int01dx,phipi(x)=1. Here fПҖsubscriptрқ‘“рқңӢfpi denotes the decay constant, and OОі+вҖӢОі5вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5рқңҶрқ‘ӣOgamma+gamma5(lambda n) has the same structure as that used in Eq. with Оі+superscriptрқӣҫgamma+ replaced by Оі+вҖӢОі5superscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5gamma+gamma5. The pion DA can be constrained from experimental measurements of, e. g. , ОіОівҲ—вҶ’ПҖ0gammagamma*rightarrowpi0 from BaBar and Bellecitebib.bib28, bib.bib427, and then used as an input to test QCD in other measurements such as the pion form factorcitebib.bib186, bib.bib197. In the asymptotic limit, it is well known that the pion DA takes the form 6вҖӢxвҖӢ(1вҲ’x)6рқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘Ҙ6x(1-x)citebib.bib186, bib.bib319. However, how it behaves at lower scales remains under debate (see e. g. citebib.bib123). Calculating the pion DA with controllable systematics in LaMET will be able to shed new lights on its shape and thus on our understanding of pion structure. Following the same strategy as before, we can access the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of the pion DA by studying the following quasi-DAcitebib.bib277, bib.bib450 П•ПҖвҖӢ(y,Pz)subscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§displaystyletildephipi(y,Pz) =1iвҖӢfПҖвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢPzвҖӢeiвҖӢ(yвҲ’12)вҖӢО»вҖӢвҹЁ0|OОіzвҖӢОі5вҖӢ(z)|ПҖвҖӢ(P)вҹ©,absent1рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ұ12рқңҶquantum-operator-product0subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫ5рқ‘§рқңӢрқ‘ғdisplaystyle=frac1ifpiintfracdlambda2pi Pzei(y-frac12)lambda 0|Ogammazgamma5(z)|pi(P),, The longitudinally and transversely polarized vector meson quasi-DAs can be defined analogously by replacing ОіzвҖӢОі5superscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫ5gammazgamma5 in the above equation with Оі0superscriptрқӣҫ0gamma0, ОіzвҖӢОівҹӮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫperpendicular-togammazgammaperp, respectivelycitebib.bib342. In the literature, the Wilson-line mass-subtraction scheme was used in the first LaMET calculations of the meson DAscitebib.bib450, bib.bib454, and the RI/MOM scheme has been adopted in more recent workscitebib.bib456. Its one-loop results have been calculated in both MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS and RI/MOM schemescitebib.bib342, which agrees with matching coefficient for the quasi-GPDscitebib.bib277, bib.bib445, bib.bib341 in Eq. with the replacement of ОҫвҶ’1/(2вҖӢyвҲ’1)вҶ’рқңү12рқ‘Ұ1xito 1/(2y-1), x/ОҫвҶ’2вҖӢxвҲ’1вҶ’рқ‘Ҙрқңү2рқ‘Ҙ1x/xito 2x-1, and the external momentum pzsuperscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§pz to pz/2superscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§2pz/2. Here the matching coefficient C2subscriptрқҗ¶2C2 depends on the choice of the currents. Its explicit expression can be found incitebib.bib34. The above factorization is controlled by рқ’ӘвҖӢ(z2вҖӢОӣQCD2)рқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2O(z2Lambdarm QCD2), with power corrections denoted by вҖңвӢҜвӢҜcdotsвҖқ. Incitebib.bib34, a combined analysis of several current-current correlations has been performed where twist-four contributions were also included using the model estimate incitebib.bib82, bib.bib43. The leading-twist pion DA was then extracted from a global fit to the data, and the second moment of the pion DA has been fitted with controlled precision, both of which favor a considerably broader shape than the asymptotic DA at a scale of 222 GeV. A large pion momentum is required to access information at large О»рқңҶlambda so that we can extract wider range of xрқ‘Ҙx or higher moments of the pion DAcitebib.bib37.

Higher-twist distributions are quantities of great interest because they describe the coherent quark-gluon correlations in the proton. In contrast with the leading-twist distributions, our understanding of the higher-twist ones is rather poor. On one hand, they often depend on more than one parton momentum fractions; on the other hand, there is no physical intuition about what they may look like, in particular, about how they behave asymptotically at small and large xрқ‘Ҙxcitebib.bib80. There have been studies on the higher-twist distributions in the context of their connection to the DIS structure function, the transverse single-spin asymmetries in various hadron productions, GPDs related to quark and gluon OAM, parton DAs, etc. LaMET will be able to shed new lights by providing a possibility to access them from the lattice. Higher-twist contributions also appear in LaMET expansion, where the suppression is provided by powers of the hadron momentum squared. In all factorizations presented in previous sections, only the leading-twist terms that capture the logarithmic dependence on hadron momentum are taken into account. The higher-twist contributions have been assumed to be small. If the hadron momentum is not sufficiently large compared and/or one is close to the endpoint region (xвҶ’0вҶ’рқ‘Ҙ0xto 0 and xвҶ’1вҶ’рқ‘Ҙ1xto 1), the higher-twist contributions can become non-negligible, whose structure and impact require understanding.Beyond leading-twist, there exist three simplest twist-three quark distributions eвҖӢ(x)рқ‘’рқ‘Ҙe(x), gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘgT(x) and hLвҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һрқҗҝрқ‘ҘhL(x) related to the unpolarized, transversely and longitudinally polarized protoncitebib.bib250, eвҖӢ(x)рқ‘’рқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle e(x) =12вҖӢMвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢО»absent12рқ‘Җрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶdisplaystyle=frac12Mintfracdlambda2pieixlambda Г—вҹЁPS|ПҲ+вҖ Оі0ПҲвҲ’(О»n)|PSвҹ©+h. c. ,displaystylequadtimes PS|psidagger+gamma0psi-(lambda n)|PS+rm h. c. ,, gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle gT(x) =12вҖӢMвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢО»absent12рқ‘Җрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶdisplaystyle=frac12Mintfracdlambda2pieixlambda Г—вҹЁPSвҹӮ|ПҲ+вҖ Оі0ОівҹӮОі5ПҲвҲ’(О»n)|PSвҹӮвҹ©+h. c. ,displaystylequadtimes PSperp|psidagger+gamma0gammaperpgamma5psi-(lambda n)|PSperp+rm h. c. ,, hLвҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һрқҗҝрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle hL(x) =12вҖӢMвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢО»absent12рқ‘Җрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶdisplaystyle=frac12Mintfracdlambda2pieixlambda Г—вҹЁPSz|ПҲ+вҖ Оі0Оі5ПҲвҲ’(О»n)|PSzвҹ©+h. c. ,displaystylequadtimes PSz|psidagger+gamma0gamma5psi-(lambda n)|PSz+rm h. c. ,, where we have again employed the decomposition of quark fields ПҲ=ПҲ++ПҲвҲ’рқң“subscriptрқң“subscriptрқң“psi=psi++psi- in Sec. and the light-cone gauge A+=0superscriptрқҗҙ0A+=0, and вҖңh. c. вҖқ stands for Hermitian conjugate. The twist-three distributions can contribute as leading effects in certain experimental observables. For example, gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘgT(x) and hLвҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һрқҗҝрқ‘ҘhL(x) can be measured as the leading effects in the longitudinal-transverse spin asymmetry in polarized Drell-Yan process. Since ПҲвҲ’subscriptрқң“psi- is a non-dynamical component depending on ПҲ+subscriptрқң“psi+, all the above distributions can be shown to be related to more complicated quark-gluon correlation functionscitebib.bib261, bib.bib39. A complete set of such correlation functions has been given incitebib.bib388, bib.bib254, bib.bib266, bib.bib298, where the quark-gluon correlations in a transversely-polarized proton take the following form TqвҖӢ(x1,x2)=1(P+)2вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»вҖӢdвҖӢО¶(2вҖӢПҖ)2вҖӢeiвҖӢО»вҖӢx1+iвҖӢО¶вҖӢ(x2вҲ’x1)subscriptрқ‘Үрқ‘һsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1subscriptрқ‘Ҙ21superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғ2рқ‘‘рқңҶрқ‘‘рқңҒsuperscript2рқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘–рқңҒsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ2subscriptрқ‘Ҙ1displaystyle Tq(x1,x2)=frac1(P+)2intfracdlambda dzeta(2pi)2eilambda x1+izeta(x2-x1) Г—вҹЁPвҖӢSвҹӮ|ПҲВҜвҖӢвҖӢОі+вҖӢПө+вҲ’SвҹӮвҖӢiвҖӢgвҖӢF+iвҖӢ(О¶вҖӢn)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹӮвҹ©,absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toВҜрқң“0superscriptрқӣҫsuperscriptitalic-Пөabsentsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toрқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–рқңҒрқ‘ӣрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-todisplaystylehskip 20.00003pttimes PSperp|barpsigamma+epsilon+-SperpigF+i(zeta n)psi(lambda n)|PSperp, TО”вҖӢqвҖӢ(x1,x2)=1(P+)2вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»вҖӢdвҖӢО¶(2вҖӢПҖ)2вҖӢeiвҖӢО»вҖӢx1+iвҖӢО¶вҖӢ(x2вҲ’x1)subscriptрқ‘ҮО”рқ‘һsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1subscriptрқ‘Ҙ21superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғ2рқ‘‘рқңҶрқ‘‘рқңҒsuperscript2рқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘–рқңҒsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ2subscriptрқ‘Ҙ1displaystyle TDelta q(x1,x2)=frac1(P+)2intfracdlambda dzeta(2pi)2eilambda x1+izeta(x2-x1) Г—вҹЁPвҖӢSвҹӮ|ПҲВҜвҖӢвҖӢiвҖӢОі+вҖӢОі5вҖӢSвҹӮiвҖӢgвҖӢF+iвҖӢ(О¶вҖӢn)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹӮвҹ©. absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toВҜрқң“0рқ‘–superscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘–perpendicular-toрқ‘”superscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–рқңҒрқ‘ӣрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-todisplaystylehskip 20.00003pttimes PSperp|barpsiigamma+gamma5SiperpgF+i(zeta n)psi(lambda n)|PSperp,. There are also ones in an unpolarized and longitudinally-polarized proton. Generalizing to off-forward kinematics, the resulting twist-three GPDs are also related to quark and gluon OAM contribution to the proton spincitebib.bib232, bib.bib281. One can also define twist-four distributions in a similar way as in Eq. by using ПҲвҲ’subscriptрқң“psi- for both quark fields. More general twist-four distributions will involve three light-cone variables, which will contribute to, e. g. , 1/Q21superscriptрқ‘„21/Q2 term in DIScitebib.bib252, bib.bib188, bib.bib250, bib.bib255. In principle, all the above higher-twist distributions, as well as others that have not been listed here, can be computed using the LaMET approach by choosing appropriate quasi-LF correlations. For example, the first exploratory lattice calculation of gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘgT(x) has been done incitebib.bib62, which will be discussed in Sec. . However, extra complications are expected due to their complex structure. For instance, the lightcone zero modes that do not enter in dealing with leading-twist distributions come into play here. Recently, one of the authors has shown how to study the properties of these zero modes from lattice simulations in LaMETcitebib.bib270. In addition, the higher-twist distributions will have a more complex mixing patterncitebib.bib261, bib.bib39. Thus, their matching from the corresponding quasi distributions must take into account such mixings, making them more challenging than calculating the twist-two PDFs. One-loop studies of the matching for twist-three disributions have been carried out incitebib.bib63, bib.bib64.Let us turn to the power suppressed higher-twist contributions appearing in the extraction of leading-twist quark PDFs using LaMET. Such contributions have two distinct origins. To understand them, let us recall the OPE for the quasi-LF correlation in Eq. . For simplicity, we ignore the renormalization here. Recovering the leading-twist quark PDF requires removing the contributions of both trace terms in that equation. The trace terms on the r. h. s. of Eq. , which lead to contributions suppressed by powers of M2/(Pz)2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2M2/(Pz)2, are known as kinematic power contributions or target mass corrections. In DIS, they can be accounted for by changing the scaling variable xрқ‘Ҙx to the Nachtmann variablecitebib.bib376. In the case of LaMET, it behaves slightly differently, as shown in the following. The second type of power corrections come from the trace terms in the operators on the r. h. s. of Eq. , and in general leads to contributions of рқ’ӘвҖӢ(ОӣQCD2/(Pz)2)рқ’ӘsubscriptsuperscriptОӣ2QCDsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2O(Lambda2rmQCD/(Pz)2) . These are genuine higher-twist contributions that involve multi-parton correlations, sometimes also known as dynamical higher-twist contributions. The target mass corrections have been computed to all orders in M2/(Pz)2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2M2/(Pz)2 for the quark quasi-PDFs incitebib.bib114, bib.bib394. The genuine higher-twist contributions have been investigated using two different approachescitebib.bib114, bib.bib84. According tocitebib.bib114, the M2/(Pz)2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2M2/(Pz)2 corrections can be obtained from the ratio Kmsubscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҡdisplaystyle Km вүЎn(Ој1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢnОјm)вҖӢPОј1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢPОјmnОј1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢnОјmвҖӢPОј1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢPОјm=вҲ‘i=0imaxCmвҲ’iiвҖӢci,displaystyleequivfracn(mu1cdots nmum)Pmu1cdots Pmumnmu1cdots nmumPmu1cdots Pmum=sumi=0itextmaxCm-iici,, where imax=(mвҲ’ModвҖӢ[m,2])/2subscriptрқ‘–maxрқ‘ҡModрқ‘ҡ22itextmax=(m-textMod[m,2])/2, Cрқҗ¶C is the binomial function and c=вҲ’n2вҖӢM2/4вҖӢ(nвӢ…P)2=M2/4вҖӢ(Pz)2рқ‘җsuperscriptрқ‘ӣ2superscriptрқ‘Җ24superscriptвӢ…рқ‘ӣрқ‘ғ2superscriptрқ‘Җ24superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2c=-n2M2/4left(ncdot Pright)2=M2/4(Pz)2 with nОј=(0,0,0,вҲ’1)superscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮ0001nmu=(0,0,0,-1) and nвӢ…P=PzвӢ…рқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§ncdot P=Pz. Plugged into the tree-level OPE formula in Eq. , the above factors can then be converted to the following relation between unpolarized PDF and quasi-PDFcitebib.bib114 qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle q(x) =1+4вҖӢcвҲ‘n=0вҲһ(4вҖӢc)nf+2вҖӢn+1[(1+(вҲ’1)n)q(f+2вҖӢn+1вҖӢx2вҖӢ(4вҖӢc)n)displaystyle=sqrt1+4csumn=0inftyfrac(4c)nf+2n+1Big[(1+(-1)n)tildeqBig(fracf+2n+1x2(4c)nBig) +(1вҲ’(вҲ’1)n)q(вҲ’f+2вҖӢn+1вҖӢx2вҖӢ(4вҖӢc)n)],displaystyleqquad+(1-(-1)n)tildeqBig(frac-f+2n+1x2(4c)nBig)Big], where f+=1+4вҖӢc+1subscriptрқ‘“14рқ‘җ1f+=sqrt1+4c+1. It is worth noting that quark number conservation is preserved in the above result. The target mass corrections for the longitudinally and transversely polarized quasi-PDFs can be derived analogously. The trace part on the r. h. s. of Eq. is a genuine higher-twist effect. One may try to construct a non-local form of the higher-twist operators from OPE. The leading trace term, which is a twist-four effect, has been studied incitebib.bib114 (see alsocitebib.bib42) and shown to give rise to a twist-four PDF q4вҖӢ(x,Pz)=вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢО»8вҖӢПҖвҖӢPzвҖӢО“0вҖӢ(вҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢО»)вҖӢвҹЁP|OtrвҖӢ(z)|Pвҹ©,subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқңҶ8рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptО“0рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ӯtrрқ‘§рқ‘ғdisplaystyleqtext4(x,Pz)=int-inftyinfty!fracdlambda8pi Pz,Gamma0left(-ixlambdaright)left Pleft|Otexttr(z)right|Pright, with Otr(z)=вҲ«0zdz1ПҲВҜ[О“ОҪW(0,z1)DОҪW(z1,z)displaystyle Otexttr(z)=int0z!dz1,barpsiBig[GammanuWleft(0,z1right)DnuWleft(z1,zright) +вҲ«0z1dz2nвӢ…О“W(0,z2)DОҪW(z2,z1)DОҪW(z1,z)]ПҲ(zn),displaystyle+int0z1!!!dz2,ncdotGamma,Wleft(0,z2right)DnuWleft(z2,z1right)DnuWleft(z1,zright)Big]psi(zn),, where one has О“Ој=ОіОј,ОіОјвҖӢОі5,ОівҹӮвҖӢОіОјвҖӢОі5superscriptО“рқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқӣҫperpendicular-tosuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫ5Gammamu=gammamu,gammamugamma5,gammaperpgammamugamma5 for the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs, respectively. О“0subscriptО“0Gamma0 is the incomplete Gamma function О“0вҖӢ(вҲ’iвҖӢx)=вҲ«01dвҖӢttвҖӢeiвҖӢx/t. subscriptО“0рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptsubscript01рқ‘‘рқ‘Ўрқ‘Ўsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙрқ‘ЎGamma0left(-ixright)=int01fracdtteix/t,. The above twist-four contribution needs to be removed from the quasi-PDF to recover the leading-twist PDF. It also provides a possibility for practical computations on the lattice. However, as a multi-parton correlation involving more gauge links and covariant derivatives, its lattice computation is rather challenging and has not been carried out in any existing work yet. Another approach that has been used to estimate power corrections related to quark quasi-PDFs is the renormalon model (seecitebib.bib55 for a comprehensive review). It is based on the observation that the perturbative expansion of the matching coefficient for the quasi-PDF diverges factorially with the loop order, implying that it is only well defined up to a power accuracy. This is known as the renormalon ambiguity, which must be cancelled by terms in the higher-twist contributions. Incitebib.bib84, it was shown that the cancellation of renormalon ambiguity requires that the leading higher-twist or twist-four contribution takes the following form q4вҖӢ(y,Pz,Ој)=Ој2вҖӢвҲ«вҲ’11dвҖӢx|x|вҖӢDвҖӢ(yx)вҖӢqвҖӢ(x,Ој)+q4вҖІвҖӢ(y,Pz,Ој),subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮsuperscriptрқңҮ2superscriptsubscript11рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙрқҗ·рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙрқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘һвҖІ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyleq4(y,Pz,mu)=mu2int-11!fracdx|x|DBig(fracyxBig)q(x,mu)+qprime4(y,Pz,mu),, where the first term on the r. h. s. cancels the renormalon ambiguity from the leading-twist matching coefficient, and q4вҖІsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘һвҖІ4qprime4 depends on ОјрқңҮmu at most logarithmically. Since the first term is to merely cancel similar contributions in the matching coefficient, it does not contribute to any physical observable. The renormalon model of power corrections citebib.bib57, bib.bib170, bib.bib156, bib.bib157, bib.bib59, bib.bib83 is based on the assumption that, by replacing ОјрқңҮmu with a suitable nonperturbative scale, this contribution reflects the order and the functional form of actual power-suppressed contributions. This was known as вҖңultraviolet dominanceвҖқ incitebib.bib81, bib.bib55, bib.bib58. Under this assumption, we obtain the following estimate, q4вҖӢ(y,Pz,Ој)=ОәвҖӢОӣQCD2вҖӢвҲ«вҲ’11dвҖӢx|x|вҖӢDвҖӢ(yx)вҖӢqвҖӢ(x,Ој),subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮрқң…superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2superscriptsubscript11рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙрқҗ·рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙрқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮq4(y,Pz,mu)=kappaLambdarm QCD2int-11!fracdx|x|DBig(fracyxBig),q(x,mu),, where Оәрқң…kappa is a dimensionless coefficient of рқ’ӘвҖӢрқ’Ә1O that cannot be fixed within theory and remains a free parameter. A detailed analysiscitebib.bib84 showed that for the quasi-PDF we have q4вҖӢ(y,Pz)=ОәвҖӢОӣQCD2y2вҖӢ(1вҲ’y)вҖӢ(Pz)2subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқң…superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2superscriptрқ‘Ұ21рқ‘Ұsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2displaystyleq4(y,Pz)=frackappaLambdarm QCD2y2(1-y)(Pz)2 Г—(1вҲ’y)вҖӢ[вҲ«|y|1dвҖӢxxвҖӢ[x2(1вҲ’x)+вҲ’2вҖӢx2]вҖӢqвҖӢ(yx)+2вҖӢqвҖӢ(y)вҲ’|y|вҖӢqвҖІвҖӢ(y)],absent1рқ‘Ұdelimited-superscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ұ1рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙdelimited-superscriptрқ‘Ҙ2subscript1рқ‘Ҙ2superscriptрқ‘Ҙ2рқ‘һрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙ2рқ‘һрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘һвҖІрқ‘Ұdisplaystyletimes(1-y)!Big[!!int|y|1!!fracdxxBig[fracx2(1-x)+!-!2x2Big]qBig(fracyxBig)!+!2q(y)!-!|y|qprime(y)Big],, where the first term in the integral was reproduced in a recent analysis of the renormalon effects in the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib339. As one can see, the second row vanishes as qвҖӢ(y)рқ‘һрқ‘Ұq(y) when yвҶ’1вҶ’рқ‘Ұ1yto 1 if limyвҶ’1qвҖӢ(y)вҲј(1вҲ’y)asimilar-tosubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ұ1рқ‘һрқ‘Ұsuperscript1рқ‘Ұрқ‘Һlimyto 1q(y)sim(1-y)a with a>0рқ‘Һ0a>0. This gives an estimate of the twist-four contribution on the r. h. s. of Eq. , which implies that the higher-twist contributions are enhanced as 1/y21superscriptрқ‘Ұ21/y2 and 1/(1вҲ’y)11рқ‘Ұ1/(1-y) for yвҲј0similar-toрқ‘Ұ0ysim 0 and yвҲј1similar-toрқ‘Ұ1ysim 1, respectively. Similar analysis can also be done for the pseudo-PDF. The above result can be used as a way to model the twist-four contribution with Оәрқң…kappa as the only parameter.

Over the past three decades, much experimental and theoretical work has been done on the origin and structure of proton spin, which has been covered in depth in the review articles citebib.bib201, bib.bib47, bib.bib10, bib.bib316, bib.bib269, bib.bib166, bib.bib284. In addition to the spin-dependent PDFs and TMDs, the GCPOsвҖ”in particular the GPDsвҖ”also play an important role in understanding the spin structure of the proton. Since GPDs describe the correlation between the transverse position and longitudinal momentum of quarks and gluons inside the proton, they offer a unique channel to study the orbital angular momentum (OAM) from experiments. There are two widely known definitions of OAM in literature. One is the kinetic OAM in the gauge-invariant and frame-independent sum rule for the proton spincitebib.bib258, bib.bib257, which is related to the first moment of twist-two GPDs and can be calculated from the form factors of the symmetric QCD energy-momentum tensor. A review of the lattice calculations of kinetic OAM can be found incitebib.bib284. The other definition, which has a clear partonic interpretation in comparison to the kinetic OAM, is the canonical OAM in the naive partonic sum rulecitebib.bib251 based on the free-field form of the QCD angular momentum, JвҶ’вҶ’рқҗҪdisplaystylevecJ =вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢПҲвҖ вҖӢОЈвҶ’2вҖӢПҲ+вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢПҲвҖ вҖӢ[ОҫвҶ’Г—(вҲ’iвҖӢвҲҮвҶ’)]вҖӢПҲabsentsuperscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүsuperscriptрқң“вҖ вҶ’ОЈ2рқң“superscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүsuperscriptрқң“вҖ delimited-вҶ’рқңүрқ‘–вҶ’вҲҮрқң“displaystyle=int d3xi psidaggerfracvecSigma2psi+int d3xi psidaggerleft[vecxitimes(-ivecnabla)right]psi +вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢEвҶ’Г—AвҶ’+вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢEiвҖӢ(ОҫвҶ’Г—вҲҮвҶ’)вҖӢAi,superscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүвҶ’рқҗёвҶ’рқҗҙsuperscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүsuperscriptрқҗёрқ‘–вҶ’рқңүвҶ’вҲҮsuperscriptрқҗҙрқ‘–displaystyle +int d3xi vecEtimesvecA+int d3xi Ei left(vecxitimesvecnablaright)Ai , where iрқ‘–i is the spatial Lorentz index. Except for the first one, the other three operators are gauge dependent, and their matrix elements are generally frame dependent. In high-energy scattering, there is one frame and gauge that are special: the IMF and light-front gauge, A+=0superscriptрқҗҙ0A+=0. Therefore, the naive partonic sum rule for proton spin can be expressed ascitebib.bib251 12=12вҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+lqzвҖӢ(Ој)+О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)+lgzвҖӢ(Ој),1212О”ОЈрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮО”рқҗәрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮdisplaystylefrac12=frac12DeltaSigma(mu)+lzq(mu)+Delta G(mu)+lzg(mu),, where lqzвҖӢ(Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮlzq(mu) and lgzвҖӢ(Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮlzg(mu) are the canonical OAM of the quark and gluon partons, respectively. Both lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz and lgzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘”рқ‘§lgz can be related to twist-three GPDscitebib.bib229, bib.bib281, bib.bib232, which can be accessed through spin-asymmetries in hard exclusive processescitebib.bib283, bib.bib231, bib.bib68, bib.bib67 (see the recent reviewcitebib.bib284). To fully understand the partonic spin structure of the proton, one also needs to determine the quark and gluon canonical OAM, lqzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һlzq and lgzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”lzg. LaMET allows extraction of lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz and lgzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘”рқ‘§lgz from lattice calculation in the same way as the gluon helicity that was reviewed in Sec. . The quasi-partonic OAM operators can be chosen as the free-field operators fixed in gauges that belong to the universality classcitebib.bib230. Their matrix elements lqzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һtildelzq and lgzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”tildelzg can be calculated from the off-forward matrix elements of the relevant energy-momentum tensorscitebib.bib460, for example, lqzвҖӢ(2вҖӢSz)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һ2superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyletildelzq(2Sz) =limО”вҶ’0ПөiвҖӢjвҖӢвҲӮвҲӮiвҖӢО”iвҖӢвҹЁPвҖІвҖӢS|ПҲвҖ вҖӢвҖӢiвҖӢвҲӮjПҲвҖӢ|PвҖӢSвҹ©|вҲҮвҶ’вӢ…AвҶ’=0. absentevaluated-atsubscriptвҶ’О”0superscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—рқ‘–superscriptО”рқ‘–quantum-operator-productsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІрқ‘Ҷsuperscriptрқң“вҖ 0рқ‘–superscriptрқ‘—рқң“0рқ‘ғрқ‘ҶвӢ…вҶ’вҲҮвҶ’рқҗҙ0displaystyle=limDeltato 0epsilonijpartialoverpartial iDeltai PprimeS|psidaggeripartialjpsi|PSBig|vecnablacdotvecA=0,. where the kinematics is the same as Eq. . Along with О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G, lqzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һtildelzq and lgzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”tildelzg can be matched to the partonic quantities defined in the Jaffe-Manohar sum rule through the factorization formulas, lqzвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyletildelzq(Pz,mu) =PqвҖӢqвҖӢlqzвҖӢ(Ој)+PgвҖӢqвҖӢlgzвҖӢ(Ој)absentsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘һрқ‘һsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘”рқ‘һsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮdisplaystyle=Pqqlzq(mu)+Pgqlzg(mu) +pqвҖӢqвҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+pgвҖӢqвҖӢО”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)+вҖҰ,subscriptрқ‘қрқ‘һрқ‘һО”ОЈрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘”рқ‘һО”рқҗәрқңҮвҖҰdisplaystyleqquad+pqqDeltaSigma(mu)+pgqDelta G(mu)+. . . ,, lgzвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyletildelzg(Pz,mu) =PqвҖӢgвҖӢlqzвҖӢ(Ој)+PgвҖӢgвҖӢlgzвҖӢ(Ој)absentsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘һрқ‘”subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘”рқ‘”subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮdisplaystyle=Pqglzq(mu)+Pgglzg(mu) +pqвҖӢgвҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+pgвҖӢgвҖӢО”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)+вҖҰ,subscriptрқ‘қрқ‘һрқ‘”О”ОЈрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘”рқ‘”О”рқҗәрқңҮвҖҰdisplaystyleqquad+pqgDeltaSigma(mu)+pggDelta G(mu)+. . . ,, where вӢҜвӢҜcdots are power corrections suppressed by the momentum Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz, and the one-loop matching coefficients in front of each term on the r. h. s. have been calculated in the Coulomb gaugecitebib.bib287. Since the quasi-partonic operators are gauge-variant and need to be fixed in a particular gauge, they can mix with new operators that are not allowed by Lorentz or gauge symmetries. For example, the gauge-dependent potential angular momentum ПҲвҖ вҖӢ(rвҶ’Г—AвҶ’)вҖӢПҲsuperscriptрқң“вҖ вҶ’рқ‘ҹвҶ’рқҗҙрқң“psidagger(vecrtimesvecA)psi comes into playcitebib.bib432, bib.bib278. Such mixings must be taken into account in lattice renormalization to have a controlled calculation of the canonical OAM. Apart from the above approach, it has also been proposed to calculate the ratio of lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz and the valence quark number from the derivatives of off-forward matrix elements of staple-shaped quark Wilson line operatorscitebib.bib189, whose definition can be found in Eq. below. The first lattice calculations with this approach have been carried out incitebib.bib189, bib.bib190, which shows different size of effects between the kinetic and canonical OAM. For systematic improvement in this calculation, one should include the matching of such matrix elements to the physical lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz in the limit when the transverse separation of the quark fields approaches zero. For the transverse polarization, it is natural to define a twist-two partonic transverse angular momentum density of quarkscitebib.bib240, bib.bib280, bib.bib282, bib.bib284, JвҹӮqвҖӢ(x)=xвҖӢ[qвҖӢ(x)+EqвҖӢ(x)]/2,subscriptsuperscriptрқҗҪрқ‘һperpendicular-toрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙdelimited-рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙ2Jqperp(x)=xleft[q(x)+Eq(x)right]/2, and similarly for the gluons, where qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙq(x) is the unpolarized quark/antiquark distributions, and Eq,gвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘”рқ‘ҘEq,g(x) is the GPDs defined earlier in this section. Thus to get a simple partonic picture of the proton transverse spin from the first principles, it is important to calculate the GPD EвҖӢ(x)рқҗёрқ‘ҘE(x) using LaMET.
content_sections.V Transverse-Momentum Dependent PDFs: The transverse-momentum-dependent (TMD) parton distribution functions (TMDPDFs) are a natural generalization of the collinear PDFs to include both longitudinal and transverse momentum of partons. They are in principle probability distributions fiвҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ,Пғ)subscriptрқ‘“рқ‘–рқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toрқңҺfi(x,veckperp,sigma) of finding a parton of given species iрқ‘–i, longitudinal and transverse momentum (xвҖӢP+,kвҶ’вҹӮ)рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-to(xP+,veckperp), and polarization ПғрқңҺsigma inside the hadron state. TMDPDFs are playing an increasingly important role in understanding the partonic structure of hadrons and high-energy scattering. The TMD parton densities were firstly introduced by Collins and Soper in 1980scitebib.bib141, bib.bib142, bib.bib144, bib.bib146, bib.bib145, bib.bib72 to understand the Drell-Yan (DY) and e+вҖӢeвҲ’superscriptрқ‘’superscriptрқ‘’e+e- annihilation process, and generalized incitebib.bib264, bib.bib263 to semi-inclusive deep-inelastic scattering(SIDIS) process. The TMD factorization has been reanalyzed in the framework of SCET in which modes are made manifest by effective fields citebib.bib48, bib.bib49, bib.bib354, bib.bib51, bib.bib181, bib.bib179, bib.bib125. Various TMD factorization formalisms finally converged to the standard one where a scheme-independent TMDPDF can be definedcitebib.bib179, bib.bib139, bib.bib134. The TMD parton densities are important in understanding the experimental processes where the transverse momenta of final state particles are measured. For example, in DY pair and W,Zрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ҚW,Z production it is known that the differential cross section dвҖӢПғ/dвҖӢQT2рқ‘‘рқңҺрқ‘‘superscriptsubscriptрқ‘„рқ‘Ү2dsigma/dQT2 normally peaks at relatively small transverse momentum. For QвҲј10similar-toрқ‘„10Qsim 10 GeV, the peak is typically located at QвҹӮвҲј1similar-tosubscriptрқ‘„perpendicular-to1Qperpsim 1 GeV where nonperturbative effects are importantcitebib.bib146. A good knowledge of TMD parton densities is therefore crucial for the determination of the cross sections and precision test of perturbative QCD predictions. Besides their importance in understanding the high-energy experimental data, the TMD parton densities are also important by themselves for their crucial role in describing hadron structures. With them, one can simultaneously study the fast-moving collinear physics through the longitudinal xрқ‘Ҙx-dependencies, and the nonperturbative effect from the transverse kвҶ’вҹӮsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toveckperp-dependencies. Moreover, the TMDPDFs are sensitive to effects such as soft radiations. Therefore, the physics in the presence of transverse degrees of freedom is rather rich. This is particularly true in studies of spin-dependent phenomena where one can define various TMDPDFs through Lorentz decompositions (see Sec. V. B). One example is the Sivers function for a transversely polarized proton, ПөiвҖӢjвҖӢkвҹӮiвҖӢSвҹӮiвҖӢf1вҖӢTвҹӮвҖӢ(x,kвҹӮ)subscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҳрқ‘–perpendicular-tosubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘–perpendicular-tosubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘“perpendicular-to1рқ‘Үрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ҳperpendicular-toepsilonijkiperpSiperpfperp1T(x,kperp), which is naive-time-reversal odd and is predicted to change sign between the DY and SIDIS processescitebib.bib132. Similar properties also exist in the Boer-Mulders functioncitebib.bib73 concerning a transversely-polarized parton distribution in an unpolarized hadron. These two functions are related to the single transverse spin asymmetry. If we generalize the TMDPDFs to include the impact parameter dependence, we can further define the Wigner function, the parton orbital angular momentum distributions, etccitebib.bib54, bib.bib344. Therefore, the TMDPDFs allow for a more complete and refined 3D description (or tomography) of the hadron structurecitebib.bib99, bib.bib74. The 3D tomography of the proton is a major physical goal of the EIC program. The TMDPDFs are also important in understanding small-xрқ‘Ҙx physicscitebib.bib311, bib.bib40, bib.bib38, bib.bib307, bib.bib308. Our current knowledge on TMDPDFs mainly comes from fitting to the experimental datacitebib.bib313, bib.bib304, bib.bib423, bib.bib180, bib.bib297, bib.bib30, bib.bib408, bib.bib60, bib.bib410, bib.bib29. This is, however, rather primitive due to the paucity of data. Although the future EIC will make up the gap and produce more data for TMD measurements, it is still important to develop first-principle methods for the determination of nonperturbative TMDPDFs, which can serve as a test or provide useful inputs to constrain the global fits. LaMET provides a systematic way to extract TMDPDFs from the lattice calculations. Early studiescitebib.bib279, bib.bib271, bib.bib176, bib.bib177 have tried to construct a quasi-TMDPDF on the lattice, but its relation to the physical TMDPDF is expected to be nonperturbative due to complications in the soft functioncitebib.bib177. The recent works incitebib.bib273, bib.bib272 provide a formulation to calculate the soft function so that a perturbative matching formula can be established between the quasi- and physical TMDPDFs, allowing for a complete determination of the latter from lattice QCD. In this section we review the application of LaMET to the nonperturbative TMDPDFs. The investigation is still in its early stage and a lot remains to be explored, particularly in lattice calculations and matching. In the first subsection we introduce the TMDPDFs and discuss the associated rapidity divergences. In the following subsections, we define the quasi-TMDPDFs or TMD momentum distributions in a proton of finite momentum, and study their momentum RGEs and UV renormalization properties. In the process, we introduce the off-light-cone soft functions. We then present the factorization of the quasi-TMDPDFs into the light-cone TMDPDFs and the off-light-cone soft function, where various one-loop results and the relevant RGEs are also given. The properties of the off-light-cone soft function are discussed in the last subsection, where it is shown to be related to the form factor of a pair of charged color sources, which paves the way for its calculation on a Euclidean lattice.
content_sections.V Transverse-Momentum Dependent PDFs V.1: As explained in Sec. , we can define various TMDPDFs by choosing different gauge-links between the quark or gluon bilinears. The one relevant to high-energy phenomena is defined with light-like Wilson lines. The links represent the propagation of high-energy color-charged particles, and are crucial in forming gauge-invariant nonlocal operatorscitebib.bib53. As argued in previous sections, such operators are the result of an EFT description (more explicitly so in SCET) arising from taking the infinite-momentum limit of the proton. Thus, it is natural to expect that they require additional regularization and renormalization. Let us take the non-singlet quark unpolarized TMDPDF as an example. Without the field theoretic subtleties, the distribution is fвҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ)=12вҖӢP+вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢd2вҖӢbвҶ’вҹӮ(2вҖӢПҖ)2вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢО»вҖӢx+iвҖӢkвҶ’вҹӮвӢ…bвҶ’вҹӮрқ‘“рқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-to12superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘‘2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tosuperscript2рқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶрқ‘ҘвӢ…рқ‘–subscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-tosubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-todisplaystyle f(x,veckperp)=frac12P+intfracdlambda2pifracd2vecbperp(2pi)2e-ilambda x+iveckperpcdotvecbperp Г—вҹЁP|ПҲВҜвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn/2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢОі+вҖӢрқ’ІnвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn/2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(вҲ’О»вҖӢn/2)|Pвҹ©,absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғВҜрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tosuperscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңҶрқ‘ӣ2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2рқ‘ғdisplaystyletimes P|barpsi(lambda n/2+vecbperp)gamma+cal Wn(lambda n/2+vecbperp)psi(-lambda n/2)|P , where рқ’ІnвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn+bвҶ’вҹӮ)subscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңҶрқ‘ӣsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tocal Wn(lambda n+vecbperp) is the staple-shaped gauge-link of the form рқ’ІnвҖӢ(Оҫ)=WnвҖ вҖӢ(Оҫ)вҖӢWвҹӮвҖӢWnвҖӢ(вҲ’ОҫвӢ…pвҖӢn),subscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңүsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҠвҖ рқ‘ӣрқңүsubscriptрқ‘Ҡperpendicular-tosubscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣвӢ…рқңүрқ‘қрқ‘ӣdisplaystylecal Wn(xi)=Wdaggern(xi)WperpWn(-xicdot pn) , WnвҖӢ(Оҫ)=рқ’«вҖӢexpвҒЎ[вҲ’iвҖӢgвҖӢвҲ«0вҲ’вҲһрқ‘‘О»вҖӢnвӢ…AвҖӢ(Оҫ+О»вҖӢn)],subscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣрқңүрқ’«рқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptsubscript0вӢ…differential-dрқңҶрқ‘ӣрқҗҙрқңүрқңҶрқ‘ӣdisplaystyle Wn(xi)=cal Pexpleft[-igint0-inftydlambda ncdot A(xi+lambda n)right] , along the light-cone direction nОјsuperscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮnmu, as shown in Fig. . The WвҹӮsubscriptрқ‘Ҡperpendicular-toWperp is a transverse gauge-link at light-cone infinity to maintain gauge-invariance. . If one uses LFQ and ignores the transverse gauge-link, the above distribution is just вҹЁP|bвҖ вҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢbвҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ)|Pвҹ©quantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘ҸвҖ рқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toрқ‘Ҹрқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toрқ‘ғ P|bdagger(x,veckperp)b(x,veckperp)|P for x>0рқ‘Ҙ0x>0, as expected. However, there are a number of qualifications in the above definition. First, the light-like gauge-links рқ’Іnsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣcal Wn are chosen to be past-pointing in accordance with the DY kinematics, but for SIDIS they should be chosen as future-pointing, as shown in Fig. . For unpolarized TMDPDFs there is no distinction between the two choices, but for spin-dependent TMDPDFs there are physical consequences associated with the direction of gauge-links. Second, there exists a new type of divergence associated with the infinitely-long light-like gauge-links. These divergences are due to radiation of gluons collinear to the light-like gauge-link and cannot be regularized by the standard UV regulators. An example is the following integral in dimensional regularization (DR)citebib.bib177, I=вҲ«рқ‘‘k+вҖӢрқ‘‘kвҲ’вҖӢfвҖӢ(k+вҖӢkвҲ’)(k+вҖӢkвҲ’)1+Пө=12вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢyyвҖӢвҲ«рқ‘‘m2вҖӢfвҖӢ(m2)m2+2вҖӢПө,рқҗјdifferential-dsuperscriptрқ‘ҳdifferential-dsuperscriptрқ‘ҳрқ‘“superscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳ1italic-Пө12рқ‘‘рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұdifferential-dsuperscriptрқ‘ҡ2рқ‘“superscriptрқ‘ҡ2superscriptрқ‘ҡ22italic-ПөI=int dk+dk-fracf(k+k-)(k+k-)1+epsilon=frac12intfracdyyint dm2fracf(m2)m2+2epsilon,, where m2=k+вҖӢkвҲ’superscriptрқ‘ҡ2superscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳm2=k+k- and y=k+/kвҲ’рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳy=k+/k- is the rapidity-related variable. The divergences in yрқ‘Ұy arise from large and small yрқ‘Ұy where the integral is unregulated. The contribution from k+=0superscriptрқ‘ҳ0k+=0 is called the light-zero mode in LFQ, where it is also called light-cone divergence which causes considerable problems. To regulate the light-cone or rapidity divergences, a number of methods have been introduced in the literature (for a review seecitebib.bib177). They can be put into two classes: on-light-cone regulators and off-light-cone regulators. In the former case, the gauge-links are kept along the light-cone direction nОјsuperscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮnmu after regularization. For example, the so-called Оҙрқӣҝdelta regulatorcitebib.bib182, bib.bib183 regularizes the gauge-link as: WnвҖӢ(Оҫ)вҶ’WnвҖӢ(Оҫ)|ОҙвҲ’вҶ’subscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣрқңүevaluated-atsubscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣрқңүsuperscriptрқӣҝdisplaystyle Wn(xi)rightarrow Wn(xi)|delta- =рқ’«вҖӢexpвҖӢ[вҲ’iвҖӢgвҖӢвҲ«0вҲ’вҲһрқ‘‘О»вҖӢA+вҖӢ(Оҫ+О»вҖӢn)вҖӢeвҲ’ОҙвҲ’2вҖӢp+вҖӢ|О»|],absentрқ’«expdelimited-рқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptsubscript0differential-dрқңҶsuperscriptрқҗҙрқңүрқңҶрқ‘ӣsuperscriptрқ‘’superscriptрқӣҝ2superscriptрқ‘қрқңҶdisplaystyle=cal Prm expleft[-igint0-inftydlambda A+(xi+lambda n)e-fracdelta-2p+|lambda|right],, and similarly for the conjugate direction. The Оҙрқӣҝdelta regulator breaks gauge-invariance, but preserves the boost invariance ОҙВұвҶ’eВұYвҖӢОҙВұвҶ’superscriptрқӣҝplus-or-minussuperscriptрқ‘’plus-or-minusрқ‘Ңsuperscriptрқӣҝplus-or-minusdeltapmrightarrow epm Ydeltapm where Yрқ‘ҢY is the rapidity of the Lorentz boost. Other on-light-cone regulators include the exponential regulatorcitebib.bib323, О·рқңӮeta regulatorcitebib.bib125, analytical regulatorcitebib.bib51, etc. In the remainder of this section, we will use the Оҙрқӣҝdelta regulator as a representative whenever we need an on-light-cone regulator. The off-light-cone regulator was introduced in citebib.bib141, bib.bib264, bib.bib263, bib.bib132, and also used in citebib.bib264. This type of regulator chooses off-light-cone directions to avoid the rapidity divergence. One can choose, for instance, to deform the gauge-links into the space-like region: nвҶ’nY=nвҲ’eвҲ’2вҖӢYвҖӢp(p+)2. вҶ’рқ‘ӣsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘Ңрқ‘ӣsuperscriptрқ‘’2рқ‘Ңрқ‘қsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘қ2displaystyle nrightarrow nY=n-e-2Yfracp(p+)2,. Here Yрқ‘ҢY plays the role of a rapidity regulator, as when YвҶ’вҲһвҶ’рқ‘ҢYrightarrowinfty, nYвҶ’nвҶ’subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘Ңрқ‘ӣnYrightarrow n. In certain cases one can also deform nYsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘ҢnY into time-like regioncitebib.bib138. The on-light-cone regulators are consistent with the spirit of parton physics, and therefore are useful to define COM-momentum-independent parton densities. The off-light-cone regulators, on the other hand, follow a similar spirit as LaMET, and therefore can be exploited for practical lattice QCD calculations, as we shall see in the next subsection. To avoid light-cone divergences, from now on we include the rapidity regulator in the definition of the light-cone TMDPDFs. The rapidity divergence for TMDPDFs can be removed by the soft function, which also plays an important role in TMD factorization. Intuitively, the soft function represents a cross section for fast-moving charged particles emitting soft gluons into final states. We should emphasize that although fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD is free from rapidity divergences, it does contain soft radiation from the charged particles in the initial state. This can be seen clearly by considering Feynman diagrams for the unsubtracted fрқ‘“f and applying soft approximation to gluons. вҖңOne-halfвҖқ of the soft contribution in fрқ‘“f is subtracted to define the physical fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD due to the requirement of factorization of physical processes. The remaining soft radiation also has a natural rapidity cut-off associated with lnвҒЎ(xвҖӢP+)рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғln(xP+), reflected in the О¶рқңҒzeta-dependence. What is remarkable, however, is that fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD is rapidity-regulator independent. Although a general proof to all orders in perturbation theory is beyond the scope of this review, it is due to factorization and exponentiation of the soft physics in fрқ‘“f and thus the scheme cancellation can be done systematically in the exponent. It worth mentioning that in old-fashioned or SCET-like approaches, one can define the вҖңsubtractedвҖқ TMDPDF or вҖңbeams functionsвҖқ that contains only collinear physics. However, they are generically scheme dependent and must be combined with an extra soft functions in factorization theorems.
