content_sections.I Introduction: Parton distribution functions (PDFs) are the foundation for understanding the structure of hadrons in terms of their partonic content. Together with the generalized parton distributions (GPDs) and the transverse-momentum dependent distributions (TMDs) form a set of quantities that are needed for the mapping of hadrons, in the coordinate and momentum space. The most well-constraint distribution functions are the PDFs, which depend only on the momentum fraction carried by the partons. These can be obtained from a number of scattering processes and have a wide kinematical coverage (see, e. g. , Ref. citebib.bib1, bib.bib2). Furthermore, the individual-flavor contributions are also studied in phenomenological fits for the colinear PDFs. The present calculation is motivated by the fact that not all PDFs are well-constrained from global analyses. The number of available experimental data sets in the case of the transversity is less by рқ’ӘвҖӢрқ’Ә10cal O compared to the helicity, and рқ’ӘвҖӢрқ’Ә100cal O compared to the unpolarized PDFs. In addition, isolating the strange-quark PDF from the down-quark PDFs can be challenging, as most of the high-energy processes cannot differentiate between the two flavors. For example, there is a disagreement on the sign of the strange-quark helicity PDF, О”вҖӢsвҖӢ(x)+О”вҖӢsВҜвҖӢ(x)О”рқ‘ рқ‘ҘО”ВҜрқ‘ рқ‘ҘDelta s(x)+Deltabars(x), from analysis of polarized inclusive deep inelastic scatteringcitebib.bib3, bib.bib4 and global analyses of inclusive and semi-inclusive deep inelastic scattering data setscitebib.bib5, bib.bib6, bib.bib7, bib.bib8. The large uncertainties in the strange PDFs have an effect on other quantities, such as the Wрқ‘ҠW-boson mass and the determination of the CKM matrix element VcвҖӢssubscriptрқ‘үрқ‘җрқ‘ Vcscitebib.bib9, bib.bib10. Therefore, calculations of the individual-quark PDFs from lattice QCD can, eventually, be used as input in analysis requiring knowledge of PDFs. The flavor decomposition of proton charges and form factors had been under investigation in the last few years with calculations of disconnected diagrams using ensembles at or near the physical values for the quark masses(physical point)citebib.bib11, bib.bib12, bib.bib13, bib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib16, bib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib22, bib.bib23, bib.bib24. Such a success in lattice calculations of hadron structure is partly due to available computational resources, but also due to novel methodologies to extract the individual quark Mellin moments and form factors. A notable example is the hierarchical probingcitebib.bib25, which improves the signal significantly. In this work, we extend hierarchical probing to non-local operators. Such an approach was shown to be successful in our first calculation on the helicity PDFcitebib.bib26. Matrix elements of non-local operators are of great interest in recent years. These can be related to light-cone PDFs through a factorization and matching procedure. Methods to access the xрқ‘Ҙx dependence of PDFs, such as the quasi-PDFscitebib.bib27, bib.bib28, pseudo-ITDscitebib.bib29, and current-current correlatorscitebib.bib30, bib.bib31 are now well established. Progress in terms of the renormalizability, renormalization prescription, and factorization of light-cone PDFs has been made, alleviating major sources of systematic uncertainties. For their application in lattice QCD see the recent reviews of Ref. citebib.bib32, bib.bib33, bib.bib34. Results from lattice QCD simulations on the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of PDFs are very promising, and therefore, their flavor decomposition is the extension of these investigations. The matching for the singlet case, as well as the mixing with the gluon PDFs have been recently addressedcitebib.bib35, bib.bib36. In Ref. citebib.bib26 we presented the first calculation for the helicity PDFs including disconnected contributions and the flavor decomposition for the up-, down- and strange-quark PDFs. Here we extend the calculation to the three types of collinear PDFs, that is the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs. While such calculations are becoming feasible, there are a number of computational challenges before taming the statistical uncertainties. To date, calculations of disconnected contributions for matrix elements of non-local operators at the physical point do not exist. The paper is organized as follows: Section presents the methodology of extracting the nucleon matrix elements, the renormalization and matching. Section focuses on the details of the calculation and the techniques employed. The results for the disconnected and connected matrix elements are presented in Sections and , respectively. In Section, we extract the vector, axial and tensor charges using the data with zero length for the Wilson line. The PDF reconstruction and flavor decomposition is given in Section. Finally, we give out conclusions in Section.
content_sections.II Methodology II.1 Nucleon bare matrix: The main component of this study is the calculation of the nucleon matrix elements of non-local operators, that is hО“ОҘвҖӢ(z,P3)=вҹЁNвҖӢ(P3)|ПҲВҜвҖӢ(z)вҖӢОҘвҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(z)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ|NвҖӢ(P3)вҹ©,superscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3quantum-operator-productрқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ғ3ВҜрқң“рқ‘§ОҘО“рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§рқң“0рқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ғ3hGammaUpupsilon(z,P3)=braketN(P3)overlinepsi(z)Upupsilon,Gamma,W(z)psiN(P3), where |NвҖӢ(P3)вҹ©ketрқ‘Ғsubscriptрқ‘ғ3|N(P3) is the nucleon state with momentum boost along the zрқ‘§z-direction, i. e. PвҶ’=(0,0,P3)вҶ’рқ‘ғ00subscriptрқ‘ғ3vecP=(0,0,P3). The fermionic field ПҲвҖӢ(x)вүЎПҲвҖӢ(xвҶ’,t)рқң“рқ‘Ҙрқң“вҶ’рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ўpsi(x)equivpsi(vecx,t) can be either the light quark doublet ПҲвүЎ(u,d)Tрқң“superscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқ‘Үpsiequiv(u,d)T or the strange quark field ПҲвҖӢ(x)вүЎsвҖӢ(x)рқң“рқ‘Ҙрқ‘ рқ‘Ҙpsi(x)equiv s(x). The Wilson line WвҖӢ(z)рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§W(z) is constructed in the direction parallel to the nucleon boost PвҶ’вҶ’рқ‘ғvecP and extends from zero length to up to half of the lattice, L/2рқҗҝ2L/2, in both positive and negative directions. The Dirac structure of the operator, О“О“Gamma, acts in spin space and depends on the type of the collinear PDF under study. Without loss of generality, one can take that the momentum boost is in the zрқ‘§z-direction (kрқ‘ҳk). Based on this, we employ the following О“О“Gamma matrices: вҖў О“=Оі0О“superscriptрқӣҫ0Gamma=gamma0 for the unpolarized distribution qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙq(x); вҖў О“=Оі5вҖӢОі3О“superscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқӣҫ3Gamma=gamma5gamma3 for the helicity distribution О”вҖӢqвҖӢ(x)О”рқ‘һрқ‘ҘDelta q(x); вҖў О“=Пғ3вҖӢjО“subscriptрқңҺ3рқ‘—Gamma=sigma3j with jвү 3рқ‘—3jneq 3 for the transversity distribution ОҙвҖӢqвҖӢ(x)рқӣҝрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdelta q(x). We calculate both the isovector and isoscalar quark contributions to Eq. , and, therefore, we introduce the superscript ОҘОҘUpupsilon for the matrix elements. The matrix ОҘОҘUpupsilon acts on the light quark sector and takes the value П„3вҖӢsuperscriptрқңҸ3double-struck-рқҹҷtau3, for the isovector (isoscalar) distribution, where П„3=diagвҖӢ(1,вҲ’1)superscriptрқңҸ3diag11tau3=rm diag(1,-1), is the third Pauli matrix. The matrix elements are computed from the ratio of three- and two-point functions defined as C2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;ts,0)=рқ’«ОұвҖӢОІвҖӢвҲ‘xвҶ’eвҲ’iвҖӢPвҶ’вӢ…xвҶ’вҖӢвҹЁО©|NОұвҖӢ(xвҶ’,ts)вҖӢNВҜОІвҖӢ(0вҶ’,0)|О©вҹ©C3вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;tins;ts,0)=рқ’«ОұвҖӢОІвҖӢвҲ‘xвҶ’,yвҶ’eвҲ’iвҖӢPвҶ’вӢ…xвҶ’вҖӢвҹЁО©|NОұвҖӢ(xвҶ’,ts)вҖӢрқ’ӘвҖӢ(yвҶ’,tins;z)вҖӢNВҜОІвҖӢ(0вҶ’,0)|О©вҹ©,subscriptрқҗ¶2рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0subscriptрқ’«рқӣјрқӣҪsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–вҶ’рқ‘ғвҶ’рқ‘Ҙquantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘ҒрқӣјвҶ’рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ subscriptВҜрқ‘ҒрқӣҪвҶ’00О©subscriptрқҗ¶3рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўinssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0subscriptрқ’«рқӣјрқӣҪsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҘвҶ’рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘’вӢ…рқ‘–вҶ’рқ‘ғвҶ’рқ‘Ҙquantum-operator-productО©subscriptрқ‘ҒрқӣјвҶ’рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ’ӘвҶ’рқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘Ўinsрқ‘§subscriptВҜрқ‘ҒрқӣҪвҶ’00О©beginsplitC2pt(vecP;ts,0)=Palphabetasumlimitsvecxe-ivecPcdotvecxbraketOmegaNalpha(vecx,ts)overlineNbeta(vec0,0)Omega C3pt(vecP;trm ins;ts,0)=tildePalphabetasumlimitsvecx,vecye-ivecPcdotvecxbraketOmegaNalpha(vecx,ts)O(vecy,trm ins;z)overlineNbeta(vec0,0)Omega,endsplit where tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts is the source-sink separation, and tinssubscriptрқ‘Ўinstrm ins the insertion time of the three-point function. We use the proton interpolating field NОұ=ОөaвҖӢbвҖӢcвҖӢuОұaвҖӢ(x)вҖӢ(dbвҖӢTвҖӢ(x)вҖӢрқ’һвҖӢОі5вҖӢucвҖӢ(x))subscriptрқ‘ҒрқӣјsuperscriptрқңҖрқ‘Һрқ‘Ҹрқ‘җsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ўрқӣјрқ‘Һрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘‘рқ‘Ҹрқ‘Үрқ‘Ҙрқ’һsuperscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘җрқ‘ҘNalpha=varepsilonabcualphaa(x)left(dbT(x)Cgamma5uc(x)right) with рқ’һ=Оі0вҖӢОі2рқ’һsuperscriptрқӣҫ0superscriptрқӣҫ2C=gamma0gamma2. The three-point function projector depends on the operator under study and can be found in Table and for the two-point function we use рқ’«=(1ВұОі0)/2рқ’«plus-or-minus1subscriptрқӣҫ02cal P=(1pmgamma0)/2. To increase the number of measurements for the disconnected diagrams, we average the three- and two- point functions over plus and minus parity projectors. The operator рқ’Әрқ’ӘO is defined as рқ’ӘвҖӢ(yвҶ’,tins;z)=ПҲВҜвҖӢ(yвҶ’+zвҖӢz,tins)вҖӢОҘвҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(yвҶ’+zвҖӢz,yвҶ’)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(yвҶ’,П„),рқ’ӘвҶ’рқ‘Ұsubscriptрқ‘Ўinsрқ‘§ВҜрқң“вҶ’рқ‘Ұрқ‘§рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ЎinsОҘО“рқ‘ҠвҶ’рқ‘Ұрқ‘§рқ‘§вҶ’рқ‘Ұрқң“вҶ’рқ‘ҰрқңҸO(vecy,trm ins;z)=barpsi(vecy+zhatz,trm ins)Upupsilon,Gamma,W(vecy+zhatz,vecy)psi(vecy,tau),, and is inserted in the three-point function of Eq. . The Wick contractions lead to two topologically different diagrams, as shown in Fig. and . In Fig. , we also show pictorially the two-point function of Eq. . For the case that the fermionic field in Eq. is ПҲ=(u,d)Tрқң“superscriptрқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқ‘Үpsi=(u,d)T and ОҘ=П„3ОҘsuperscriptрқңҸ3Upupsilon=tau3, we obtain the matrix elements for the isovector distribution uвҲ’dрқ‘ўрқ‘‘u-d, which receive contribution from the connected diagram only (Fig. ). However, in the case where ОҘ=рқҹҷОҘdouble-struck-рқҹҷUpupsilon=1, the three-point function takes contributions from both connected and disconnected diagrams. For the nucleon, the strange-quark contribution comes exclusively from the disconnected diagram. We emphasize that, disconnected contributions have a considerably smaller signal-to-noise ratio compared to the connected ones and their evaluation requires the use of stochastic and gauge noise reduction techniques described in detail in Sec. . The ratio of three- over two-point functions becomes, вҹЁC3вҖӢpвҖӢt(P3;tins;ts,0вҹ©вҹЁC2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(P3;ts,0)вҹ©=0вүӘtinsвүӘts(hО“ОҘ)bareвҖӢ(z,P3),fracbraketC3pt(P3;trm ins;ts,0braketC2pt(P3;ts,0)stackrelscriptstyle 0ll trm insll ts=left(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare(z,P3),,, and is used to obtain the matrix elements of Eq. . This relation is meaningful for the ground-state contribution. To isolate the latter, we apply constant (plateau) fits in a range where the operator insertion time is large enough, and away from the source and sink. Besides the plateau fit method, we employ different techniques allowing the extraction of the nucleon matrix element, as described in Sec. .
content_sections.II Methodology II.2 Non-perturbative ren: The bare matrix elements of Eq. must be renormalized in coordinate space prior to obtaining the quasi-PDFs, which are defined in the momentum space (see Sec. ). The renormalization of both the non-singlet and singlet quantities is multiplicative. In this work, we use the non-singlet renormalization function, as the difference with the singlet is expected to be smallcitebib.bib37, which was demonstrated numerically for local operatorscitebib.bib20, bib.bib18. The mixing between the unpolarized and helicity singlet-quark PDFs with the gluon PDFs arises at the matching level because there is no additional non-local ultraviolet divergence in the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib38, bib.bib35, bib.bib36. To renormalize the matrix elements we apply the regularization independent (RI) scheme, and use the momentum source methodcitebib.bib39 that offers high statistical accuracy. More details on the setup can be found in Refs. citebib.bib40, bib.bib41. In Refs. citebib.bib42, bib.bib43, we proposed an extension of the renormalization prescription to include non-local operators, which we also follow in this work. The conditions for the renormalization functions of the non-local operator, ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma, and the quark field, Zqsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һZq, are (ZqimprвҖӢ(Ој0))вҲ’1вҖӢZО“вҖӢ(z,Ој0)вҖӢTrвҖӢ[рқ’ұО“вҖӢ(p,z)вҖӢpМё]|p2=Ој02=TrвҖӢ[рқ’ұО“BornвҖӢ(p,z)вҖӢpМё]|p2=Ој02,evaluated-atsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һimprsubscriptрқңҮ01subscriptрқ‘ҚО“рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Trdelimited-subscriptрқ’ұО“рқ‘қрқ‘§italic-pМёsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮ02evaluated-atTrdelimited-superscriptsubscriptрқ’ұО“Bornрқ‘қрқ‘§italic-pМёsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮ02displaystyleleft(Zqrm impr(mu0)right)-1,ZGamma(z,mu0),rm Trleft[cal VGamma(p,z),notpright]Bigr|p2=mu02=rm Trleft[cal VGammarm Born(p,z),notpright]Bigr|p2=mu02,, ZqimprвҖӢ(Ој0)=(112вҖӢTrвҖӢ[(SвҖӢ(p))вҲ’1вҖӢSBornвҖӢ(p)]вҲ’dвҖӢZqвҲһвҖӢ(p))|p2=Ој02. subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қimprрқ‘һsubscriptрқңҮ0evaluated-at112Trdelimited-superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘қ1superscriptрқ‘ҶBornрқ‘қрқ‘‘subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һрқ‘қsuperscriptрқ‘қ2superscriptsubscriptрқңҮ02displaystyle Zrm imprq(mu0)=left(frac112rm Trleft[(S(p))-1,Srm Born(p)right]-dZinftyq(p)right)Bigr|p2=mu02,. qquad рқ’ұвҖӢ(p,z)рқ’ұрқ‘қрқ‘§cal V(p,z) (SвҖӢ(p)рқ‘Ҷрқ‘қS(p)) is the amputated vertex function of the operator (fermion propagator) and SBornвҖӢ(p)superscriptрқ‘ҶBornрқ‘қSrm Born(p) is the tree-level of the propagator. These conditions are applied at each value of zрқ‘§z separately. We improve Zqsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һZq, and consequently ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma, by subtracting lattice artifacts calculated to one-loop level in perturbation theory and to all orders in the lattice spacing, dвҖӢZqвҲһвҖӢ(p)рқ‘‘subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘һрқ‘қdZinftyq(p). The details of the calculation can be found in Ref. citebib.bib40. The RI-type schemes are mass-independent, and therefore ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma is calculated at several ensembles with different values for the quark masses. Eventually, a chiral extrapolation is applied to remove residual artifacts related to the quark mass. Here we use five ensembles that are generated at different pion masses in the range of 350 MeV - 520MeV with a lattice volume of 243Г—48superscript24348243times 48. For the chiral extrapolation to be meaningful, all quark masses should be degenerate. Therefore, we use Nf=4subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘“4Nf=4 ensembles generated by the Extended Twisted Mass collaboration (ETMC) that are dedicated to the renormalization program. These ensembles have the same lattice spacing and action parameters as the Nf=2+1+1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘“211Nf=2+1+1 ensemble used for the production of the nucleon matrix elements. ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma depends on the RI renormalization scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, and it will be converted to MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS and evolved at a scale of choice. To reliably perform this procedure we use several values of Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, and the conversion and evolution formulas is applied on ZО“subscriptрқ‘ҚО“ZGamma obtained at each scale. We choose the initial scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0 such that discretization effects are smallcitebib.bib40. In particular, the 4-vector momentum pрқ‘қp, which is set equal to Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, has the same spatial components: p=(p0,p1,p1,p1)рқ‘қsubscriptрқ‘қ0subscriptрқ‘қ1subscriptрқ‘қ1subscriptрқ‘қ1p=(p0,p1,p1,p1). The values of p0subscriptрқ‘қ0p0 and p1subscriptрқ‘қ1p1 are chosen such that the ratio p4(p2)2superscriptрқ‘қ4superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘қ22fracp4(p2)2 is less than 0.28, as suggested in Ref. citebib.bib44. The values of aвҖӢОј0рқ‘ҺsubscriptрқңҮ0a,mu0 cover the range [1,5]15[1,5]. For each Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0 value, we apply a chiral extrapolation using the fit ZО“RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0,mПҖ)=ZО“,0RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0)+mПҖ2вҖӢZО“,1RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0),subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0subscriptрқ‘ҡрқңӢsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“0рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқңӢ2subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“1рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Zrm RIGamma(z,mu0,mpi)=Zrm RIGamma,0(z,mu0)+mpi2,Zrm RIGamma,1(z,mu0),, to extract the mass-independent ZО“,0RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“0рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Zrm RIGamma,0(z,mu0) at each value of the initial scale. ZО“,0RIвҖӢ(z,Ој0)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚRIО“0рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮ0Zrm RIGamma,0(z,mu0) is converted to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme and evolved to Ој=2рқңҮ2mu=2 GeV (Ој=2рқңҮ2mu=sqrt2 GeV) using the results of Ref. citebib.bib42 for the unpolarized and helicity (transversity) PDFs. The conversion and evolution depends on both the initial scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0 and the final scale in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS. The appropriate expressions have been obtained to one-loop perturbation theory in dimensional regularization. Therefore, there is residual dependence on the initial scale Ој0subscriptрқңҮ0mu0, which is eliminated by taking the limit (aвҖӢОј0)2вҶ’0вҶ’superscriptрқ‘ҺsubscriptрқңҮ020(a,mu0)2to 0 using a linear fit on the data in the region (aвҖӢОј0)2вҲҲ[1вҲ’2.6]superscriptрқ‘ҺsubscriptрқңҮ02delimited-12.6(a,mu0)2in[1-2.6]. The last step of the renormalization program is the conversion to a modified MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scheme (MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS), developed in Ref. citebib.bib41, and is given by рқ’өО“,0MвҖӢMSВҜвҖӢ(z,ОјВҜ)=рқ’өО“,0MSВҜвҖӢ(z,ОјВҜ)вҖӢрқ’һMSВҜ,MвҖӢMSВҜ,subscriptsuperscriptрқ’өMВҜMSО“0рқ‘§ВҜрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ’өВҜMSО“0рқ‘§ВҜрқңҮsuperscriptрқ’һВҜMSMВҜMSdisplaystylecal ZtextMoverlinetextMSGamma,0(z,barmu)=cal ZoverlinetextMSGamma,0(z,barmu),cal CoverlinetextMS,rm MoverlineMS,, where рқ’һО“MSВҜ,MвҖӢMSВҜ=1+CFвҖӢg216вҖӢПҖ2[displaystylecal CGammaoverlinerm MS,rm MoverlineMS=1+fracCFg216pi2Bigg[ eО“+eО“вҖӢlnвҒЎ(ОјВҜ24вҖӢОјF2)+eО“вҖӢ(iвҖӢПҖвҖӢ|ОјFвҖӢz|2вҖӢОјFвҖӢzвҲ’lnвҒЎ(|ОјFвҖӢz|)вҲ’CiвҖӢ(ОјFвҖӢz)вҲ’iвҖӢSiвҖӢ(ОјFвҖӢz)+lnвҒЎ(ОјFвҖӢz))subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’1О“subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’2О“superscriptВҜрқңҮ24superscriptsubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№2subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’3О“рқ‘–рқңӢsubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§2subscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§CisubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§рқ‘–SisubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§subscriptрқңҮрқҗ№рқ‘§displaystyle eGamma+eGammalnleft(fracbarmu24muF2right)+eGammaleft(fracipileft|muFzright|2muFz-ln(left|muFzright|)-textCi(muFz)-itextSi(muFz)+ln(muFz)right) This scheme was introduced to satisfy particle number conservation. In Ref. citebib.bib41 we showed that the difference between MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS and MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS is numerically very small, but brings the PDFs closer to the phenomenological ones. In Eq. ОјFsubscriptрқңҮрқҗ№muF is the factorization scale set equal to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scale. CiCirm Ci, SiSirm Si, EiEirm Ei and sgnsgnrm sgn are the special functions cosine integral, sine integral, exponential integral, and sign function, respectively. The coefficients eО“(i)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–О“e(i)Gamma depend on the operator: eО“,eО“,eО“,eО“superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“1superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“3superscriptsubscriptрқ‘’О“4eGamma,,eGamma,,eGamma,,eGamma is вҲ’5,вҲ’3,+3,вҲ’3/253332-5,,-3,,+3,,-3/2, вҲ’7,вҲ’3,+3,вҲ’3/273332-7,,-3,,+3,,-3/2, вҲ’4,вҲ’4,+4,вҲ’4/244442-4,,-4,,+4,,-4/2, for the vector, axial and tensor operator, respectively. Due to the presence of the Wilson line, both the matrix elements and renormalization functions are complex functions. As a consequence, a complex multiplication is required to extract the renormalized matrix element, that is hО“ОҘ=ZО“MвҖӢMSВҜвӢ…(hО“ОҘ)baresuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘвӢ…subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚMВҜMSО“superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘbaredisplaystyle hGammaUpupsilon=ZtextMoverlinetextMSGammacdotleft(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare =displaystyle= (ReвҖӢ[ZО“MвҖӢMSВҜ]вҖӢReвҖӢ[(hО“ОҘ)bare]вҲ’ImвҖӢ[ZО“MвҖӢMSВҜ]вҖӢImвҖӢ[(hО“ОҘ)bare])Redelimited-subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚMВҜMSО“Redelimited-superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘbareImdelimited-subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҚMВҜMSО“Imdelimited-superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘbaredisplaystyle,left(rm Re[ZtextMoverlinetextMSGamma],rm Re[left(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare]-rm Im[ZtextMoverlinetextMSGamma],rm Im[left(hGammaUpupsilonright)rm bare]right) For simplicity in the notation, the dependence on zрқ‘§z, P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3, scheme and scale is implied. As can be seen, the real (imaginary) part of the renormalized matrix elements are not simple multiples of the real (imaginary) part of the bare matrix element. Therefore, controlling systematic uncertainties in the renormalization is an important aspect of the calculation.
content_sections.II Methodology II.3 Quasi-PDFs and match: Quasi-PDFs are defined as the Fourier transform of the renormalized nucleon matrix elements in Eq. with respect to the Wilson line length zрқ‘§z qвҖӢ(x,P3)=вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢz4вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢzвҖӢP3вҖӢhО“ОҘвҖӢ(z,P3). рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқ‘§4рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3tildeq(x,P3)=intlimits-inftyinftyfracdz4pie-ixzP3,hGammaUpupsilon(z,P3),. Note that the renormalized matrix elements, hО“ОҘsuperscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘhGammaUpupsilon, depend on the scheme and scale, which also propagates to qвҖӢ(x,P3)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3tildeq(x,P3). For simplicity in the notation, this dependence is implied. As mentioned in the previous paragraph, the matrix elements are renormalized in the MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS scheme and evolved to 2GeV (22sqrt2 GeV) for the unpolarized and helicity (transversity) PDFs. On the lattice, we can only evaluate the matrix elements for discrete and finite values of zрқ‘§z. Therefore, the integral of Eq. is replaced by a discrete sum over a finite number of Wilson line lengths qвҖӢ(x,P3)=вҲ‘вҲ’zmaxzmaxdвҖӢz4вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢzвҖӢP3вҖӢhО“ОҘвҖӢ(z,P3),рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptsubscriptрқ‘§maxsubscriptрқ‘§maxрқ‘‘рқ‘§4рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3superscriptsubscriptв„ҺО“ОҘрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3tildeq(x,P3)=sumlimits-zrm maxzrm maxfracdz4pie-ixzP3,hGammaUpupsilon(z,P3),, where dвҖӢz/a=1рқ‘‘рқ‘§рқ‘Һ1dz/a=1. For the summation in Eq. to accurately reproduce Eq. , both the real and imaginary parts of the matrix element should be zero beyond zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max. Practically, this is not always possible due to the finite momentum boost and limited volume in the lattice formulation. The choice of the cutoff zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max, which is anyway limited up to L/2рқҗҝ2L/2, requires an extensive study. We note that systematic effects related to the reconstruction of the PDFs is operator dependent, as each matrix element may have different large-zрқ‘§z behavior. We will show the results of such analysis in Sec. . From the finite-momentum quasi-PDF, it is possible to obtain the light-cone parton distribution function (infinite momentum) through the so-called matching procedure. This is accomplished through a convolution of the quasi distribution with a kernel evaluated in continuum perturbation theory within the large momentum effective theory (LaMET)citebib.bib45, bib.bib33. The matching formula reads qвҖӢ(x,Ој)=вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢОҫ|Оҫ|вҖӢCвҖӢ(Оҫ,ОјxвҖӢP3)вҖӢqвҖӢ(xОҫ,Ој,P3),рқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқңүрқңүрқҗ¶рқңүрқңҮрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ғ3рқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңүрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғ3q(x,mu)=int-inftyinftyfracdxi|xi|,Cleft(xi,fracmuxP3right),widetildeqleft(fracxxi,mu,P3right),, and the factorization scale ОјрқңҮmu is chosen to be the same as the renormalization scale. The matching kernel Cрқҗ¶C contains information on P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 which, in principle, is eliminated in qвҖӢ(x,Ој)рқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮq(x,mu). However, there is residual P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 due to the limitations in accessing large values of momentum from lattice QCD (see, e. g. , Ref. citebib.bib41) and the matching kernel being available to limited order in perturbation theory. Most of the calculations of the matching kernel have been performed to one-loop level (see, e. g. , Refs. citebib.bib46, bib.bib30, bib.bib47, bib.bib48, bib.bib49, bib.bib50, bib.bib51, bib.bib52). Recently, the computation of the kernel Cрқҗ¶C was extended to two loopscitebib.bib52, bib.bib53, bib.bib54, bib.bib55. In this study, we employ the kernel in the MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS-scheme which is known at one-loop levelcitebib.bib41. This matching kernel relates the quasi-PDFs defined in the MвҖӢMSВҜMВҜMStextMoverlinetextMS scheme at some scale, to the light-cone PDFs in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS at the same scale. For the unpolarized and helicity we choose a scale of 2 GeV, while for the transversity we choose 22sqrt2 GeV. To calculate the anti-quark distributions from qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙq(x), we exploit the crossing relationscitebib.bib56, that is qВҜfвҖӢ(x)=вҲ’qfвҖӢ(вҲ’x),О”вҖӢqВҜfвҖӢ(x)=О”вҖӢqfвҖӢ(вҲ’x),ОҙвҖӢqВҜfвҖӢ(x)=вҲ’ОҙвҖӢqfвҖӢ(вҲ’x). formulae-sequencesuperscriptВҜрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙformulae-sequenceО”superscriptВҜрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘ҘО”superscriptрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘ҘрқӣҝsuperscriptВҜрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙрқӣҝsuperscriptрқ‘һрқ‘“рқ‘Ҙbarqf(x)=-qf(-x),;;;Deltabarqf(x)=Delta qf(-x),;;;deltabarqf(x)=-delta qf(-x),.

In addition, we use APE smearing for the gauge links that enter the Gaussian smearing. The optimal parameters for the Gaussian and APE smearing techniques, determined in Ref. citebib.bib67, are (ОұG,NG)=(4.0,50)subscriptрқӣјGsubscriptрқ‘ҒG4.050(alpharm G,Nrm G)=(4.0,50) and (ОұAPE,NAPE)=(0.5,50)subscriptрқӣјAPEsubscriptрқ‘ҒAPE0.550(alpharm APE,Nrm APE)=(0.5,50), respectively. Stochastic techniques employ noise sources ОҫrвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ҙxir(x) that obey two properties 1NrвҖӢвҲ‘rОҫrвҖӢ(x)=0+рқ’ӘвҖӢ(1Nr)1NrвҖӢвҲ‘rОҫrвҖӢ(x)вҠ—ОҫrвҲ—вҖӢ(y)=ОҙвҖӢ(x,y)вҖӢОҙОұвҖӢОІвҖӢОҙaвҖӢb+рқ’ӘвҖӢ(1Nr),1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ҹsubscriptрқ‘ҹsubscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ҙ0рқ’Ә1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ҹ1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ҹsubscriptрқ‘ҹtensor-productsubscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ҙsubscriptsuperscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ұрқӣҝрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰsubscriptрқӣҝрқӣјрқӣҪsubscriptрқӣҝрқ‘Һрқ‘Ҹрқ’Ә1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ҹbeginsplitfrac1Nrsumlimitsrxir(x)=0+Oleft(frac1sqrtNrright) frac1Nrsumlimitsrxir(x)otimesxi*r(y)=delta(x,y)deltaalphabetadeltaab+Oleft(frac1sqrtNrright),,endsplit where the product between source vector has to be intended as a tensor product in volume, spin and color subspaces. Given the set of vectors ОҫrвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ҙxir(x), the all-to-all propagator can be constructed by solving the equation MвҖӢ(x,y)вҖӢП•rвҖӢ(y)=ОҫrвҖӢ(x),рқ‘Җрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұsubscriptitalic-П•рқ‘ҹрқ‘Ұsubscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹрқ‘ҘM(x,y)phir(y)=xir(x),, MвҖӢ(x,y)рқ‘Җрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ҰM(x,y) being the Dirac twisted-mass operator. Having the set of solutions П•rвҖӢ(x)subscriptitalic-П•рқ‘ҹрқ‘Ҙphir(x), the all-to-all propagator can be estimated via рқ’ўвҖӢ(x;y)=1NrвҖӢвҲ‘rП•rвҖӢ(x)вҖӢОҫrвҖ вҖӢ(y)+рқ’ӘвҖӢ(1Nr). рқ’ўрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұ1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ҹsubscriptрқ‘ҹsubscriptitalic-П•рқ‘ҹрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptsubscriptрқңүрқ‘ҹвҖ рқ‘Ұрқ’Ә1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ҹG(x;y)=frac1Nrsumlimitsrphir(x)xirdagger(y)+Oleft(frac1sqrtNrright),. The number of stochastic vectors required so that the stochastic error becomes comparable to the gauge error should be much smaller than calculating the all-to-all propagator exactly. In addition, exploiting a property of the twisted mass operator, it is possible to design a stochastic algorithm that further reduce the computational cost. Recalling the transformation of Eq. , the insertion operator О“О“Gamma in twisted basis reads О“tmвүЎeiвҖӢОұ2вҖӢОі5вҖӢП„3вҖӢО“вҖӢeiвҖӢОұ2вҖӢОі5вҖӢП„3,superscriptО“tmsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқӣј2superscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқңҸ3О“superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқӣј2superscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқңҸ3Gammarm tmequiv eifracalpha2gamma5tau3Gamma eifracalpha2gamma5tau3,, with Оұ=ПҖ/2рқӣјрқңӢ2alpha=pi/2 at maximal-twist. Depending on the operator О“О“Gamma it is possible to exploit two properties of the twisted-mass operator to evaluate the loop of Eq. with a stochastic techniquecitebib.bib72, bib.bib73: 1. If [О“tm,Оі5]=0superscriptО“tmsuperscriptрқӣҫ50[Gammarm tm,gamma5]=0 for a particular О“О“Gamma, then П„3superscriptрқңҸ3tau3 appears in the loop of Eq. when expressed in the twisted basis в„’u+dвҖӢ(П„;z;О“)=в„’tmu+dвҖӢ(П„;z;iвҖӢОі5вҖӢП„3вҖӢО“)=вҲ‘yвҶ’TrвҒЎ[(рқ’ўutmвҖӢ(y;y+z)вҲ’рқ’ўdtmвҖӢ(y;y+z))вҖӢiвҖӢОі5вҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(z,0)]. superscriptв„’рқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқңҸрқ‘§О“subscriptsuperscriptв„’рқ‘ўрқ‘‘tmрқңҸрқ‘§рқ‘–superscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқңҸ3О“subscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҰTrsuperscriptsubscriptрқ’ўрқ‘ўtmрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptрқ’ўtmрқ‘‘рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұрқ‘§рқ‘–superscriptрқӣҫ5О“рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§0beginsplitLu+d(tau;z;Gamma)=Lu+drm tm(tau;z;igamma5tau3Gamma) =sumlimitsvecyoperatornameTrleft[left(Gurm tm(y;y+z)-Grm tmd(y;y+z)right)igamma5Gamma W(z,0)right],. endsplit In this case, we apply the standard one-end trick, that exploit the following property of the twisted mass operator рқ’ўutmвҲ’рқ’ўdtm=вҲ’2вҖӢiвҖӢОјвҖӢ(в„іuвҖ вҖӢв„іu)вҲ’1вҖӢОі5. superscriptsubscriptрқ’ўрқ‘ўtmsuperscriptsubscriptрқ’ўрқ‘‘tm2рқ‘–рқңҮsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ірқ‘ўвҖ subscriptв„ірқ‘ў1superscriptрқӣҫ5Gurm tm-Gdrm tm=-2imu(MudaggerMu)-1gamma5,. The transversity operator О“=Пғ3вҖӢjО“subscriptрқңҺ3рқ‘—Gamma=sigma3j belongs in this category. 2. If О“tm,Оі5=0superscriptО“tmsuperscriptрқӣҫ50Gammarm tm,gamma5=0, then the loop in twisted basis possess the same analytical form as in the physical basis в„’u+dвҖӢ(П„;z;О“)=в„’tmu+dвҖӢ(П„;z;О“)=вҲ‘yвҶ’TrвҒЎ[(рқ’ўutmвҖӢ(y;y+z)+рқ’ўdtmвҖӢ(y;y+z))вҖӢО“вҖӢWвҖӢ(z,0)]. superscriptв„’рқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқңҸрқ‘§О“subscriptsuperscriptв„’рқ‘ўрқ‘‘tmрқңҸрқ‘§О“subscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҰTrsuperscriptsubscriptрқ’ўрқ‘ўtmрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptрқ’ўtmрқ‘‘рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұрқ‘§О“рқ‘Ҡрқ‘§0beginsplitLu+d(tau;z;Gamma)=Lu+drm tm(tau;z;Gamma) =sumlimitsvecyoperatornameTrleft[left(Gurm tm(y;y+z)+Grm tmd(y;y+z)right)Gamma W(z,0)right],. endsplit The quantity of interest can be computed with the generalized one-end trick, exploiting the following property рқ’ўutm+рқ’ўdtm=2вҖӢОі5вҖӢDWвҖӢ(в„іuвҖ вҖӢв„іu)вҲ’1вҖӢОі5,superscriptsubscriptрқ’ўрқ‘ўtmsuperscriptsubscriptрқ’ўрқ‘‘tm2superscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptрқҗ·рқ‘Ҡsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscriptв„ірқ‘ўвҖ subscriptв„ірқ‘ў1superscriptрқӣҫ5Gurm tm+Gdrm tm=2gamma5DW(MudaggerMu)-1gamma5,, where DWsubscriptрқҗ·рқ‘ҠDW is the massless Wilson-Dirac clover operator. Note that if the twisted mass parameter becomes very small (close to the physical point) this type of one-end trick is approaching the standard definition in Eq. . The helicity operator О“=Оі5вҖӢОі3О“superscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқӣҫ3Gamma=gamma5gamma3 and the unpolarized О“=Оі0О“superscriptрқӣҫ0Gamma=gamma0 belong to this category. One of the technical aspects of the calculation is the evaluation of the traces in Eqs. - . With small quark masses, the contribution to the loops coming from the low modes of the spectrum of the Dirac operator may be sizeable, and contributes significantly to the stochastic noisecitebib.bib74. Therefore, we compute the first NeвҖӢv=200subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘’рқ‘Ј200Nev=200 eigen-pairs О»j,|vjвҹ©subscriptрқңҶрқ‘—ketsubscriptрқ‘Јрқ‘—lambdaj,ketvj of the squared Dirac operator в„іuвҖӢв„іuвҖ subscriptв„ірқ‘ўsuperscriptsubscriptв„ірқ‘ўвҖ MuMudagger, that allow to reconstruct exactly the low-mode contribution to the disconnected quark loops. At this stage, stochastic techniques can be employed with the deflated operator to evaluate the high-modes contribution to the traces. To reduce the stochastic noise, we use the hierarchical probing algorithmcitebib.bib25, that allows to reduce the contamination to the trace coming from off-diagonal terms up to a distance 2ksuperscript2рқ‘ҳ2k. This improvement is achieved by partitioning the lattice with 2dвҖӢ(kвҲ’1)+1superscript2рқ‘‘рқ‘ҳ112d(k-1)+1 Hadamard vectors, where d=4рқ‘‘4d=4 is the number of dimensions of the lattice. Finally, to remove the contamination from off-diagonal terms in spin-color subspaces, we apply full dilutioncitebib.bib75. The algorithm employed in the present work has been successfully used in other studies involving the evaluation of disconnected contributionscitebib.bib18, bib.bib20, bib.bib19, bib.bib17. For each value of the proton boost P3=0.41,0.83,1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ30.410.831.24GeVP3=0.41,0.83,1.24,operatornamerm GeV, we evaluated the two-point functions contributing to the disconnected diagram of Eq. with NsвҖӢrвҖӢcвҖӢs=200subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘ рқ‘ҹрқ‘җрқ‘ 200Nsrcs=200 source positions (see Sec. ). In addition, apart from averaging over all possible directions and orientations of the nucleon boost, we also average over forward and backward projections. In Table, we report the total statistics collected for the disconnected three point correlators. We note that, for the second largest value of the momentum used, namely P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,operatornamerm GeV we use вүҲ106absentsuperscript106approx 106 measurements. In addition, we also compute the matrix elements for P3=1.65вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.65GeVP3=1.65,operatornamerm GeV and all О“О“Gamma using approximately the same statistics as the previous smaller boost. While this number of statistics is not sufficient to obtain the same statistical accuracy as lower momenta, it allows us to check whether convergence with P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 is reached.
content_sections.III Lattice setup III.2 Two-point functi: The two-point functions enter the calculation through the ratio of Eq. , but also contribute to the evaluation of the disconnected diagram, as shown in Eq. . For this reason, to obtain a significant amount of measurements for the disconnected contributions we compute the two-point functions with a large number of source positions, Nsrc2вҖӢpвҖӢt=200superscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ғsrc2pt200Nrm srcrm 2pt=200, and consider boosts of the nucleon along the different spatial directions and orientations. This procedure allowed us to considerably reduce the statistical error in the disconnected contributions at small computational cost because the same loops is combined with all 200 two-point functions on the same configurations. Given that the computational cost of the two-points function is considerably lower compared to the one required to evaluate the disconnected quark loops, using multiple source positions is highly beneficial. In Table we report the number of measurements of the two-point function performed at each nucleon boost P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3. The two-point function can be written as C2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t,0)=вҲ‘n|вҹЁО©|NвҖӢ(0вҶ’,0)|nвҹ©|2вҖӢeвҲ’tвҖӢEnвҖӢ(P),subscriptрқҗ¶2рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғрқ‘Ў0subscriptрқ‘ӣsuperscriptquantum-operator-productО©рқ‘ҒвҶ’00рқ‘ӣ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘Ўsubscriptрқҗёрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғC2pt(vecP;t,0)=sumlimitsn|braketOmegaN(vec0,0)n|2e-tEn(P),, with |nвҹ©ketрқ‘ӣketn being the nthsuperscriptрқ‘ӣthnrm th energy state of the interpolator NОұвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘Ғрқӣјрқ‘ҘNalpha(x) and EnвҖӢ(P)subscriptрқҗёрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғEn(P) its energy. We performed the analysis by keeping up to two terms in the expansion of Eq. . In particular, the two-state fit function of the two-point correlator consists of C2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t,0)=c0вҖӢeвҲ’tвҖӢE0+c1вҖӢeвҲ’tвҖӢE1=c0вҖӢeвҲ’tвҖӢE0вҖӢ(1+c1c0вҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢt),subscriptрқҗ¶2рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғрқ‘Ў0subscriptрқ‘җ0superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘Ўsubscriptрқҗё0subscriptрқ‘җ1superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘Ўsubscriptрқҗё1subscriptрқ‘җ0superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘Ўsubscriptрқҗё01subscriptрқ‘җ1subscriptрқ‘җ0superscriptрқ‘’О”рқҗёрқ‘ЎbeginsplitC2pt(vecP;t,0)=c0e-tE0+c1e-tE1 =c0e-tE0left(1+fracc1c0e-Delta Etright),,endsplit while the effective energy reads EEffвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t,0)вүЎlogвҒЎ(C2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t,0)C2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t+1,0))=E0+logвҒЎ(1+BвҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢt1+BвҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢ(t+1)),subscriptрқҗёEffвҶ’рқ‘ғрқ‘Ў0subscriptрқҗ¶2рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғрқ‘Ў0subscriptрқҗ¶2рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғрқ‘Ў10subscriptрқҗё01рқҗөsuperscriptрқ‘’О”рқҗёрқ‘Ў1рқҗөsuperscriptрқ‘’О”рқҗёрқ‘Ў1Erm Eff(vecP;t,0)equivlogleft(fracC2pt(vecP;t,0)C2pt(vecP;t+1,0)right)=E0+logleft(frac1+Be-Delta Et1+Be-Delta E(t+1)right),, with О”вҖӢE=(E1вҲ’E0)О”рқҗёsubscriptрқҗё1subscriptрқҗё0Delta E=(E1-E0) and B=c1/c0рқҗөsubscriptрқ‘җ1subscriptрқ‘җ0B=c1/c0. In Fig. , we show the results of the two-state fits of the correlator and the effective energy for the parameters E0,О”вҖӢE,c0subscriptрқҗё0О”рқҗёsubscriptрқ‘җ0E0,,Delta E,,c0 and c1/c0subscriptрқ‘җ1subscriptрқ‘җ0c1/c0, varying the low-end of the fit interval tminsubscriptрқ‘Ўmintrm min. The results show that the fits on the correlator or the effective energy lead to the same ground state energy. Furthermore, the plateau and two-state fits converge at ts/a=9subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ9ts/a=9 for momentum 1.24 GeV. In Table we report the parameters extracted using one- and two-state fits. The results for E0subscriptрқҗё0E0 are obtained with the plateau fit of the effective energy, and they are compatible with the values extracted using two-state fit results. In Fig. we show the effective energy for the second largest momentum P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,operatornamerm GeV, together with the plateau fit and two-state fit results. By iterating the fit procedure described above over the data for the different nucleon boosts, we reconstructed the dispersion relation a2вҖӢE2=a2вҖӢmN2вҖӢc4+a2вҖӢPвҶ’2вҖӢc2,superscriptрқ‘Һ2superscriptрқҗё2superscriptрқ‘Һ2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқ‘Ғ2superscriptрқ‘җ4superscriptрқ‘Һ2superscriptвҶ’рқ‘ғ2superscriptрқ‘җ2a2E2=a2mN2c4+a2vecP2c2, with mNsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқ‘ҒmN being the nucleon mass. In Fig. we show the observed trend of the energy with the nucleon boost P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 together with a linear fit performed with the function of Eq. , giving a2вҖӢmN2вҖӢc4=0.2678вҖӢsuperscriptрқ‘Һ2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҡрқ‘Ғ2superscriptрқ‘җ40.26788a2mN2c4=0.2678 and c2=1.003вҖӢsuperscriptрқ‘җ21.0037c2=1.003. As can be seen, the lattice data are fully compatible with the dispersion relation for all values of P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3.
content_sections.IV Disconnected matrix elements: Obtaining the disconnected contributions to the up-, down- and strange-quark PDFs is the central goal, and most laborious aspect of this work. We use the techniques outlined in Section to extract the matrix elements and study systematic uncertainties, such as excited-states contamination.
content_sections.IV Disconnected matrix elements IV.1 Exc: To extract reliably the ground-state contribution to the matrix elements, we evaluate the ratio between the three- and two-point functions at seven source-sink separations, ranging from ts=0.563вҖӢfmsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0.563fmts=0.563,rm fm to ts=1.126вҖӢfmsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 1.126fmts=1.126,rm fm in steps of a=0.0938вҖӢfmрқ‘Һ0.0938fma=0.0938,operatornamerm fm. For disconnected contributions, the evaluation of different source-sink separations does not require new inversions. This allowed us to study the excited-states contamination to the matrix elements using several tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts values and three analysis methods: plateau fit, two-state fit and summation method. We briefly summarize these methods. 1. Two-state fit. In Sec. , the two-point correlator is expanded up to the first excited state. Likewise, we can expand the three-point correlator keeping terms up to the first excited state. This gives four terms, that is C3вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;ts,П„)=рқ’ң0,0вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢeвҲ’E0вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢts+рқ’ң0,1вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢeвҲ’E0вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢtsвҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢП„+рқ’ң1,0вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢeвҲ’E1вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢtsвҖӢeО”вҖӢEвҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢП„+рқ’ң1,1вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢeвҲ’E1вҖӢ(PвҶ’)вҖӢts. subscriptрқҗ¶3рқ‘қрқ‘ЎвҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқңҸsubscriptрқ’ң00вҶ’рқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’subscriptрқҗё0вҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ subscriptрқ’ң01вҶ’рқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’subscriptрқҗё0вҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ superscriptрқ‘’О”рқҗёвҶ’рқ‘ғрқңҸsubscriptрқ’ң10вҶ’рқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’subscriptрқҗё1вҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ superscriptрқ‘’О”рқҗёвҶ’рқ‘ғрқңҸsubscriptрқ’ң11вҶ’рқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’subscriptрқҗё1вҶ’рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ beginsplitC3pt(vecP;ts,tau)=A0,0(vecP)e-E0(vecP)ts +A0,1(vecP)e-E0(vecP)tse-Delta E(vecP)tau +A1,0(vecP)e-E1(vecP)tseDelta E(vecP)tau +A1,1(vecP)e-E1(vecP)ts,. endsplit Being interested in the forward kinematic limit allows one to reduce the number of independent parameters, since рқ’ң0,1=рқ’ң1,0subscriptрқ’ң01subscriptрқ’ң10A0,1=A1,0. We performed a fit of the ratio of Eq. with the function вҹЁC3вҖӢpвҖӢt(PвҶ’;t;ts,0вҹ©вҹЁC2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t,0)вҹ©=рқ’ң0,0c0вҖӢ[1+(рқ’ң0,1/рқ’ң0,0)вҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢП„+(рқ’ң0,1/рқ’ң0,0)вҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢ(tsвҲ’П„)+(рқ’ң1,1/рқ’ң0,0)вҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢts][1+c1c0вҖӢeвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢts],fracbraketC3pt(vecP;t;ts,0braketC2pt(vecP;t,0)=fracA0,0c0fracleft[1+left(A0,1/A0,0right)e-Delta Etau+left(A0,1/A0,0right)e-Delta E(ts-tau)+left(A1,1/A0,0right)e-Delta Etsright]left[1+fracc1c0e-Delta Etsright],, where the parameters c1/c0subscriptрқ‘җ1subscriptрқ‘җ0c1/c0 and О”вҖӢEО”рқҗёDelta E are determined through the effective energy fit and the results are reported in Table. Thus, the parameters determined by fitting the ratio of three- and two-point functions are рқ’ң0,0/c0subscriptрқ’ң00subscriptрқ‘җ0A0,0/c0, рқ’ң0,1/рқ’ң0,0subscriptрқ’ң01subscriptрқ’ң00A0,1/A0,0 and рқ’ң1,1/рқ’ң0,0subscriptрқ’ң11subscriptрқ’ң00A1,1/A0,0. рқ’ң0,0/c0subscriptрқ’ң00subscriptрқ‘җ0A0,0/c0 corresponds to the matrix element we are interested in. Such fits are weighted by the statistical errors, and therefore the fit is driven by the most accurate data. Since the statistics for the disconnected contributions are independent of th tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts value, we repeat the two-state fits modifying, each time, the starting value of tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts entering the fit (tslowsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ lowtsrm low). The results from the two-state fit method, allows us to verify ground-state dominance by comparing the matrix elements from the individual tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts values. 2. Plateau fit. For 0вүӘП„вүӘtsmuch-less-than0рқңҸmuch-less-thansubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0lltaull ts and О”вҖӢEвҖӢtsвү«0much-greater-thanО”рқҗёsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0Delta E,tsgg 0, the first term in the ratio of Eq. dominates. Thus, the matrix elements can be extracted by performing a constant fit on the ratio of Eq. in the region defined 0вүӘП„вүӘtsmuch-less-than0рқңҸmuch-less-thansubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0lltaull ts, with large enough source-sink separation. We exclude from the fit range three points from left and right, i. e. we evaluate the weighted average in the interval П„вҲҲ[3,tsвҲ’3]рқңҸ3subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 3tauin[3,ts-3]. While the excited-states contamination decreases with tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts, at the same time the statistical uncertainty exponentially increases. For this reason, the determination of the ground state of the matrix elements with the plateau fit method is a challenging task, and the results need to be compared with other analysis techniques. 3. Summation method. Summing over the insertion time П„рқңҸtau of the ratio of the three- and two-point functions we findcitebib.bib76, bib.bib77 SвҖӢ(ts)=вҲ‘П„=2П„=tsвҲ’2вҹЁC3вҖӢpвҖӢt(PвҶ’;t;ts,0вҹ©вҹЁC2вҖӢpвҖӢtвҖӢ(PвҶ’;t,0)вҹ©=tsвҖӢA0,0c0+c+рқ’ӘвҖӢ(eвҲ’О”вҖӢEвҖӢts). S(ts)=sumlimitstau=2tau=ts-2fracbraketC3pt(vecP;t;ts,0braketC2pt(vecP;t,0)=tsfracA0,0c0+c+Oleft(e-Delta Etsright). Thus, the matrix elements corresponds with the slope of the straight line SвҖӢ(ts)рқ‘Ҷsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ S(ts), and can be measured by performing a linear regression. Using the three aforementioned approaches we analyze the excited-states effects on the matrix elements for the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs. In the next three subsections, we present the analysis of the real and imaginary parts of the matrix elements at P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,operatornamerm GeV, as a representative example.We start by discussing the analysis of the unpolarized isoscalar u+dрқ‘ўрқ‘‘u+d disconnected matrix elements. In Fig. we show the ratio of three- and two-point functions at P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,operatornamerm GeV for the unpolarized operator for z/a=3рқ‘§рқ‘Һ3z/a=3 and we compare the results obtained with the three analysis methods reported in Sec. . The real part of the matrix elements shows no substantial dependence on the source-sink separation, and the plateau fit results obtained at different tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts give all compatible results. The dependence of the two-state fit on the lowest source-sink separation tslowsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ lowtsrm low, included in the fit shows a constant trend, which is also compatible with the results obtained with the summation method. The reduced chi-square ПҮ2/d. o. f=0.96formulae-sequencesuperscriptрқң’2dof0.96chi2/rm d. o. f=0.96 suggests that the function of Eq. provides a good description of the data. To extract the matrix elements, we compute the constant correlated fit of the plateau fit results starting from tslow/a=9superscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ lowрқ‘Һ9tsrm low/a=9. In contrast to the real part, the imaginary part shows a large effect due to the excited-states contamination. However, the two-state fit is compatible with the plateau value using ts/a=11subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ11ts/a=11. Also, the results obtained at different tslowsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ lowtsrm low using the summation method are compatible with the other methods within uncertainties. As final results for the unpolarized matrix element, we report the ones from the plateau fit for ts/a=11subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ11ts/a=11, which is compatible with the results obtained with the two-state fit and summation method.The disconnected contributions to the helicity isoscalar matrix elements is purely real and exhibits a non-negligible dependence on the source-sink separation. We observe a decreasing behavior as tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts increases, which is a behavior also observed in the axial chargecitebib.bib18. In addition, the plateau fit for ts/a>10subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ10ts/a>10 are compatible with the two-state fit. Therefore, we use the plateau fit for ts/a=10subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ10ts/a=10 as our final results, so that statistical uncertainties are controlled.The ratio of the three- and two-point functions for the disconnected contributions to the isoscalar transversity matrix elements does not shows dependence on the source-sink separation for both the real and imaginary parts (see Fig. ). Thus, the matrix elements are computed from the plateau fit for ts/a=9subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ9ts/a=9, both for the real and the imaginary parts. Using the criterion adopted for selecting the final results for each PDF case we compared the extracted matrix elements in Fig. for all values of zрқ‘§z. In summary, the plateau fits are evaluated at ts/a=9, 10, 9subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ9109ts/a=9,,10,,9 (ts/a=11, 10, 9subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ11109ts/a=11,,10,,9) for the real (imaginary) part of the unpolarized, helicity and transversity, respectively. For the summation method, we employ all tssubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ ts values available, except for the imaginary part of the unpolarized and the real part of the helicity matrix elements, where ts/a=6subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ6ts/a=6 is excluded as explained above. The two-state fit is performed in the range ts/aвҲҲ[6,12]subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ612ts/ain[6,12] in all cases, except for the imaginary part of the unpolarized and transversity matrix elements, where the ts/a=6subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ6ts/a=6 value, as explained, is not included in the regression. Our conclusions for the isoscalar disconnected matrix elements apply also to the strange matrix elements, with the excited-states contamination showing similar effects.
content_sections.IV Disconnected matrix elements IV.2 Mom: As explained in Sec. , the matrix elements and the quasi-PDFs have a dependence on the nucleon boost P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3, which also enters the matching formula leading to the PDFs. An important aspect of the study is the investigation of the momentum dependence of the matrix elements, which affects the convergence to the light-cone PDFs. In Fig. , we present the results for the renormalized strange and isoscalar disconnected matrix elements as a function of the momentum boost. For the unpolarized case, the real part decreases in magnitude as the P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 increases, and becomes compatible with zero. In contrast, its imaginary part is non-zero and shows convergence for the two largest values of P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3. We find that the isoscalar disconnected matrix elements share the same qualitative behavior as the strange-quark ones. First results on the helicity distribution appeared in Ref. citebib.bib26. Here we show results with increased statistics, and with the addition of P3=1.65вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.65GeVP3=1.65,operatornamerm GeV. The matrix elements show a mild residual dependence on the momentum. The imaginary part of the renormalized matrix elements arises entirely from the complex multiplication with the renormalization function and the bare matrix elements (see Eq. ). Indeed, as mentioned already in Sec. , the disconnected contribution to the bare matrix element for the helicity distribution is purely real. The real part of the matrix elements for the transversity distribution exhibit a strong dependence on the nucleon boost, changing dramatically as we increase P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 from 0.83вҖӢGeV0.83GeV0.83,operatornamerm GeV to 1.24вҖӢGeV1.24GeV1.24,operatornamerm GeV. However, results obtained for P3=1.65вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.65GeVP3=1.65,operatornamerm GeV show agreement with those for P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,operatornamerm GeV albeit the large uncertainty. From the current results it is still unclear if convergence is reached. However, in order to fully check this would require a larger momentum and much more measurements to reach the required accuracy. This is beyond the current study and will be tested in a followup work. We thus, construct the PDFs using the results for P3=1.24subscriptрқ‘ғ31.24P3=1.24GeV. In Sec. , we comment on the region of xрқ‘Ҙx affected by the gap observed in the real part of the matrix elements as momentum increases. In contrast, the imaginary part is fully compatible with zero for the two lowest momenta, while it is slightly non-zero at large zрқ‘§z at the highest momentum.
content_sections.V Connected matrix elements: The evaluation of the connected matrix elements contributing to the three types of PDFs has been studied in our previous works. In particular, we refer the reader to the study of Ref. citebib.bib41, where several sources of systematic uncertainties were discussed in great detail. For completeness, we briefly discuss here the connected contributions which are needed for the flavor decomposition. In Fig. , we show the momentum dependence of the bare connected contributions to the isoscalar and isovector matrix elements for the three types of PDFs. In all cases, as the nucleon boost increases, the real part of the matrix elements decay to zero faster, and the magnitude for the imaginary part increases in the region z/aвүІ9less-than-or-similar-toрқ‘§рқ‘Һ9z/alesssim 9. The unpolarized matrix elements show convergence with P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 while the imaginary parts of the helicity and transversity distributions increase in magnitude. We note that in order to compute the connected contributions for a fourth larger boost would require new inversions and large number of measurements to reduce the errors sufficiently enough to check convergence. Thus for the current work we opt to use the results for P3=1.24subscriptрқ‘ғ31.24P3=1.24GeV for the connected parts since the focus of this work is the evaluation of the disconnected contributions. The uncertainty on the unpolarized distribution is smaller as compared to the other two distributions. This behavior is due to the fact that both the three- and two-point functions share the same projector, (1+Оі0)/21superscriptрқӣҫ02(1+gamma0)/2, which increases the correlation between the two quantities and, as a result, drastically decreases the noise-to-signal ratio. We note also that the transversity distribution reported here is the average over the two insertions Пғ3вҖӢjsubscriptрқңҺ3рқ‘—sigma3j with j=1,2рқ‘—12j=1,2.
content_sections.VI Nucleon charges: The nucleon charges are usually extracted from the nucleon matrix elements of local operators. This limit is obtained from the matrix elements of non-local operators at z=0рқ‘§0z=0. Since the charges are frame independent, any value of P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 may be used. Indeed, in Sec. we demonstrate that the z=0рқ‘§0z=0 have little dependence on P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 For the disconnected contributions, we have the matrix elements at P3=0subscriptрқ‘ғ30P3=0 (rest frame). For the connected contributions to the charges, we use the lowest momentum, so we control statistical uncertainties. In what follows, we will show the results obtained for the isovector uвҲ’dрқ‘ўрқ‘‘u-d, isoscalar u+dрқ‘ўрқ‘‘u+d and strange-quark vector, axial and tensor charges, gVsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘үgV, gAsubscriptрқ‘”рқҗҙgA and gTsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘ҮgT. First, we describe our results for the disconnected contributions, whose total number of measurements in the rest frame is Nmeas=66вӢ…103subscriptрқ‘ҒmeasвӢ…66superscript103Nrm meas=66cdot 103. In Fig. we show our results for the renormalized disconnected vector, axial and tensor charges. The integral over the volume (i. e. Fourier transform at zero momentum transfer) of the trace of the vector current ПҲВҜвҖӢ(x)вҖӢОі0вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(x)ВҜрқң“рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқӣҫ0рқң“рқ‘Ҙbarpsi(x)gamma0psi(x) is zero because quark and antiquark loops contributions cancel each other. Thus, the disconnected contribution to the unpolarized isoscalar and strange matrix elements in the absence of the Wilson line and at P3=0subscriptрқ‘ғ30P3=0 expected to be zero is verified and this constitutes a consistency check of our computations. Due to excited-states contamination, the disconnected isoscalar and strange vector charges gVu+dвҖӢ(disc)superscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘үuddiscgVrm u+d,(disc) and gVssuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘үрқ‘ gVs obtained with the two-state and plateau fits are not compatible with zero at small source-sink separation. In particular, the two-state fit results become compatible with zero when tlowвүҘ10subscriptрқ‘Ўlow10trm lowgeq 10. The results obtained with the summation method have the largest uncertainties and are compatible with zero. The axial charge shows the larger contamination from excited states. In particular, the plateau fit results show a decreasing trend with the tlowsubscriptрқ‘Ўlowtrm low, converging to a constant value for tlow/aвүҘ11subscriptрқ‘Ўlowрқ‘Һ11trm low/ageq 11 for the isoscalar and tlow/aвүҘ8subscriptрқ‘Ўlowрқ‘Һ8trm low/ageq 8 for the strange charges, which are selected as our final values. In contrast, both the results obtained with two-state fit and summation are constant and compatible with the selected plateau fit results. We find gAu+dвҖӢ(disc)=вҲ’0.104вҖӢ,gAs=вҲ’0.0320вҖӢ. formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқҗҙuddisc0.10410superscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқҗҙрқ‘ 0.032028gArm u+d,(disc)=-0.104,quad gAs=-0.0320,. The results on the disconnected contributions of the tensor charge show very mild excited states effects. We use the plateau value extracted by fitting the ratio to a constant for ts/aвүҘ9subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ9ts/ageq 9 for the isoscalar connected tensor charge gTu+dвҖӢ(disc)superscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘ҮuddiscgTrm u+d(disc) and for ts/aвүҘ8subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ8ts/ageq 8 for gTssuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ gTs. Our final results for these two quantities are gTu+dвҖӢ(disc)=вҲ’0.00818вҖӢ,gTs=вҲ’0.00265вҖӢ. formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үuddisc0.0081891superscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ 0.0026560gTrm u+d,(disc)=-0.00818,quad gTs=-0.00265,. We stress that despite the agreement of the strange tensor charge with the value extracted using local operatorscitebib.bib78, bib.bib12, a direct comparison is not meaningful since we are using gauge ensembles simulated with heavier than physical pion mass. It thus comes with no surprise that the disconnected isoscalar tensor charge differs from the value obtained at the physical pion mass. The connected contributions to the nuclear charges are computed for the smallest momentum P3=0.41вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ30.41GeVP3=0.41,operatornamerm GeV. Using these results we can extract the values for each quark flavor for the vector, axial an tensor charges. The details on the computation of the connected isoscalar and isovector contributions are given in Sec. . The connected contributions used to extract the charges are obtained from plateau fits with ts/a=12subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ рқ‘Һ12ts/a=12. The nucleon axial and tensor charges are given in Table. We note that for the vector charge gVsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘үgV we find results that are consistent with charge conservation.
content_sections.VII Parton distribution functions VII.1: In Fig. we show the momentum dependence of the total renormalized isoscalar and isovector matrix elements, including disconnected contributions. The renormalized matrix elements are reported as a function of zвҖӢP3рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3zP3. We renormalize and apply the matching procedure independently for the isoscalar and isovector distributions, allowing us to obtain the individual up and down quark PDFs. The source-sink separation used is ts=0.94subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 0.94ts=0.94fm for the lowest momentum and ts=1.13вҖӢfmsubscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 1.13fmts=1.13,operatornamerm fm for P3=0.83subscriptрқ‘ғ30.83P3=0.83 and 1.24вҖӢGeV1.24GeV1.24,operatornamerm GeV. From previous studies of the isovector distributions (see, e. g. Ref. citebib.bib41) and nucleon chargescitebib.bib18, we expect that excited-states contamination is more significant for the nucleon three-point correlators of the axial and tensor currents as compared to the vector. However, the statistical uncertainty is larger for these quantities and within our current errors the source-sink separation employed at the highest momentum is sufficient to suppress excited states to this level of accuracycitebib.bib41. A summary of the results for the connected matrix elements in the absence of the Wilson line are reported in Table. The momentum dependence of all the matrix elements analyzed is negligible for z=0рқ‘§0z=0 as expected For example, the isoscalar connected matrix elements for the unpolarized distribution, hu+dвҖӢ(z=0)superscriptв„Һudрқ‘§0hrm u+d(z=0), for the largest momentum differs from the others by less then 1%percent11%. The isovector unpolarized matrix elements at z=0рқ‘§0z=0 is independent of the momentum boost, and equal to 1, as expected from charge conservation. Regarding the isoscalar helicity case, we still find agreement for different P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 within uncertainties, but with larger fluctuations of the mean values, as the disconnected contribution is about вҲј17%similar-toabsentpercent17sim 17% of the connected part. We note that the О”вҖӢhuвҲ’dвҖӢ(z=0)О”superscriptв„Һрқ‘ўрқ‘‘рқ‘§0Delta hu-d(z=0) is compatible with the experimental value gAuвҲ’d=1.27641вҖӢsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘”рқҗҙud1.2764156gArm u-d=1.27641citebib.bib79. Insensitivity to the momentum boost is also observed in the transversity case.
content_sections.VII Parton distribution functions VII.2: In order to construct the x-dependence of PDFs we need to take the Fourier transform. Since the matrix elements are determined for discrete finite number of zрқ‘§z values, we study the dependence on the cutoff zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max to understand systematic effects related to the reconstruction. In particular, for all types of distributions we verify that a zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max exists such that, addition of information for z>zmaxрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘§maxz>zrm max in the Fourier transform leaves the PDF unchanged within statistical uncertainties. This value of zрқ‘§z is selected as the maximum value zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max included in the Fourier transform and, typically, the matrix elements at this value has a vanishing real part. Note that the latter is just a qualitative criterion and in practice we always check by increasing zрқ‘§z for convergence. In Fig. we show the dependence on the cutoff zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max for the isoscalar and isovector distributions at P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,rm GeV. We compare the results obtained with the discrete Fourier transform of Eq. with the results from the Bayes-Gauss-Fourier transform (BGFT)citebib.bib80. The latter is an advanced reconstruction technique based on Gaussian process regression, which allows to obtain an improved estimate of quasi-PDF for continuous values of xрқ‘Ҙx, starting from a discrete set of data obtained with lattice QCD computations. The chosen values of zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max for each quark flavor and each operator are given in Table. . From Fig. it is clear that increase of the cutoff beyond the reported values of Table, does not affect the results for the PDFs. We also find compatible of the discreet Fourier transform with the results using BGFT.
content_sections.VII Parton distribution functions VII.3: The isoscalar and isovector PDFs are extracted from the corresponding renormalized matrix elements shown in Fig. . For the isoscalar combination, we add both the connected and disconnected contributions. We plot the matrix elements against zвҖӢP3рқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ғ3zP3, which is the argument of the exponential in the Fourier transform. In all cases, we find that the matrix elements for the lowest momentum P3=0.41subscriptрқ‘ғ30.41P3=0.41 GeV do not decayed to zero for large zрқ‘§z, demonstrating, as expected, that the momentum is not large enough. By increasing the momentum to P3=1.24subscriptрқ‘ғ31.24P3=1.24GeV, the matrix elements become consistent with zero within their uncertainties. While the imaginary parts show a residual momentum dependence, the convergence must be checked at the level of the reconstructed PDF. This is due to the fact that P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 enters the matching kernel and affects the convergence. Therefore, to address the momentum convergence as we increase P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3, we show in Fig. the momentum dependence of the isoscalar and isovector PDFs. We use the standard Fourier transform, with the values of zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max given in Table, as discussed in Sec. . As can be seen in Fig. , the overall dependence on the two largest values of the momentum is relatively small. Dependence on P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 is observed in the unpolarized isoscalar PDF. In general, the PDFs for the smallest momentum, do not show convergence, and exhibit non-physical oscillations due to the presence of systematic effects in the reconstruction of the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence. However, such oscillations are suppressed for the higher values of P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3. The isoscalar and isovector helicity distributions have a similar magnitude and exhibit milder dependence on the boost as compared to the unpolarized. In particular, both isoscalar and isovector helicity distributions are consistent for P3=0.83вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ30.83GeVP3=0.83,operatornamerm GeV and P3=1.24вҖӢGeVsubscriptрқ‘ғ31.24GeVP3=1.24,operatornamerm GeV. Finally, the isoscalar and isovector transversity distributions also show nice convergence with P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3 for the two largest values. These distributions will be used for the flavor decomposition presented in Sec. together with comparison of our data with phenomenology.
content_sections.VII Parton distribution functions VII.4: Our results on the isoscalar and isovector distributions presented in Sec. allow us to extract the up and down quark contributions for the unpolarized, helicity and transversity distributions. The disconnected contributions are taken into account in all cases. We stress that the comparison with phenomenology can only be qualitative for a number of reasons: i) We use an ensemble with larger than physical pion mass. We know from previous studies that there is a non-negligible pion mass dependence on the PDFs; ii) lattice systematics, such as cut-off effects, are not taken into account; iii) the renormalization ignores mixing present in the case of the unpolarized and helicity singlet PDFs; and iv) errors are still sizable and may hide systematics, such as convergence with the boost. However, it is still interesting to compare with phenomenology keeping these caveats in mind. The results for the unpolarized PDF at the largest momentum are compared with data by NNPDF3вҖӢ.1NNPDF3.1rm NNPDF3.1citebib.bib81, while the helicity distribution is compared with JAM17citebib.bib82 and NNPDFPOL1вҖӢ.1subscriptNNPDFPOL1.1rm NNPDFrm POL1.1citebib.bib2. Finally, the quark transversity distribution obtained in this study is compared against the SIDIS datacitebib.bib83 and SIDIS data constrained by the value of tensor charge gTsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘ҮgT computed in lattice QCDcitebib.bib83. For the anti-quark region for the NNPDF3вҖӢ.1NNPDF3.1rm NNPDF3.1 data, we include the crossing relations of Eq. , such that we show the antiquark distributions in the negative-xрқ‘Ҙx region. The light-quark contributions to the unpolarized PDF show good agreement with phenomenology in the region xвүі0.2greater-than-or-equivalent-toрқ‘Ҙ0.2xgtrsim 0.2. Also, the region xвүІвҲ’0.2less-than-or-similar-toрқ‘Ҙ0.2xlesssim-0.2 both estimates are compatible with zero. Note that lattice results for the small-xрқ‘Ҙx region (|x|вүІ0.15less-than-or-similar-toрқ‘Ҙ0.15|x|lesssim 0.15) suffer from uncontrolled uncertainties due to the reconstruction of the PDFs and the values of the lattice spacing used. The case of the helicity distributions is very interesting, as it has non-negligible contribution from the disconnected diagram. Our results for the up quark helicity show similar features as the NNPDF data, but are have higher values. The down quark distribution gives compatible results both with NNPDFPOL1вҖӢ.1subscriptNNPDFPOL1.1rm NNPDFrm POL1.1 and JAM17 data for all xрқ‘Ҙx in the physical region [вҲ’1,1]11[-1,1]. The transversity distribution is the least known collinear PDF and it is not well-constrained by SIDIS data. As a result, global fits for the light quark ОҙвҖӢqвҖӢ(x)рқӣҝрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdelta q(x) carry large relative error of вүҲ50вҲ’100%absent50percent100approx 50-100% citebib.bib83. A more precise phenomenological estimate of the transversity PDFs can be obtained by constraining the distributions with the value of the tensor charge gTsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘ҮgT computed within lattice QCDcitebib.bib83. A comparison with the latter, reveals a similar agreement as for the helicity PDFs. We would like to stress that the overall qualitative agreement is very promising, as this computation is done using simulations with heavier than physical pions.The strange distributions presented here are computed using the renormalized matrix elements shown in Fig. . The values of zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max employed in the Fourier transform defining the quasi-PDF are reported in Table. The criterion adopted to select zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max is to analyze the dependence of the PDF as zmaxsubscriptрқ‘§maxzrm max is increased, as discussed in the previous section. In Fig. we show the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs. The antiquark distribution reported here takes into account the crossing relations in Eq. , showing the anti-quark distributions in the negative xрқ‘Ҙx region. Although the unpolarized PDFs extracted from the matrix element using the two largest momenta tend towards the phenomenological result, there is still some residual dependence, which points to the need to increase the momentum boost to check the independence on P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3. Due to the simultaneous suppression of the real part of the matrix elements and the enhancement of the imaginary part, sВҜвҖӢ(x)ВҜрқ‘ рқ‘Ҙbars(x) becomes symmetrical with respect to x=0рқ‘Ҙ0x=0 as the momentum boost increases. This symmetry feature is exploited in the global fits. The results for the helicity distribution are approximately symmetric in the quark and antiquark regions, and are compatible with the results from the NNPDFPOL1вҖӢ.1subscriptNNPDFPOL1.1rm NNPDFrm POL1.1citebib.bib2 and with JAM17 global fits analysis both of which have larger uncertainties. Our results, thus, provide valuable input for phenomenological studies. In fact, this is more evident for the strange transversity distribution where experimental results are lacking. We obtained results on the transversity PDF with small uncertainties that show no residual momentum dependence for the two largest momentum values. Besides the individual sвҖӢ(x)рқ‘ рқ‘Ҙs(x) and sВҜвҖӢ(x)ВҜрқ‘ рқ‘Ҙbars(x) distributions, there is also an interest on the strange-quark asymmetry. This is partly due to the fact that there is no symmetry to suggest that the two distributions have to be the same. The strange and anti-strange asymmetry has been discussed within chiral effective theorycitebib.bib85, bib.bib86, perturbative evolution of QCDcitebib.bib87, and a physical model for parton momentacitebib.bib88. Here, we study the asymmetry using our data for P3=0.41, 0.83, 1.24subscriptрқ‘ғ30.410.831.24P3=0.41,,0.83,,1.24 GeV, and the results are shown in Fig. . In contrast to the individual sвҖӢ(x)рқ‘ рқ‘Ҙs(x) and sВҜвҖӢ(x)ВҜрқ‘ рқ‘Ҙbars(x) distributions, here we find that there is no momentum dependence in the strange-quark asymmetry. We also note that the difference between sвҖӢ(x)рқ‘ рқ‘Ҙs(x) and sВҜвҖӢ(x)ВҜрқ‘ рқ‘Ҙbars(x) is a non-singlet combination and, thus, does not mix with the gluon PDFs. Focusing on the most accurate results at P3subscriptрқ‘ғ3P3, we find that the asymmetry vanishes at xвүі0.2greater-than-or-equivalent-toрқ‘Ҙ0.2xgtrsim 0.2 and is small but non negligible in the small-xрқ‘Ҙx region. This conclusion is, at present stage, qualitative, and an investigation of systematic effects is needed before drawing quantitative conclusions.
content_sections.VII Parton distribution functions VII.5: In this section, we calculate the moments вҹЁxnвҹ©expectationsuperscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣbraketxn of the three PDFs considering n=0,вҖҰ,3рқ‘ӣ0вҖҰ3n=0,. . . ,3. The nвҲ’limit-fromрқ‘ӣn-th moment of the unpolarized, helicity and transversity distributions are defined as вҹЁxnвҹ©q=вҲ«01xnвҖӢ[qвҖӢ(x)+(вҲ’1)n+1вҖӢqВҜвҖӢ(x)]вҖӢрқ‘‘x=вҲ«вҲ’11xnвҖӢqвҖӢ(x)вҖӢрқ‘‘x,вҹЁxnвҹ©О”вҖӢq=вҲ«01xnвҖӢ[О”вҖӢqвҖӢ(x)+(вҲ’1)nвҖӢО”вҖӢqВҜвҖӢ(x)]вҖӢрқ‘‘x=вҲ«вҲ’11xnвҖӢО”вҖӢqвҖӢ(x)вҖӢрқ‘‘x,вҹЁxnвҹ©ОҙвҖӢq=вҲ«01xnвҖӢ[ОҙвҖӢqвҖӢ(x)+(вҲ’1)n+1вҖӢОҙвҖӢqВҜвҖӢ(x)]вҖӢрқ‘‘x=вҲ«вҲ’11xnвҖӢОҙвҖӢqвҖӢ(x)вҖӢрқ‘‘x,formulae-sequencesubscriptexpectationsuperscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣрқ‘һsuperscriptsubscript01superscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣdelimited-рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsuperscript1рқ‘ӣ1ВҜрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdifferential-dрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptsubscript11superscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdifferential-dрқ‘Ҙsubscriptexpectationsuperscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣО”рқ‘һsuperscriptsubscript01superscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣdelimited-О”рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsuperscript1рқ‘ӣО”ВҜрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdifferential-dрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptsubscript11superscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣО”рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdifferential-dрқ‘Ҙsubscriptexpectationsuperscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣрқӣҝрқ‘һsuperscriptsubscript01superscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣdelimited-рқӣҝрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsuperscript1рқ‘ӣ1рқӣҝВҜрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdifferential-dрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptsubscript11superscriptрқ‘Ҙрқ‘ӣрқӣҝрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdifferential-dрқ‘Ҙbeginsplitbraketxnq=int01xn,left[q(x)+(-1)n+1barq(x)right],dx=int-11xn,q(x),dx, braketxnDelta q=int01xn,left[Delta q(x)+(-1)nDeltabarq(x)right],dx=int-11xn,Delta q(x),dx, braketxndelta q=int01xn,left[delta q(x)+(-1)n+1deltabarq(x)right],dx=int-11xn,delta q(x),dx,endsplit where we employed the crossing relations of Eq. , to write the moments as a function of the quark distributions only. In Table we report the results for the isovector, isoscalar and flavor diagonal moments. The zero-th moments are compatible with the nucleon charges reported in Tab. This is a non-trivial check, as the calculation of the charges follows a totally different procedure and undergoes a Fourier transform and matching.
content_sections.VIII Conclusions: In this work we present a study of the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of proton collinear quark PDFs from lattice QCD considering both connected and disconnected diagrams. These contributions are necessary to determine the individual-flavor contributions to PDFs. We present results for the up, down and strange quark for the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs. This work extends our first calculation for the flavor decomposition of the helicity PDFscitebib.bib26; here we increase the statistics by about a factor of two, and include results on the unpolarized and transversity PDFs. The main goal of this work is to explore the feasibility of the calculation of disconnected quark loops with non-local operators. To this end, we provide the necessary details on technical and theoretical aspects, as well as the examination of some sources of systematic uncertainties. The calculation is carried out using one ensemble of Nf=2+1+1subscriptрқ‘Ғрқ‘“211Nf=2+1+1 twisted mass fermions simulated with quark mass value that produces a pion mass of 260 MeV. Using a single ensemble, we can address excited-states contamination, reconstruction of the xрқ‘Ҙx dependence, and the convergence with increasing the momentum boost in the final PDFs. The matrix elements contain non-local operators with the length of the Wilson line extending up to half the spatial extend of the lattice. The proton states are boosted with momentum using three values, namely P3=0.41, 0.83, 1.24subscriptрқ‘ғ30.410.831.24P3=0.41,,0.83,,1.24 GeV. Several values of the source-sink time separation are considered. As we increase the boost we also increase the source-sink time separation in order to investigate the effect of excited states; we employ up to ts=1.13subscriptрқ‘Ўрқ‘ 1.13ts=1.13 fm for the highest momentum (see Figs. - ). Both the isovector and isoscalar flavor combinations are calculated, with the latter receiving contributions from the connected and disconnected diagrams. All matrix elements are renormalized multiplicatively using the RI scheme and evolved to the modified-MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scheme at a scale of 2 GeV (unpolarized and helicity) or 22sqrt2 GeV (transversity). The renormalization is followed by the transform to the momentum space, xрқ‘Ҙx, which produces the quasi-PDFs. We apply the standard Fourier transform that is confirmed using the Bayes-Gauss-Fourier transform, finding compatible results (see Fig. ). The quasi-PDFs are matched to the light-cone PDFs using one-loop perturbation theory. The matching kernel contains information on the renormalization scheme and scale for the quasi-PDFs (modified-MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scheme at 2 or 22sqrt2 GeV), which are then matched to the light-cone PDFs in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinetextMS scheme at the same scale. In this proof-of-principle study we neglect the mixing with the gluon PDFs for the unpolarized and helicity case. The extraction of the latter has its own challenges and will be included in our future studies. To test the influence of systematic uncertainties, we calculate the charges by integrating the PDFs (Table), and compare with the values obtained directly from the matrix elements (Table). We find good consistence among the results. We find that the light-quark disconnected contributions have the most impact for the helicity PDF, while the transversity disconnected contribution is very small. Regardless, a clear non-zero signal is found in all cases. The strange-quark PDFs are nonzero up to xвҲј0.5similar-toрқ‘Ҙ0.5xsim 0.5, with the unpolarized and helicity having a similar magnitude, and the transversity being an order of magnitude smaller, as can be seen in Fig. . These distributions are very challenging to extract from experimental data due to the lack of sensitivity to the strange-quark. In a qualitative comparison of our results with phenomelogically extracted PDFs we find: i) our results on the unpolarized have a statistical precision which is similar to the NNPDF data; ii) the helicity strange-quark PDF is significantly more accurate than the JAM and NNPDF results and iii) our results for the strange-quark transversity PDF serve as a prediction. There are a number of improvements that one can do to quantify and eliminate systematic uncertainties. These include, but not limited to, pion mass dependence, mixing under matching with the gluon PDFs for the unpolarized and helicity case, and address the inverse problem using, e. g. , Bayesian reconstruction methods. One can also address finite-volume and discretization effects, which require extracting the PDFs using multiple ensembles. However, this work clearly demonstrates the great potential in the extraction of the x-dependence of individual quark PDFs from lattice QCD. Acknowledgements. We would like to thank all members of ETMC for their constant and pleasant collaboration. We also thank Fernanda Steffens and Jeremy Green for useful discussions. M. M. is funded under this program. This research includes calculations carried out on HPC resources of the Cyprus Institute (the Cyclone and Cyclamen machines) and of Temple University, supported in part by the National Science Foundation through major research instrumentation grant number 1625061 and by the US Army Research Laboratory under contract number W911NF-16-2-0189. Computations for this work were carried out in part on facilities of the USQCD Collaboration, which are funded by the Office of Science of the U. S. Department of Energy.
