Note that ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m vanishes in ZOsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘ӮZO due to the use of DR. By Fourier transforming the above renormalized matrix element to momentum space, one can then work out the RI/MOM matching coefficient for the quasi-PDFscitebib.bib420. The one-loop matching coefficient for different spin structures has been obtained in citebib.bib420, bib.bib343, bib.bib340, and the two-loop result for the unpolarized case can be found incitebib.bib120. Alternatively, one can also first convert the RI/MOM matrix element to the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS or modified MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS schemescitebib.bib149, bib.bib18, and then do the Fourier transform and momentum-space matching.In the coordinate-space factorization, |z|вүӘОӣQCDвҲ’1much-less-thanрқ‘§subscriptsuperscriptОӣ1QCD|z|llLambda-1rm QCD must be small, whereas Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz can be of any value. In this case, the ratio scheme incitebib.bib390, bib.bib381 can be an effective choice for lattice renormalization. Consider the ratio hвҖӢ(О»,z2,a)/hвҖӢ(0,z2,a),в„ҺрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һв„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һdisplaystyletildeh(lambda,z2,a)/tildeh(0,z2,a),, where the denominator is a nonperturbative matrix element at Pz=0superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§0Pz=0. Since hвҖӢ(О»,z2,a)в„ҺрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һtildeh(lambda,z2,a) and hвҖӢ(0,z2,a)в„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һtildeh(0,z2,a) calculated from the same lattice ensemble are correlated with each other, the error in the ratio can be reduced. Besides, the ratio does not need further renormalization on the lattice, so one can directly take the continuum limit limaвҶ’0hвҖӢ(О»,z2,a)hвҖӢ(0,z2,a)=hвҖӢ(О»,z2)hвҖӢ(0,z2),subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Һ0в„ҺрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һв„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һв„ҺрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2в„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2displaystylelimato 0fractildeh(lambda,z2,a)tildeh(0,z2,a)=fractildeh(lambda,z2)tildeh(0,z2),, which has referred to as the вҖңreduced Ioffe-time pseudoвҖқ distribution incitebib.bib390, bib.bib381. In the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme, hвҖӢ(0,z2вҖӢОј2)в„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2tildeh(0,z2mu2) has a small-zрқ‘§z expansion, hвҖӢ(0,z2вҖӢОј2)=C0вҖӢ(z2вҖӢОј2)+рқ’ӘвҖӢ(z2вҖӢM2,z2вҖӢОӣQCD2),в„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2subscriptрқҗ¶0superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2рқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2displaystyletildeh(0,z2mu2)=C0(z2mu2)+cal O(z2M2,z2Lambdarm QCD2),, where the lowest moment of the iso-vector quark PDF a0subscriptрқ‘Һ0a0 is trivially one. If we ignore all the power corrections, then hвҖӢ(0,z2вҖӢОј2)в„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2tildeh(0,z2mu2) is perturbative and can be regarded as a renormalization factor. Therefore, the ratio in Eq. still satisfies a similar OPE or factorization formula to Eqs. and , except that the matching coefficient must be modified correspondinglycitebib.bib391, bib.bib247, рқ’һratioвҖӢ(Оұ,z2вҖӢОј2)=рқ’һвҖӢ(Оұ,z2вҖӢОј2)вҲ’ОҙвҖӢ(1вҲ’Оұ)вҖӢC0вҖӢ(z2вҖӢОј2). superscriptрқ’һratioрқӣјsuperscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2рқ’һрқӣјsuperscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2рқӣҝ1рқӣјsubscriptрқҗ¶0superscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2displaystylecal Crm ratio(alpha,z2mu2)=cal C(alpha,z2mu2)-delta(1-alpha)C0(z2mu2),. In other variants of the ratio scheme, it has also been suggested that one replaces hвҖӢ(0,z2,a)в„Һ0superscriptрқ‘§2рқ‘Һtildeh(0,z2,a) by the vacuum matrix element of the nonlocal Wilson line operatorcitebib.bib84, bib.bib326, as the UV divergence does not depend on the external state.Since the factorization formula for the quasi-PDF is only proven in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme, it is not legitimate to use momentum-space factorization for any other scheme that differ from MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS nonperturbatively. The RI/MOM and ratio schemes fall into this category as the conversion factors that match them to MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS includes logarithms of z2superscriptрқ‘§2z2citebib.bib149, bib.bib247, which requires running Оұssubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ alphas to the IR region when zвҲјОӣQCDвҲ’1similar-toрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD1zsimLambdarm QCD-1. In contrast, the Wilson-line mass-subtraction scheme with wavefunction renormalizations is essentially the same as MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS, so it will not introduce extra IR effects. However, the Wilson-line mass-subtraction scheme also has disadvantages. On the lattice, due to discretization effects at zвҲјasimilar-toрқ‘§рқ‘Һzsim a, the lattice scheme cannot reproduce the short-distance lnвҒЎz2superscriptрқ‘§2ln z2 behavior of the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS matrix elements of the nonlocal operator. Such discretization effects, however, are cancelled in the RI/MOM or ratio scheme. To take advantages of both types of schemes, the hybrid scheme was proposed incitebib.bib275 which provides a viable approach to renormalize the quasi-LF correlations at all zрқ‘§z. To begin with, for |z|вүӨzSрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘§S|z|leq zrm S where zSsubscriptрқ‘§Szrm S is smaller than the distance at which the leading-twist approximation in the OPE becomes unreliable, one renormalizes the quasi-LF correlation as hвҖӢ(z,a,Pz)ZXвҖӢ(z,a),в„Һрқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘Ӣрқ‘§рқ‘Һdisplaystylefractildeh(z,a,Pz)ZX(z,a),, where вҖңXрқ‘ӢXвҖқ can be the RI/MOM or ratio scheme. For |z|>zSрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘§S|z|>zrm S, one applies the Wilson-line mass subtraction hвҖӢ(z,a,Pz)вҖӢeвҲ’ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ|z|вҖӢZhybrid,в„Һрқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘Қhybriddisplaystyletildeh(z,a,Pz)e-delta m|z|Zrm hybrid,, where Zhybridsubscriptрқ‘ҚhybridZrm hybrid denotes the wavefunction and vertex renormalizations, which can be nonperturbatively determined by imposing a continuity condition at z=zSрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘§Sz=zrm S, ZhybridвҖӢeвҲ’ОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ|zS|вҖӢhвҖӢ(z,a,Pz)=hвҖӢ(z,a,Pz)ZXвҖӢ(zS,a),subscriptрқ‘Қhybridsuperscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡsubscriptрқ‘§Sв„Һрқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§в„Һрқ‘§рқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘Ӣsubscriptрқ‘§Sрқ‘Һdisplaystyle Zrm hybride-delta m|zrm S|tildeh(z,a,Pz)=fractildeh(z,a,Pz)ZX(zrm S,a),, leading to ZhybridвҖӢ(zS,a)=eОҙвҖӢmвҖӢ|zS|/ZXвҖӢ(zS,a). subscriptрқ‘Қhybridsubscriptрқ‘§Sрқ‘Һsuperscriptрқ‘’рқӣҝрқ‘ҡsubscriptрқ‘§Ssubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘Ӣsubscriptрқ‘§Sрқ‘Һdisplaystyle Zrm hybrid(zrm S,a)=edelta m|zrm S|/ZX(zrm S,a),. In this way, one only has to calculate ОҙвҖӢmрқӣҝрқ‘ҡdelta m. Note that the final result should be independent of zSsubscriptрқ‘§Szrm S, so one should try multiple values and find the optimal one around which the result changes the most slightly. The perturbative matching for the hybrid renormalized quasi-PDF can be derived accordingly. Taking ZXsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘ӢZX being the zero-momentum matrix element in the ratio scheme as an example, the OвҖӢ(Оұs)рқ‘Ӯsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ O(alphas) matching has been derived ascitebib.bib275 ChybridвҖӢ(Оҫ,Ој2/(pz)2,zS2вҖӢОј2)=CratioвҖӢ(Оҫ,Ој2/(pz)2)subscriptрқҗ¶hybridрқңүsuperscriptрқңҮ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptрқ‘§S2superscriptрқңҮ2subscriptрқҗ¶ratioрқңүsuperscriptрқңҮ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§2displaystyle Crm hybrid(xi,mu2/(pz)2,zrm S2mu2)=Crm ratio(xi,mu2/(pz)2) +ОұsвҖӢCF2вҖӢПҖвҖӢ32вҖӢ[вҲ’1|1вҲ’Оҫ|++2вҖӢSiвҖӢ((1вҲ’Оҫ)вҖӢО»S)ПҖвҖӢ(1вҲ’Оҫ)],subscriptрқӣјрқ‘ subscriptрқҗ¶рқҗ№2рқңӢ32delimited-1subscript1рқңү2Si1рқңүsubscriptрқңҶSрқңӢ1рқңүdisplaystylequad+alphasCFover 2pi3over 2left[-1over|1-xi|++frac2textSi((1-xi)lambdarm S)pi(1-xi)right],, where Cratiosubscriptрқҗ¶ratioCrm ratio can be found incitebib.bib247, Оҫ=y/xрқңүрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙxi=y/x, and О»S=zSвҖӢpzsubscriptрқңҶSsubscriptрқ‘§Ssuperscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§lambdarm S=zrm Spz with pz=xвҖӢPzsuperscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§pz=xPz being the parton momentum. The plus function is defined as 1|1вҲ’Оҫ|+1subscript1рқңүdisplaystyle1over|1-xi|+ вүЎlimОІвҶ’0+[ОёвҖӢ(|1вҲ’Оҫ|вҲ’ОІ)|1вҲ’Оҫ|+2вҖӢОҙвҖӢ(1вҲ’Оҫ)вҖӢlnвҒЎОІ]. absentsubscriptвҶ’рқӣҪsuperscript0delimited-рқңғ1рқңүрқӣҪ1рқңү2рқӣҝ1рқңүрқӣҪdisplaystyleequivlimbetato 0+left[theta(|1-xi|-beta)over|1-xi|+2delta(1-xi)lnbetaright],. Due to finite lattice volume and deteriorating signal-to-noise ratios at large zрқ‘§z, the available lattice data have to be truncated at zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L. As we have discussed in Sec. , the quasi-LF correlation has a correlation length ОҫzвҲјОӣQCDвҲ’1similar-tosubscriptрқңүрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD1xizsimLambdarm QCD-1 and exhibits an exponential decay at large zрқ‘§z (вҲј1similar-toabsent1sim 1 fm). If zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L is not sufficiently large and the quasi-LF correlation still has a considerable nonzero value, then a direct Fourier transform truncated at zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L will lead to unphysical oscillations and other systematics in the quasi-PDF. To improve this situation, it is suggested in the hybrid scheme to perform an extrapolation to zвҶ’вҲһвҶ’рқ‘§ztoinftycitebib.bib275. When Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz is not very large and the lattice matrix elements exhibit the exponential behavior near zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L, one can use the form вҲјeвҲ’z/Оҫzsimilar-toabsentsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘§subscriptрқңүрқ‘§sim e-z/xiz to do the extrapolation, although some algebraic behavior can be added on top to better reflect the zрқ‘§z-dependence. If Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz is very large, then the signal-to-noise ratio gets worse, so zLsubscriptрқ‘§Lzrm L is smaller. In this case, the quasi-LF correlation is yet to show exponential decay and dominated by the leading-twist contributions, so one can use the algebraic decay form to do the extrapolation. Since О»L=zLвҖӢPzsubscriptрқңҶLsubscriptрқ‘§Lsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§lambdarm L=zrm LPz can reach reasonably large values with contemporary computing resources, the extrapolation will only affect very small-xрқ‘Ҙx region, for which the LaMET expansion is not well under control after all. To summarize, the hybrid scheme provides a proper renormalization of the quasi-LF correlations at all zрқ‘§z, which allows for a controlled calculation of the PDF for xвҲҲ[xmin,xmax]рқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ҙminsubscriptрқ‘Ҙmaxxin[xrm min,xrm max] through LaMET expansion in momentum space. Therefore, we expect it to play a dominant role in the LaMET calculation of PDFs in the future.

Apart from the collinear PDFs, the first application of LaMET is the gluon helicity contribution О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G to the proton spincitebib.bib286. In the naive sum rule for the proton spincitebib.bib251, О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G is related to the matrix element of a nonlocal light-cone correlation operatorcitebib.bib353, О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәdisplaystyleDelta G =вҹЁPвҖӢS|iвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢxвҖӢdвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢxвҖӢP+вҖӢeiвҖӢО»вҖӢxвҖӢF+ОұвҖӢвҖӢWвҖӢ(0,О»вҖӢn)вҖӢFОұ+вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹ©,absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷрқ‘–рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶрқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқҗ№рқӣј0рқ‘Ҡ0рқңҶрқ‘ӣsuperscriptsubscriptрқҗ№рқӣјрқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷdisplaystyle!=! PSbig|i!int!fracdxdlambda2pi xP+eilambda x!F+alphaW(0,!lambda n)tildeFalpha+(lambda n)big|PS,, which in the light-cone gauge A+=0superscriptрқҗҙ0A+=0 reduces to О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәdisplaystyleDelta G =вҹЁPвҖӢS|(EвҶ’Г—AвҶ’)z|PвҖӢSвҹ©/(2вҖӢP+). absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғрқ‘ҶsuperscriptвҶ’рқҗёвҶ’рқҗҙрқ‘§рқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷ2superscriptрқ‘ғdisplaystyle= PS|big(vecEtimesvecAbig)z|PS/(2P+),. Within the LaMET framework, one can start from a static вҖңgluon spinвҖқ operator, which is defined as EвҶ’Г—AвҶ’вҶ’рқҗёвҶ’рқҗҙvecEtimesvecA fixed in a time-independent gauge which maintains the transverse polarizations of the gluon field in the IMF limit. For example, the Coulomb gauge вҲҮвҶ’вӢ…AвҶ’=0вӢ…вҶ’вҲҮвҶ’рқҗҙ0vecnablacdotvecA=0, axial gauges Az=0superscriptрқҗҙрқ‘§0Az=0 and A0=0superscriptрқҗҙ00A0=0 are viable optionscitebib.bib230. In the Coulomb gauge and MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scheme, the static вҖңgluon spinвҖқ О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDeltawidetildeG in a massive on-shell quark state at one-loop order iscitebib.bib122, bib.bib286 О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)вҖӢ(2вҖӢSz)=вҹЁPвҖӢS|ПөвҹӮiвҖӢjвҖӢFiвҖӢ0вҖӢAj|PвҖӢSвҹ©q|вҲҮвҶ’вӢ…AвҶ’=0О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§evaluated-atsubscriptquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—perpendicular-tosuperscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–0superscriptрқҗҙрқ‘—рқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷрқ‘һвӢ…вҶ’вҲҮвҶ’рқҗҙ0displaystyleDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu)(2Sz)=left. PS|epsilonijperpFi0Aj|PSqrightarrowvertvecnablacdotvecA=0 =ОұsвҖӢCF4вҖӢПҖвҖӢ[53вҖӢlnвҒЎОј2m2вҲ’19+43вҖӢlnвҒЎ(2вҖӢPz)2m2]вҖӢ(2вҖӢSz),absentsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ subscriptрқҗ¶рқҗ№4рқңӢdelimited-53superscriptрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘ҡ21943superscript2superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2superscriptрқ‘ҡ22superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyleqquadqquad=fracalphasCF4pileft[5over 3lnmu2over m2-frac19+frac43lnfrac(2Pz)2m2right](2Sz),, where the subscript qрқ‘һq denotes a quark, and SОјsuperscriptрқ‘ҶрқңҮSmu is the spin vector. The collinear divergence is regulated by the finite quark mass mрқ‘ҡm. If we follow the procedure incitebib.bib438 and take PzвҶ’вҲһвҶ’superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pztoinfty limit before UV regularizationcitebib.bib286, then О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(вҲһ,Ој)вҖӢ(2вҖӢSz)О”рқҗәрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyleDeltawidetildeG(infty,mu)(2Sz) =вҹЁPвҖӢS|ПөвҹӮiвҖӢjвҖӢFiвҖӢ0вҖӢAj|PвҖӢSвҹ©q|вҲҮвҶ’вӢ…AвҶ’=0absentevaluated-atsubscriptquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—perpendicular-tosuperscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–0superscriptрқҗҙрқ‘—рқ‘ғрқ‘Ҷрқ‘һвӢ…вҶ’вҲҮвҶ’рқҗҙ0displaystyle=left. PS|epsilonijperpFi0Aj|PSqrightarrowvertvecnablacdotvecA=0 =ОұsвҖӢCF4вҖӢПҖвҖӢ(3вҖӢlnвҒЎОј2m2+7)вҖӢ(2вҖӢSz),absentsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ subscriptрқҗ¶рқҗ№4рқңӢ3superscriptрқңҮ2superscriptрқ‘ҡ272superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyle=fracalphasCF4pileft(3lnmu2over m2+7right)(2Sz),, which is exactly the same as the light-cone gluon helicity О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)О”рқҗәрқңҮDelta G(mu)citebib.bib239. Therefore, despite the difference in the UV divergence, the collinear divergences of О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu) and О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)О”рқҗәрқңҮDelta G(mu) are exactly the same, which allows for a perturbative matching between them. The complete factorization formula that relates О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu) to О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G and О”вҖӢОЈО”ОЈDeltaSigma is О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)О”рқҗәsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyleDeltawidetildeG(Pz,mu) =ZgвҖӢgвҖӢ(Pz/Ој)вҖӢО”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)absentsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘”superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮО”рқҗәрқңҮdisplaystyle=Zgg(Pz/mu)Delta G(mu) +ZgвҖӢqвҖӢ(Pz/Ој)вҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+вҖҰ,subscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮО”ОЈрқңҮвҖҰdisplaystyleqquadqquad+Zgq(Pz/mu)DeltaSigma(mu)+. . . ,, where вҖҰвҖҰ. . . are power corrections suppressed by 1/Pz1superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§1/Pz, and the matching coefficients ZgвҖӢgsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘”Zgg and ZgвҖӢgsubscriptрқ‘Қрқ‘”рқ‘”Zgg have been calculated for the Coulomb gauge at one-loopcitebib.bib287. Besides, one can also calculate the gluon helicity PDF О”вҖӢgвҖӢ(x)О”рқ‘”рқ‘ҘDelta g(x) according to the factorization formula in Sec. , and then integrate it over xрқ‘Ҙx to obtain О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G. At leading-twist, apart from the unpolarized and helicity PDFs that we have discussed before, there is also the transversity PDF defined ascitebib.bib249, bib.bib250 h1вҖӢ(x)=12вҖӢP+вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢО»вҖӢxвҖӢвҹЁPвҖӢSвҹӮ|ПҲВҜвҖӢвҖӢОі+вҖӢОівҹӮвҖӢОі5вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹӮвҹ©. subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙ12superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶрқ‘Ҙquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toВҜрқң“0superscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫperpendicular-tosubscriptрқӣҫ5рқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toh1(x)!=!frac12P+!intfracdlambda2pie-ilambda x PSperp|overlinepsigamma+gammaperpgamma5psi(lambda n)|PSperp,. The h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) simply counts the number of transversely polarized quarks carrying the momentum fraction xрқ‘Ҙx in a transversely polarized proton. The first moment of this distribution corresponds to the so-called tensor charge ОҙвҖӢqрқӣҝрқ‘һdelta q, which is the matrix element of a chiral-odd operator. h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) can be accessed through the transverse-transverse spin asymmetry in Drell-Yan processescitebib.bib400, bib.bib249, bib.bib250 or the Collins single-spin asymmetry in SIDIS where the transversity TMDPDF couples to a chiral-odd TMD fragmentation functioncitebib.bib136. At present, experimental results on the transversity PDF are very limitedcitebib.bib45, bib.bib297, bib.bib330, bib.bib389, bib.bib101, especially for the sea quark contributionscitebib.bib113, so this is one scenario where lattice QCD calculation can make an important difference. The LaMET calculation of h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) is straightforward as the nonlocal operator has the same renormalization as the unpolarized case, and its one-loop matching has been calculated in the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS and RI/MOM schemes at one-loop ordercitebib.bib21, bib.bib340. First lattice calculations of h1вҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һ1рқ‘Ҙh1(x) have been done incitebib.bib114, bib.bib21, bib.bib340, which will be discussed with more details in Sec. .
content_sections.IV Generalized Collinear Parton Observab: In the previous section, we have extensively discussed the leading-twist collinear PDFs that characterize the 1D structure of the proton in longitudinal momentum space. There exist various other parton observables that provide complementary information. In this section, we focus on observables defined by collinear parton correlators, in the sense that only the collinear quark and gluon mode contribute, corresponding to the so-called collinear expansion in QCD factorizationscitebib.bib419, bib.bib132. We call them вҖңgeneralized collinear parton observablesвҖқ (GCPOs), and discuss their calculations through LaMET framework. For observables defined by parton correlators involving transverse separations, in particular, the TMDPDFs, Wigner functions, and LFWFs, we will consider them in the following sections. One of the important GCPOs is the GPDs introduced incitebib.bib365, and rediscoveredcitebib.bib258 from their connection to the spin structure of the proton. They describe the correlation between the transverse position and longitudinal momentum of partons inside the proton, and thus provide important information for 3D imaging of the proton. A proton spin sum rule was derived in terms of the moments of the GPDs, which has stimulated considerable general interest in the GPDs. It was also found that in the so-called zero skewness limit or when the longitudinal momentum transfer vanishes, the GPD has a probability interpretation in the impact parameter spacecitebib.bib99. In general case, it is related to the quantum phase-space distributions or Wigner functionscitebib.bib260, bib.bib54. Experimentally, the GPDs can be measured through hard exclusive processes such as deeply virtual Compton scattering (DVCS) or meson production (DVMP) that were first proposed incitebib.bib258, bib.bib257. Much effort has been devoted to measuring such processes at completed and ongoing experiments, including HERA, COMPASS and JLab. For a more comprehensive discussion on the GPDs, we refer the readers to the review articlescitebib.bib259, bib.bib253, bib.bib167, bib.bib52. Despite that the GPDs have more complicated kinematic dependence and relation to experimental observables, various fitting methods have been proposed in the literature to fit available DVCS and DVMP datacitebib.bib310, bib.bib198. In parallel, one can also extract certain information on the GPDs from lattice calculations of their momentscitebib.bib223, bib.bib211, bib.bib15, which, however, is again very limited due to the same difficulties existing in lattice calculations of the PDF moments. For JLab 12 GeV program and future EIC, it is critically important to have first-principle calculations of GPDs with much better understanding of the physical landscape in different kinematic variables. A simpler but closely related GCPO is the parton distribution amplitudes (DAs), which are collinear matrix elements of light-cone operators between a hadron state and the QCD vacuum, representing the probability amplitude of finding a given Fock state in the hadron. They can be probed in certain exclusive processescitebib.bib91, and are crucial inputs for processes relevant to measuring fundamental parameters of the Standard Model and probing new physics. There exists a vast amount of literature on this subject, particularly about the pion DA. For a review see e. g. citebib.bib92, bib.bib219, bib.bib77. Another type of GCPO is the higher-twist parton distributions. They are defined by multi-parton correlation functions, and quantify the proton structure in terms of longitudinal momentum correlationscitebib.bib252, bib.bib188, bib.bib250. Although physically interesting, they are hard to separate theoretically due to mixing with the leading-twist onescitebib.bib370, bib.bib256, and difficult to extract experimentally because they are power-suppressedcitebib.bib255. Higher-twist effects can become important in kinematic regions where the suppression is relaxed. Moreover, some twist-three distributions, gTsubscriptрқ‘”рқ‘ҮgT and hLsubscriptв„ҺрқҗҝhL, are different; they have no leading-twist to mix with and are dominant in spin-related observablescitebib.bib250. Twist-three GPDs are also relevant for studying parton OAM in the protoncitebib.bib232, bib.bib281, bib.bib152 and can be accessed through DVCS processcitebib.bib383, bib.bib301. In principle, all the GCPOs discussed above can be computed within LaMET. In addition, an accurate LaMET expansion for the leading-twist PDFs requires calculations of quasi higher-twist matrix elements. In the following, we begin with the flavor non-singlet quark GPDs and hadronic DAs for which the computational procedure has been well established, and then give some generic discussions on higher-twist distributions, followed by the discussion on power-suppressed contributions required to extract the leading-twist quark PDFs, which have been investigated using different approaches though not yet implemented in numerical computations.

The operators defining the GPDs are the same as those defining the PDFs. Thus, the LaMET calculation of PDFs can be rather straightforwardly generalized to the GPDs by taking into account the non-forward kinematicscitebib.bib342. To illustrate how it works, let us take the nonsinglet unpolarized quark GPDs in the nucleon as an example. With these kinematic constraints, the GPDs can be divided into several kinematic regions that have different physical interpretations. As shown in Fig. , in the region Оҫ<x<1вҖӢ(вҲ’1<x<вҲ’Оҫ)рқңүрқ‘Ҙ11рқ‘Ҙрқңүxi<x<1,(-1<x<-xi) the distribution describes the emission and reabsorption of a quark (antiquark), while in the region вҲ’Оҫ<x<Оҫрқңүрқ‘Ҙрқңү-xi<x<xi it represents the creation of a quark and antiquark pair. The first region is similar to that present in usual PDFs and referred to as the DGLAP region, whereas the second is similar to that in a meson DA, which will be discussed later in this section, and referred to as the Efremov-Radyushkin-Brodsky-Lepage (ERBL) region. The easiest way to see this is in light-cone quantization and light-cone gauge where the matrix element defining the GPDs can be rewritten in terms of parton creation and annihilation operators, for details see e. g. citebib.bib253. The quark GPDs defined above have a number of remarkable properties, see, e. g. ,citebib.bib259, bib.bib253, bib.bib167, bib.bib52 , which either hold or have similar counterparts for the quark quasi-GPDs to be defined below. Apart from their physical significance, these properties also serve as useful checks on calculations related to GPDs. We can replace Оҫрқңүtildexi with Оҫрқңүxi and attribute the difference to generic power suppressed contributions. The quasi-GPDs defined above can be renormalized by observing that their UV divergence depends only on the operators defining them, but not on the external states. Since OОі0вҖӢ(z)subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫ0рқ‘§Ogamma0(z) is multiplicatively renormalized, we can choose the same renormalization factor as that for the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib420, bib.bib343 to renormalize the quasi-GPD. After renormalization, the quasi-GPD can then be matched to the usual GPD through a factorization formula. The factorization of quasi-GPDs was first proposed and verified at one-loop order incitebib.bib277, bib.bib445, where a transverse momentum cutoff and a quark mass were used as the UV and IR regulator, respectively. Later on, a detailed derivation based on OPE was given incitebib.bib341. In contrast with the OPE for the quasi-PDF, a crucial difference here is that the total derivative of operators can come into play, as it simply gives momentum transfer factors when sandwiched between non-forward external states, and therefore is non-vanishing. In other words, the local twist-two operators as those in Eq. will mix under renormalization with operators with total derivatives. Both forms have been used in the literaturecitebib.bib277, bib.bib445, bib.bib341 with the matching coefficients being related by CвҖӢ(xy,Оҫy,ОјyвҖӢPВҜz)=|yОҫ|вҖӢCВҜвҖӢ(xОҫ,yОҫ,ОјОҫвҖӢPВҜz),рқҗ¶рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұрқңүрқ‘ҰрқңҮрқ‘ҰsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқ‘ҰрқңүВҜрқҗ¶рқ‘Ҙрқңүрқ‘ҰрқңүрқңҮрқңүsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§displaystyle Cleft(xover y,xiover y,muover ybarPzright)=left|yoverxiright|barCleft(xoverxi,yoverxi,muoverxibarPzright),, and вҖҰвҖҰldots denotes the higher-twist contributions which have the same power-counting as in Eq. except that z2superscriptрқ‘§2z2 is replaced by 1/(xвҖӢPВҜz)21superscriptрқ‘ҘsuperscriptВҜрқ‘ғрқ‘§21/(xbarPz)2. For the helicity and transversity quark quasi-GPDs, the factorization formula has the same form as Eq. citebib.bib341. The matching coefficient can be obtained by replacing the hadron states in Eqs. and with the quark states carrying momentum p+О”/2рқ‘қО”2p+Delta/2 and pвҲ’О”/2рқ‘қО”2p-Delta/2 with pОј=(p0,0,0,pz)superscriptрқ‘қрқңҮsuperscriptрқ‘қ000superscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§pmu=(p0,0,0,pz), and calculating the quark matrix element in perturbation theory. The explicit expression for the рқ’ӘвҖӢ(Оұs)рқ’Әsubscriptрқӣјрқ‘ O(alphas) matching coefficients can be found incitebib.bib341. An important feature of the result is: The quasi-GPDs do not vanish in all yрқ‘Ұy range, but the collinear singularities only show up in DGLAP and ERBL regions at one-loop. They are exactly the same as those in light-cone GPDs, and thus cancel in the matching coefficient. Moreover, one can derive momentum RGEs for the quasi-GPDs, which are turned into RGE for the scale dependence of the GPDs by the matching procedure. To conclude this subsection, let us make some remarks on the EFT formula for the quark GPD above. First, at zero skewness Оҫ=0рқңү0xi=0, we have FвҖӢ(x,0,t,Ој)рқҗ№рқ‘Ҙ0рқ‘ЎрқңҮdisplaystyle F(x,0,t,mu) =вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢy|y|вҖӢCвҖӢ(xy,0,ОјyвҖӢPz)вҖӢFвҖӢ(y,0,t,Pz,Ој)absentsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұрқҗ¶рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұ0рқңҮрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқҗ№рқ‘Ұ0рқ‘Ўsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyle=!!int-inftyinfty!dyover|y|C!left(xover y,0,muover yPzright)!tildeF(y,0,t,Pz,mu) +вҖҰ,вҖҰdisplaystyleqquad+ldots,, where the matching kernel CвҖӢ(x/y,0,Ој/(yвҖӢPz))рқҗ¶рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ұ0рқңҮрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§C(x/y,0,mu/(yPz)) is exactly the same as the matching coefficient for the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib247, even when tвү 0рқ‘Ў0tneq 0. This can be understood as follows: At zero skewness, both the longitudinal momentum transfer and the energy transfer vanish, the momentum transfer is purely transverse and thus is not affected by Lorentz boost along the longitudinal zрқ‘§z direction. As a result, no extra matching related to tрқ‘Ўt is required in the large Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz limit, and the matching remains the same as in the quasi-PDF case. If we take the forward limit О”вҶ’0вҶ’О”0Deltato 0, then Eq. reduces exactly to the EFT expansion formula for the PDFcitebib.bib277, bib.bib247. Second, in the limit ОҫвҶ’1вҶ’рқңү1xito 1 and tвҶ’0вҶ’рқ‘Ў0tto 0, the quasi-GPD reduces to the quasi-DA that will be discussed in the next subsection, and the corresponding matching kernel also reduces to that for the quasi-DA.

Within LaMET, the DAs of protons as well as other hadrons can also be extracted from lattice simulations of appropriately chosen quasi-DAs. In this subsection, we show how this can be done in practice. For illustration, we take the leading-twist pion DA as an example. The application to other hadronscitebib.bib454, bib.bib434 is analogous. The leading-twist DA of the pion is the simplest and most extensively studied hadronic DA. It represents the probability amplitude of finding the valence qвҖӢqВҜрқ‘һВҜрқ‘һqbarq Fock state in the pion with the quark carrying a fraction xрқ‘Ҙx of the total pion momentum, and is defined as П•ПҖвҖӢ(x)subscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘Ҙdisplaystylephipi(x) =1iвҖӢfПҖвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢP+вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢ(xвҲ’12)вҖӢО»вҖӢвҹЁ0|OОі+вҖӢОі5вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|ПҖвҖӢ(P)вҹ©,absent1рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙ12рқңҶquantum-operator-product0subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқңӢрқ‘ғdisplaystyle=frac1ifpiintfracdlambda2pi P+e-i(x-frac12)lambda 0|Ogamma+gamma5(lambda n)|pi(P),, with normalization вҲ«01рқ‘‘xвҖӢП•ПҖвҖӢ(x)=1superscriptsubscript01differential-dрқ‘Ҙsubscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘Ҙ1int01dx,phipi(x)=1. Here fПҖsubscriptрқ‘“рқңӢfpi denotes the decay constant, and OОі+вҖӢОі5вҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5рқңҶрқ‘ӣOgamma+gamma5(lambda n) has the same structure as that used in Eq. with Оі+superscriptрқӣҫgamma+ replaced by Оі+вҖӢОі5superscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5gamma+gamma5. The pion DA can be constrained from experimental measurements of, e. g. , ОіОівҲ—вҶ’ПҖ0gammagamma*rightarrowpi0 from BaBar and Bellecitebib.bib28, bib.bib427, and then used as an input to test QCD in other measurements such as the pion form factorcitebib.bib186, bib.bib197. In the asymptotic limit, it is well known that the pion DA takes the form 6вҖӢxвҖӢ(1вҲ’x)6рқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘Ҙ6x(1-x)citebib.bib186, bib.bib319. However, how it behaves at lower scales remains under debate (see e. g. citebib.bib123). Calculating the pion DA with controllable systematics in LaMET will be able to shed new lights on its shape and thus on our understanding of pion structure. Following the same strategy as before, we can access the xрқ‘Ҙx-dependence of the pion DA by studying the following quasi-DAcitebib.bib277, bib.bib450 П•ПҖвҖӢ(y,Pz)subscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§displaystyletildephipi(y,Pz) =1iвҖӢfПҖвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢPzвҖӢeiвҖӢ(yвҲ’12)вҖӢО»вҖӢвҹЁ0|OОіzвҖӢОі5вҖӢ(z)|ПҖвҖӢ(P)вҹ©,absent1рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ұ12рқңҶquantum-operator-product0subscriptрқ‘Ӯsuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫ5рқ‘§рқңӢрқ‘ғdisplaystyle=frac1ifpiintfracdlambda2pi Pzei(y-frac12)lambda 0|Ogammazgamma5(z)|pi(P),, The longitudinally and transversely polarized vector meson quasi-DAs can be defined analogously by replacing ОіzвҖӢОі5superscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫ5gammazgamma5 in the above equation with Оі0superscriptрқӣҫ0gamma0, ОіzвҖӢОівҹӮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқӣҫperpendicular-togammazgammaperp, respectivelycitebib.bib342. The quark bilinear operators defining quasi-DAs follow the same renormalization pattern as those defining the quasi-PDFs or quasi-GPDs. In the literature, the Wilson-line mass-subtraction scheme was used in the first LaMET calculations of the meson DAscitebib.bib450, bib.bib454, and the RI/MOM scheme has been adopted in more recent workscitebib.bib456. The matching coefficient for the quasi-DAs can be obtained by replacing the meson state |ПҖвҖӢ(P)вҹ©ketрқңӢрқ‘ғ|pi(P) in Eqs. and with the lowest Fock state |qвҖӢ(yвҖӢP)вҖӢqВҜвҖӢ((1вҲ’y)вҖӢP)вҹ©ketрқ‘һрқ‘Ұрқ‘ғВҜрқ‘һ1рқ‘Ұрқ‘ғ|q(yP)barq((1-y)P) and calculating the quark matrix elements, where yвҖӢPрқ‘Ұрқ‘ғyP and (1вҲ’y)вҖӢP1рқ‘Ұрқ‘ғ(1-y)P are the momenta of the quark qрқ‘һq and anti-quark qВҜВҜрқ‘һbarq, respectively. Its one-loop results have been calculated in both MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS and RI/MOM schemescitebib.bib342, which agrees with matching coefficient for the quasi-GPDscitebib.bib277, bib.bib445, bib.bib341 in Eq. with the replacement of ОҫвҶ’1/(2вҖӢyвҲ’1)вҶ’рқңү12рқ‘Ұ1xito 1/(2y-1), x/ОҫвҶ’2вҖӢxвҲ’1вҶ’рқ‘Ҙрқңү2рқ‘Ҙ1x/xito 2x-1, and the external momentum pzsuperscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§pz to pz/2superscriptрқ‘қрқ‘§2pz/2. Apart from the LaMET approach in momentum space, the shape of the pion DA has also been studied using equal-time current-current correlation in coordinate space approachcitebib.bib37, bib.bib34, вҹЁ0|TвҖӢJОјвҖӢ(z2)вҖӢJОҪвҖӢ(вҲ’z2)|ПҖ0вҖӢ(P)вҹ©quantum-operator-product0рқ‘ҮsubscriptрқҗҪрқңҮрқ‘§2subscriptрқҗҪрқңҲрқ‘§2superscriptрқңӢ0рқ‘ғdisplaystyle 0|TBigJmuBig(fracz2Big)JnuBig(-fracz2Big)Big|pi0(P) =2вҖӢiвҖӢfПҖ3вҖӢПҖ2вҖӢz4вҖӢПөОјвҖӢОҪвҖӢОұвҖӢОІвҖӢPОұвҖӢzОІвҖӢОҰПҖвҖӢ(О»,z2),absent2рқ‘–subscriptрқ‘“рқңӢ3superscriptрқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘§4subscriptitalic-ПөрқңҮрқңҲрқӣјрқӣҪsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқӣјsuperscriptрқ‘§рқӣҪsubscriptОҰрқңӢрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2displaystylehskip 30.00005pt=frac2i,fpi3pi2z4epsilonmunualphabetaPalphazbetaPhipi(lambda,z2),, where ОҰПҖвҖӢ(О»,z2)subscriptОҰрқңӢрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2Phipi(lambda,z2) can be factorized as ОҰПҖвҖӢ(О»,z2)subscriptОҰрқңӢрқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2displaystylePhipi(lambda,z2) =C2вҖӢ(О»,z2вҖӢОј2,x)вҠ—П•ПҖвҖӢ(x,Ој)+вӢҜ. absenttensor-productsubscriptрқҗ¶2рқңҶsuperscriptрқ‘§2superscriptрқңҮ2рқ‘Ҙsubscriptitalic-П•рқңӢрқ‘ҘрқңҮвӢҜdisplaystyle=C2(lambda,z2mu2,x)otimesphipi(x,mu)+cdots,. Here the matching coefficient C2subscriptрқҗ¶2C2 depends on the choice of the currents. Its explicit expression can be found incitebib.bib34. The above factorization is controlled by рқ’ӘвҖӢ(z2вҖӢОӣQCD2)рқ’Әsuperscriptрқ‘§2superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2O(z2Lambdarm QCD2), with power corrections denoted by вҖңвӢҜвӢҜcdotsвҖқ. Incitebib.bib34, a combined analysis of several current-current correlations has been performed where twist-four contributions were also included using the model estimate incitebib.bib82, bib.bib43. The leading-twist pion DA was then extracted from a global fit to the data, and the second moment of the pion DA has been fitted with controlled precision, both of which favor a considerably broader shape than the asymptotic DA at a scale of 222 GeV. A large pion momentum is required to access information at large О»рқңҶlambda so that we can extract wider range of xрқ‘Ҙx or higher moments of the pion DAcitebib.bib37.

Higher-twist distributions are quantities of great interest because they describe the coherent quark-gluon correlations in the proton. In contrast with the leading-twist distributions, our understanding of the higher-twist ones is rather poor. On one hand, they often depend on more than one parton momentum fractions; on the other hand, there is no physical intuition about what they may look like, in particular, about how they behave asymptotically at small and large xрқ‘Ҙxcitebib.bib80. There have been studies on the higher-twist distributions in the context of their connection to the DIS structure function, the transverse single-spin asymmetries in various hadron productions, GPDs related to quark and gluon OAM, parton DAs, etc. LaMET will be able to shed new lights by providing a possibility to access them from the lattice. Higher-twist contributions also appear in LaMET expansion, where the suppression is provided by powers of the hadron momentum squared. In all factorizations presented in previous sections, only the leading-twist terms that capture the logarithmic dependence on hadron momentum are taken into account. The higher-twist contributions have been assumed to be small. If the hadron momentum is not sufficiently large compared and/or one is close to the endpoint region (xвҶ’0вҶ’рқ‘Ҙ0xto 0 and xвҶ’1вҶ’рқ‘Ҙ1xto 1), the higher-twist contributions can become non-negligible, whose structure and impact require understanding.Beyond leading-twist, there exist three simplest twist-three quark distributions eвҖӢ(x)рқ‘’рқ‘Ҙe(x), gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘgT(x) and hLвҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һрқҗҝрқ‘ҘhL(x) related to the unpolarized, transversely and longitudinally polarized protoncitebib.bib250, eвҖӢ(x)рқ‘’рқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle e(x) =12вҖӢMвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢО»absent12рқ‘Җрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶdisplaystyle=frac12Mintfracdlambda2pieixlambda Г—вҹЁPS|ПҲ+вҖ Оі0ПҲвҲ’(О»n)|PSвҹ©+h. c. ,displaystylequadtimes PS|psidagger+gamma0psi-(lambda n)|PS+rm h. c. ,, gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle gT(x) =12вҖӢMвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢО»absent12рқ‘Җрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶdisplaystyle=frac12Mintfracdlambda2pieixlambda Г—вҹЁPSвҹӮ|ПҲ+вҖ Оі0ОівҹӮОі5ПҲвҲ’(О»n)|PSвҹӮвҹ©+h. c. ,displaystylequadtimes PSperp|psidagger+gamma0gammaperpgamma5psi-(lambda n)|PSperp+rm h. c. ,, hLвҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һрқҗҝрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle hL(x) =12вҖӢMвҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢeiвҖӢxвҖӢО»absent12рқ‘Җрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶdisplaystyle=frac12Mintfracdlambda2pieixlambda Г—вҹЁPSz|ПҲ+вҖ Оі0Оі5ПҲвҲ’(О»n)|PSzвҹ©+h. c. ,displaystylequadtimes PSz|psidagger+gamma0gamma5psi-(lambda n)|PSz+rm h. c. ,, where we have again employed the decomposition of quark fields ПҲ=ПҲ++ПҲвҲ’рқң“subscriptрқң“subscriptрқң“psi=psi++psi- in Sec. and the light-cone gauge A+=0superscriptрқҗҙ0A+=0, and вҖңh. c. вҖқ stands for Hermitian conjugate. The twist-three distributions can contribute as leading effects in certain experimental observables. For example, gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘgT(x) and hLвҖӢ(x)subscriptв„Һрқҗҝрқ‘ҘhL(x) can be measured as the leading effects in the longitudinal-transverse spin asymmetry in polarized Drell-Yan process. Since ПҲвҲ’subscriptрқң“psi- is a non-dynamical component depending on ПҲ+subscriptрқң“psi+, all the above distributions can be shown to be related to more complicated quark-gluon correlation functionscitebib.bib261, bib.bib39. A complete set of such correlation functions has been given incitebib.bib388, bib.bib254, bib.bib266, bib.bib298, where the quark-gluon correlations in a transversely-polarized proton take the following form TqвҖӢ(x1,x2)=1(P+)2вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»вҖӢdвҖӢО¶(2вҖӢПҖ)2вҖӢeiвҖӢО»вҖӢx1+iвҖӢО¶вҖӢ(x2вҲ’x1)subscriptрқ‘Үрқ‘һsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1subscriptрқ‘Ҙ21superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғ2рқ‘‘рқңҶрқ‘‘рқңҒsuperscript2рқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘–рқңҒsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ2subscriptрқ‘Ҙ1displaystyle Tq(x1,x2)=frac1(P+)2intfracdlambda dzeta(2pi)2eilambda x1+izeta(x2-x1) Г—вҹЁPвҖӢSвҹӮ|ПҲВҜвҖӢвҖӢОі+вҖӢПө+вҲ’SвҹӮвҖӢiвҖӢgвҖӢF+iвҖӢ(О¶вҖӢn)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹӮвҹ©,absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toВҜрқң“0superscriptрқӣҫsuperscriptitalic-Пөabsentsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toрқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–рқңҒрқ‘ӣрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-todisplaystylehskip 20.00003pttimes PSperp|barpsigamma+epsilon+-SperpigF+i(zeta n)psi(lambda n)|PSperp, TО”вҖӢqвҖӢ(x1,x2)=1(P+)2вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»вҖӢdвҖӢО¶(2вҖӢПҖ)2вҖӢeiвҖӢО»вҖӢx1+iвҖӢО¶вҖӢ(x2вҲ’x1)subscriptрқ‘ҮО”рқ‘һsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1subscriptрқ‘Ҙ21superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғ2рқ‘‘рқңҶрқ‘‘рқңҒsuperscript2рқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ1рқ‘–рқңҒsubscriptрқ‘Ҙ2subscriptрқ‘Ҙ1displaystyle TDelta q(x1,x2)=frac1(P+)2intfracdlambda dzeta(2pi)2eilambda x1+izeta(x2-x1) Г—вҹЁPвҖӢSвҹӮ|ПҲВҜвҖӢвҖӢiвҖӢОі+вҖӢОі5вҖӢSвҹӮiвҖӢgвҖӢF+iвҖӢ(О¶вҖӢn)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn)|PвҖӢSвҹӮвҹ©. absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-toВҜрқң“0рқ‘–superscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқӣҫ5subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘–perpendicular-toрқ‘”superscriptрқҗ№рқ‘–рқңҒрқ‘ӣрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ҷperpendicular-todisplaystylehskip 20.00003pttimes PSperp|barpsiigamma+gamma5SiperpgF+i(zeta n)psi(lambda n)|PSperp,. There are also ones in an unpolarized and longitudinally-polarized proton. Generalizing to off-forward kinematics, the resulting twist-three GPDs are also related to quark and gluon OAM contribution to the proton spincitebib.bib232, bib.bib281. One can also define twist-four distributions in a similar way as in Eq. by using ПҲвҲ’subscriptрқң“psi- for both quark fields. More general twist-four distributions will involve three light-cone variables, which will contribute to, e. g. , 1/Q21superscriptрқ‘„21/Q2 term in DIScitebib.bib252, bib.bib188, bib.bib250, bib.bib255. In principle, all the above higher-twist distributions, as well as others that have not been listed here, can be computed using the LaMET approach by choosing appropriate quasi-LF correlations. For example, the first exploratory lattice calculation of gTвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқ‘”рқ‘Үрқ‘ҘgT(x) has been done incitebib.bib62, which will be discussed in Sec. . However, extra complications are expected due to their complex structure. For instance, the lightcone zero modes that do not enter in dealing with leading-twist distributions come into play here. Recently, one of the authors has shown how to study the properties of these zero modes from lattice simulations in LaMETcitebib.bib270. In addition, the higher-twist distributions will have a more complex mixing patterncitebib.bib261, bib.bib39. Thus, their matching from the corresponding quasi distributions must take into account such mixings, making them more challenging than calculating the twist-two PDFs. One-loop studies of the matching for twist-three disributions have been carried out incitebib.bib63, bib.bib64.Let us turn to the power suppressed higher-twist contributions appearing in the extraction of leading-twist quark PDFs using LaMET. Such contributions have two distinct origins. To understand them, let us recall the OPE for the quasi-LF correlation in Eq. . For simplicity, we ignore the renormalization here. Recovering the leading-twist quark PDF requires removing the contributions of both trace terms in that equation. The trace terms on the r. h. s. of Eq. , which lead to contributions suppressed by powers of M2/(Pz)2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2M2/(Pz)2, are known as kinematic power contributions or target mass corrections. In DIS, they can be accounted for by changing the scaling variable xрқ‘Ҙx to the Nachtmann variablecitebib.bib376. In the case of LaMET, it behaves slightly differently, as shown in the following. The second type of power corrections come from the trace terms in the operators on the r. h. s. of Eq. , and in general leads to contributions of рқ’ӘвҖӢ(ОӣQCD2/(Pz)2)рқ’ӘsubscriptsuperscriptОӣ2QCDsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2O(Lambda2rmQCD/(Pz)2) . These are genuine higher-twist contributions that involve multi-parton correlations, sometimes also known as dynamical higher-twist contributions. The target mass corrections have been computed to all orders in M2/(Pz)2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2M2/(Pz)2 for the quark quasi-PDFs incitebib.bib114, bib.bib394. The genuine higher-twist contributions have been investigated using two different approachescitebib.bib114, bib.bib84. According tocitebib.bib114, the M2/(Pz)2superscriptрқ‘Җ2superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2M2/(Pz)2 corrections can be obtained from the ratio Kmsubscriptрқҗҫрқ‘ҡdisplaystyle Km вүЎn(Ој1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢnОјm)вҖӢPОј1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢPОјmnОј1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢnОјmвҖӢPОј1вҖӢвӢҜвҖӢPОјm=вҲ‘i=0imaxCmвҲ’iiвҖӢci,displaystyleequivfracn(mu1cdots nmum)Pmu1cdots Pmumnmu1cdots nmumPmu1cdots Pmum=sumi=0itextmaxCm-iici,, where imax=(mвҲ’ModвҖӢ[m,2])/2subscriptрқ‘–maxрқ‘ҡModрқ‘ҡ22itextmax=(m-textMod[m,2])/2, Cрқҗ¶C is the binomial function and c=вҲ’n2вҖӢM2/4вҖӢ(nвӢ…P)2=M2/4вҖӢ(Pz)2рқ‘җsuperscriptрқ‘ӣ2superscriptрқ‘Җ24superscriptвӢ…рқ‘ӣрқ‘ғ2superscriptрқ‘Җ24superscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2c=-n2M2/4left(ncdot Pright)2=M2/4(Pz)2 with nОј=(0,0,0,вҲ’1)superscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮ0001nmu=(0,0,0,-1) and nвӢ…P=PzвӢ…рқ‘ӣрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§ncdot P=Pz. Plugged into the tree-level OPE formula in Eq. , the above factors can then be converted to the following relation between unpolarized PDF and quasi-PDFcitebib.bib114 qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙdisplaystyle q(x) =1+4вҖӢcвҲ‘n=0вҲһ(4вҖӢc)nf+2вҖӢn+1[(1+(вҲ’1)n)q(f+2вҖӢn+1вҖӢx2вҖӢ(4вҖӢc)n)displaystyle=sqrt1+4csumn=0inftyfrac(4c)nf+2n+1Big[(1+(-1)n)tildeqBig(fracf+2n+1x2(4c)nBig) +(1вҲ’(вҲ’1)n)q(вҲ’f+2вҖӢn+1вҖӢx2вҖӢ(4вҖӢc)n)],displaystyleqquad+(1-(-1)n)tildeqBig(frac-f+2n+1x2(4c)nBig)Big], where f+=1+4вҖӢc+1subscriptрқ‘“14рқ‘җ1f+=sqrt1+4c+1. It is worth noting that quark number conservation is preserved in the above result. The target mass corrections for the longitudinally and transversely polarized quasi-PDFs can be derived analogously. The trace part on the r. h. s. of Eq. is a genuine higher-twist effect. One may try to construct a non-local form of the higher-twist operators from OPE. The leading trace term, which is a twist-four effect, has been studied incitebib.bib114 (see alsocitebib.bib42) and shown to give rise to a twist-four PDF q4вҖӢ(x,Pz)=вҲ«вҲ’вҲһвҲһdвҖӢО»8вҖӢПҖвҖӢPzвҖӢО“0вҖӢ(вҲ’iвҖӢxвҖӢО»)вҖӢвҹЁP|OtrвҖӢ(z)|Pвҹ©,subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§superscriptsubscriptрқ‘‘рқңҶ8рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§subscriptО“0рқ‘–рқ‘ҘрқңҶquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Ӯtrрқ‘§рқ‘ғdisplaystyleqtext4(x,Pz)=int-inftyinfty!fracdlambda8pi Pz,Gamma0left(-ixlambdaright)left Pleft|Otexttr(z)right|Pright, with Otr(z)=вҲ«0zdz1ПҲВҜ[О“ОҪW(0,z1)DОҪW(z1,z)displaystyle Otexttr(z)=int0z!dz1,barpsiBig[GammanuWleft(0,z1right)DnuWleft(z1,zright) +вҲ«0z1dz2nвӢ…О“W(0,z2)DОҪW(z2,z1)DОҪW(z1,z)]ПҲ(zn),displaystyle+int0z1!!!dz2,ncdotGamma,Wleft(0,z2right)DnuWleft(z2,z1right)DnuWleft(z1,zright)Big]psi(zn),, where one has О“Ој=ОіОј,ОіОјвҖӢОі5,ОівҹӮвҖӢОіОјвҖӢОі5superscriptО“рқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫ5superscriptрқӣҫperpendicular-tosuperscriptрқӣҫрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҫ5Gammamu=gammamu,gammamugamma5,gammaperpgammamugamma5 for the unpolarized, helicity and transversity PDFs, respectively. О“0subscriptО“0Gamma0 is the incomplete Gamma function О“0вҖӢ(вҲ’iвҖӢx)=вҲ«01dвҖӢttвҖӢeiвҖӢx/t. subscriptО“0рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptsubscript01рқ‘‘рқ‘Ўрқ‘Ўsuperscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқ‘Ҙрқ‘ЎGamma0left(-ixright)=int01fracdtteix/t,. The above twist-four contribution needs to be removed from the quasi-PDF to recover the leading-twist PDF. It also provides a possibility for practical computations on the lattice. However, as a multi-parton correlation involving more gauge links and covariant derivatives, its lattice computation is rather challenging and has not been carried out in any existing work yet. Another approach that has been used to estimate power corrections related to quark quasi-PDFs is the renormalon model (seecitebib.bib55 for a comprehensive review). It is based on the observation that the perturbative expansion of the matching coefficient for the quasi-PDF diverges factorially with the loop order, implying that it is only well defined up to a power accuracy. This is known as the renormalon ambiguity, which must be cancelled by terms in the higher-twist contributions. Incitebib.bib84, it was shown that the cancellation of renormalon ambiguity requires that the leading higher-twist or twist-four contribution takes the following form q4вҖӢ(y,Pz,Ој)=Ој2вҖӢвҲ«вҲ’11dвҖӢx|x|вҖӢDвҖӢ(yx)вҖӢqвҖӢ(x,Ој)+q4вҖІвҖӢ(y,Pz,Ој),subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮsuperscriptрқңҮ2superscriptsubscript11рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙрқҗ·рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙрқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘һвҖІ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyleq4(y,Pz,mu)=mu2int-11!fracdx|x|DBig(fracyxBig)q(x,mu)+qprime4(y,Pz,mu),, where the first term on the r. h. s. cancels the renormalon ambiguity from the leading-twist matching coefficient, and q4вҖІsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘һвҖІ4qprime4 depends on ОјрқңҮmu at most logarithmically. Since the first term is to merely cancel similar contributions in the matching coefficient, it does not contribute to any physical observable. The renormalon model of power corrections citebib.bib57, bib.bib170, bib.bib156, bib.bib157, bib.bib59, bib.bib83 is based on the assumption that, by replacing ОјрқңҮmu with a suitable nonperturbative scale, this contribution reflects the order and the functional form of actual power-suppressed contributions. This was known as вҖңultraviolet dominanceвҖқ incitebib.bib81, bib.bib55, bib.bib58. Under this assumption, we obtain the following estimate, q4вҖӢ(y,Pz,Ој)=ОәвҖӢОӣQCD2вҖӢвҲ«вҲ’11dвҖӢx|x|вҖӢDвҖӢ(yx)вҖӢqвҖӢ(x,Ој),subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮрқң…superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2superscriptsubscript11рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙрқҗ·рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙрқ‘һрқ‘ҘрқңҮq4(y,Pz,mu)=kappaLambdarm QCD2int-11!fracdx|x|DBig(fracyxBig),q(x,mu),, where Оәрқң…kappa is a dimensionless coefficient of рқ’ӘвҖӢрқ’Ә1O that cannot be fixed within theory and remains a free parameter. A detailed analysiscitebib.bib84 showed that for the quasi-PDF we have q4вҖӢ(y,Pz)=ОәвҖӢОӣQCD2y2вҖӢ(1вҲ’y)вҖӢ(Pz)2subscriptрқ‘һ4рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқң…superscriptsubscriptОӣQCD2superscriptрқ‘Ұ21рқ‘Ұsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§2displaystyleq4(y,Pz)=frackappaLambdarm QCD2y2(1-y)(Pz)2 Г—(1вҲ’y)вҖӢ[вҲ«|y|1dвҖӢxxвҖӢ[x2(1вҲ’x)+вҲ’2вҖӢx2]вҖӢqвҖӢ(yx)+2вҖӢqвҖӢ(y)вҲ’|y|вҖӢqвҖІвҖӢ(y)],absent1рқ‘Ұdelimited-superscriptsubscriptрқ‘Ұ1рқ‘‘рқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙdelimited-superscriptрқ‘Ҙ2subscript1рқ‘Ҙ2superscriptрқ‘Ҙ2рқ‘һрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ҙ2рқ‘һрқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘һвҖІрқ‘Ұdisplaystyletimes(1-y)!Big[!!int|y|1!!fracdxxBig[fracx2(1-x)+!-!2x2Big]qBig(fracyxBig)!+!2q(y)!-!|y|qprime(y)Big],, where the first term in the integral was reproduced in a recent analysis of the renormalon effects in the quasi-PDFcitebib.bib339. As one can see, the second row vanishes as qвҖӢ(y)рқ‘һрқ‘Ұq(y) when yвҶ’1вҶ’рқ‘Ұ1yto 1 if limyвҶ’1qвҖӢ(y)вҲј(1вҲ’y)asimilar-tosubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ұ1рқ‘һрқ‘Ұsuperscript1рқ‘Ұрқ‘Һlimyto 1q(y)sim(1-y)a with a>0рқ‘Һ0a>0. Similar analysis can also be done for the pseudo-PDF. The above result can be used as a way to model the twist-four contribution with Оәрқң…kappa as the only parameter.

Over the past three decades, much experimental and theoretical work has been done on the origin and structure of proton spin, which has been covered in depth in the review articles citebib.bib201, bib.bib47, bib.bib10, bib.bib316, bib.bib269, bib.bib166, bib.bib284. In addition to the spin-dependent PDFs and TMDs, the GCPOsвҖ”in particular the GPDsвҖ”also play an important role in understanding the spin structure of the proton. Since GPDs describe the correlation between the transverse position and longitudinal momentum of quarks and gluons inside the proton, they offer a unique channel to study the orbital angular momentum (OAM) from experiments. There are two widely known definitions of OAM in literature. One is the kinetic OAM in the gauge-invariant and frame-independent sum rule for the proton spincitebib.bib258, bib.bib257, which is related to the first moment of twist-two GPDs and can be calculated from the form factors of the symmetric QCD energy-momentum tensor. A review of the lattice calculations of kinetic OAM can be found incitebib.bib284. The other definition, which has a clear partonic interpretation in comparison to the kinetic OAM, is the canonical OAM in the naive partonic sum rulecitebib.bib251 based on the free-field form of the QCD angular momentum, JвҶ’вҶ’рқҗҪdisplaystylevecJ =вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢПҲвҖ вҖӢОЈвҶ’2вҖӢПҲ+вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢПҲвҖ вҖӢ[ОҫвҶ’Г—(вҲ’iвҖӢвҲҮвҶ’)]вҖӢПҲabsentsuperscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүsuperscriptрқң“вҖ вҶ’ОЈ2рқң“superscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүsuperscriptрқң“вҖ delimited-вҶ’рқңүрқ‘–вҶ’вҲҮрқң“displaystyle=int d3xi psidaggerfracvecSigma2psi+int d3xi psidaggerleft[vecxitimes(-ivecnabla)right]psi +вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢEвҶ’Г—AвҶ’+вҲ«d3вҖӢОҫвҖӢEiвҖӢ(ОҫвҶ’Г—вҲҮвҶ’)вҖӢAi,superscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүвҶ’рқҗёвҶ’рқҗҙsuperscriptрқ‘‘3рқңүsuperscriptрқҗёрқ‘–вҶ’рқңүвҶ’вҲҮsuperscriptрқҗҙрқ‘–displaystyle +int d3xi vecEtimesvecA+int d3xi Ei left(vecxitimesvecnablaright)Ai , where iрқ‘–i is the spatial Lorentz index. Except for the first one, the other three operators are gauge dependent, and their matrix elements are generally frame dependent. In high-energy scattering, there is one frame and gauge that are special: the IMF and light-front gauge, A+=0superscriptрқҗҙ0A+=0. Therefore, the naive partonic sum rule for proton spin can be expressed ascitebib.bib251 12=12вҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+lqzвҖӢ(Ој)+О”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)+lgzвҖӢ(Ој),1212О”ОЈрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮО”рқҗәрқңҮsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮdisplaystylefrac12=frac12DeltaSigma(mu)+lzq(mu)+Delta G(mu)+lzg(mu),, where lqzвҖӢ(Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮlzq(mu) and lgzвҖӢ(Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮlzg(mu) are the canonical OAM of the quark and gluon partons, respectively. Both lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz and lgzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘”рқ‘§lgz can be related to twist-three GPDscitebib.bib229, bib.bib281, bib.bib232, which can be accessed through spin-asymmetries in hard exclusive processescitebib.bib283, bib.bib231, bib.bib68, bib.bib67 (see the recent reviewcitebib.bib284). To fully understand the partonic spin structure of the proton, one also needs to determine the quark and gluon canonical OAM, lqzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һlzq and lgzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”lzg. LaMET allows extraction of lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz and lgzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘”рқ‘§lgz from lattice calculation in the same way as the gluon helicity that was reviewed in Sec. . The quasi-partonic OAM operators can be chosen as the free-field operators fixed in gauges that belong to the universality classcitebib.bib230. Their matrix elements lqzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һtildelzq and lgzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”tildelzg can be calculated from the off-forward matrix elements of the relevant energy-momentum tensorscitebib.bib460, for example, lqzвҖӢ(2вҖӢSz)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һ2superscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘§displaystyletildelzq(2Sz) =limО”вҶ’0ПөiвҖӢjвҖӢвҲӮвҲӮiвҖӢО”iвҖӢвҹЁPвҖІвҖӢS|ПҲвҖ вҖӢвҖӢiвҖӢвҲӮjПҲвҖӢ|PвҖӢSвҹ©|вҲҮвҶ’вӢ…AвҶ’=0. absentevaluated-atsubscriptвҶ’О”0superscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—рқ‘–superscriptО”рқ‘–quantum-operator-productsuperscriptрқ‘ғвҖІрқ‘Ҷsuperscriptрқң“вҖ 0рқ‘–superscriptрқ‘—рқң“0рқ‘ғрқ‘ҶвӢ…вҶ’вҲҮвҶ’рқҗҙ0displaystyle=limDeltato 0epsilonijpartialoverpartial iDeltai PprimeS|psidaggeripartialjpsi|PSBig|vecnablacdotvecA=0,. where the kinematics is the same as Eq. . Along with О”вҖӢGО”рқҗәDelta G, lqzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һtildelzq and lgzsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”tildelzg can be matched to the partonic quantities defined in the Jaffe-Manohar sum rule through the factorization formulas, lqzвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyletildelzq(Pz,mu) =PqвҖӢqвҖӢlqzвҖӢ(Ој)+PgвҖӢqвҖӢlgzвҖӢ(Ој)absentsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘һрқ‘һsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘”рқ‘һsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮdisplaystyle=Pqqlzq(mu)+Pgqlzg(mu) +pqвҖӢqвҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+pgвҖӢqвҖӢО”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)+вҖҰ,subscriptрқ‘қрқ‘һрқ‘һО”ОЈрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘”рқ‘һО”рқҗәрқңҮвҖҰdisplaystyleqquad+pqqDeltaSigma(mu)+pgqDelta G(mu)+. . . ,, lgzвҖӢ(Pz,Ој)subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§рқңҮdisplaystyletildelzg(Pz,mu) =PqвҖӢgвҖӢlqzвҖӢ(Ој)+PgвҖӢgвҖӢlgzвҖӢ(Ој)absentsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘һрқ‘”subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘һрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘”рқ‘”subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘§рқ‘”рқңҮdisplaystyle=Pqglzq(mu)+Pgglzg(mu) +pqвҖӢgвҖӢО”вҖӢОЈвҖӢ(Ој)+pgвҖӢgвҖӢО”вҖӢGвҖӢ(Ој)+вҖҰ,subscriptрқ‘қрқ‘һрқ‘”О”ОЈрқңҮsubscriptрқ‘қрқ‘”рқ‘”О”рқҗәрқңҮвҖҰdisplaystyleqquad+pqgDeltaSigma(mu)+pggDelta G(mu)+. . . ,, where вӢҜвӢҜcdots are power corrections suppressed by the momentum Pzsuperscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘§Pz, and the one-loop matching coefficients in front of each term on the r. h. s. have been calculated in the Coulomb gaugecitebib.bib287. Since the quasi-partonic operators are gauge-variant and need to be fixed in a particular gauge, they can mix with new operators that are not allowed by Lorentz or gauge symmetries. For example, the gauge-dependent potential angular momentum ПҲвҖ вҖӢ(rвҶ’Г—AвҶ’)вҖӢПҲsuperscriptрқң“вҖ вҶ’рқ‘ҹвҶ’рқҗҙрқң“psidagger(vecrtimesvecA)psi comes into playcitebib.bib432, bib.bib278. Such mixings must be taken into account in lattice renormalization to have a controlled calculation of the canonical OAM. Apart from the above approach, it has also been proposed to calculate the ratio of lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz and the valence quark number from the derivatives of off-forward matrix elements of staple-shaped quark Wilson line operatorscitebib.bib189, whose definition can be found in Eq. below. The first lattice calculations with this approach have been carried out incitebib.bib189, bib.bib190, which shows different size of effects between the kinetic and canonical OAM. For systematic improvement in this calculation, one should include the matching of such matrix elements to the physical lqzsuperscriptsubscriptрқ‘ҷрқ‘һрқ‘§lqz in the limit when the transverse separation of the quark fields approaches zero. For the transverse polarization, it is natural to define a twist-two partonic transverse angular momentum density of quarkscitebib.bib240, bib.bib280, bib.bib282, bib.bib284, JвҹӮqвҖӢ(x)=xвҖӢ[qвҖӢ(x)+EqвҖӢ(x)]/2,subscriptsuperscriptрқҗҪрқ‘һperpendicular-toрқ‘Ҙрқ‘Ҙdelimited-рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘Ҙ2Jqperp(x)=xleft[q(x)+Eq(x)right]/2, and similarly for the gluons, where qвҖӢ(x)рқ‘һрқ‘Ҙq(x) is the unpolarized quark/antiquark distributions, and Eq,gвҖӢ(x)subscriptрқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘”рқ‘ҘEq,g(x) is the GPDs defined earlier in this section. Thus to get a simple partonic picture of the proton transverse spin from the first principles, it is important to calculate the GPD EвҖӢ(x)рқҗёрқ‘ҘE(x) using LaMET.
content_sections.V Transverse-Momentum Dependent PDFs: The transverse-momentum-dependent (TMD) parton distribution functions (TMDPDFs) are a natural generalization of the collinear PDFs to include both longitudinal and transverse momentum of partons. They are in principle probability distributions fiвҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ,Пғ)subscriptрқ‘“рқ‘–рқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toрқңҺfi(x,veckperp,sigma) of finding a parton of given species iрқ‘–i, longitudinal and transverse momentum (xвҖӢP+,kвҶ’вҹӮ)рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-to(xP+,veckperp), and polarization ПғрқңҺsigma inside the hadron state. TMDPDFs are playing an increasingly important role in understanding the partonic structure of hadrons and high-energy scattering. The TMD parton densities were firstly introduced by Collins and Soper in 1980scitebib.bib141, bib.bib142, bib.bib144, bib.bib146, bib.bib145, bib.bib72 to understand the Drell-Yan (DY) and e+вҖӢeвҲ’superscriptрқ‘’superscriptрқ‘’e+e- annihilation process, and generalized incitebib.bib264, bib.bib263 to semi-inclusive deep-inelastic scattering(SIDIS) process. The TMD factorization has been reanalyzed in the framework of SCET in which modes are made manifest by effective fields citebib.bib48, bib.bib49, bib.bib354, bib.bib51, bib.bib181, bib.bib179, bib.bib125. Various TMD factorization formalisms finally converged to the standard one where a scheme-independent TMDPDF can be definedcitebib.bib179, bib.bib139, bib.bib134. The TMD parton densities are important in understanding the experimental processes where the transverse momenta of final state particles are measured. For example, in DY pair and W,Zрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ҚW,Z production it is known that the differential cross section dвҖӢПғ/dвҖӢQT2рқ‘‘рқңҺрқ‘‘superscriptsubscriptрқ‘„рқ‘Ү2dsigma/dQT2 normally peaks at relatively small transverse momentum. For QвҲј10similar-toрқ‘„10Qsim 10 GeV, the peak is typically located at QвҹӮвҲј1similar-tosubscriptрқ‘„perpendicular-to1Qperpsim 1 GeV where nonperturbative effects are importantcitebib.bib146. A good knowledge of TMD parton densities is therefore crucial for the determination of the cross sections and precision test of perturbative QCD predictions. Besides their importance in understanding the high-energy experimental data, the TMD parton densities are also important by themselves for their crucial role in describing hadron structures. With them, one can simultaneously study the fast-moving collinear physics through the longitudinal xрқ‘Ҙx-dependencies, and the nonperturbative effect from the transverse kвҶ’вҹӮsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toveckperp-dependencies. Moreover, the TMDPDFs are sensitive to effects such as soft radiations. Therefore, the physics in the presence of transverse degrees of freedom is rather rich. This is particularly true in studies of spin-dependent phenomena where one can define various TMDPDFs through Lorentz decompositions (see Sec. V. B). One example is the Sivers function for a transversely polarized proton, ПөiвҖӢjвҖӢkвҹӮiвҖӢSвҹӮiвҖӢf1вҖӢTвҹӮвҖӢ(x,kвҹӮ)subscriptitalic-Пөрқ‘–рқ‘—subscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҳрқ‘–perpendicular-tosubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘Ҷрқ‘–perpendicular-tosubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘“perpendicular-to1рқ‘Үрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘ҳperpendicular-toepsilonijkiperpSiperpfperp1T(x,kperp), which is naive-time-reversal odd and is predicted to change sign between the DY and SIDIS processescitebib.bib132. Similar properties also exist in the Boer-Mulders functioncitebib.bib73 concerning a transversely-polarized parton distribution in an unpolarized hadron. These two functions are related to the single transverse spin asymmetry. If we generalize the TMDPDFs to include the impact parameter dependence, we can further define the Wigner function, the parton orbital angular momentum distributions, etccitebib.bib54, bib.bib344. Therefore, the TMDPDFs allow for a more complete and refined 3D description (or tomography) of the hadron structurecitebib.bib99, bib.bib74. The 3D tomography of the proton is a major physical goal of the EIC program. The TMDPDFs are also important in understanding small-xрқ‘Ҙx physicscitebib.bib311, bib.bib40, bib.bib38, bib.bib307, bib.bib308. Our current knowledge on TMDPDFs mainly comes from fitting to the experimental datacitebib.bib313, bib.bib304, bib.bib423, bib.bib180, bib.bib297, bib.bib30, bib.bib408, bib.bib60, bib.bib410, bib.bib29. This is, however, rather primitive due to the paucity of data. Although the future EIC will make up the gap and produce more data for TMD measurements, it is still important to develop first-principle methods for the determination of nonperturbative TMDPDFs, which can serve as a test or provide useful inputs to constrain the global fits. LaMET provides a systematic way to extract TMDPDFs from the lattice calculations. Early studiescitebib.bib279, bib.bib271, bib.bib176, bib.bib177 have tried to construct a quasi-TMDPDF on the lattice, but its relation to the physical TMDPDF is expected to be nonperturbative due to complications in the soft functioncitebib.bib177. The recent works incitebib.bib273, bib.bib272 provide a formulation to calculate the soft function so that a perturbative matching formula can be established between the quasi- and physical TMDPDFs, allowing for a complete determination of the latter from lattice QCD. In this section we review the application of LaMET to the nonperturbative TMDPDFs. The investigation is still in its early stage and a lot remains to be explored, particularly in lattice calculations and matching. In the first subsection we introduce the TMDPDFs and discuss the associated rapidity divergences. In the following subsections, we define the quasi-TMDPDFs or TMD momentum distributions in a proton of finite momentum, and study their momentum RGEs and UV renormalization properties. In the process, we introduce the off-light-cone soft functions. We then present the factorization of the quasi-TMDPDFs into the light-cone TMDPDFs and the off-light-cone soft function, where various one-loop results and the relevant RGEs are also given. The properties of the off-light-cone soft function are discussed in the last subsection, where it is shown to be related to the form factor of a pair of charged color sources, which paves the way for its calculation on a Euclidean lattice.
content_sections.V Transverse-Momentum Dependent PDFs V.1: As explained in Sec. , we can define various TMDPDFs by choosing different gauge-links between the quark or gluon bilinears. The one relevant to high-energy phenomena is defined with light-like Wilson lines. The links represent the propagation of high-energy color-charged particles, and are crucial in forming gauge-invariant nonlocal operatorscitebib.bib53. As argued in previous sections, such operators are the result of an EFT description (more explicitly so in SCET) arising from taking the infinite-momentum limit of the proton. Thus, it is natural to expect that they require additional regularization and renormalization. Let us take the non-singlet quark unpolarized TMDPDF as an example. Without the field theoretic subtleties, the distribution is fвҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ)=12вҖӢP+вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢО»2вҖӢПҖвҖӢd2вҖӢbвҶ’вҹӮ(2вҖӢПҖ)2вҖӢeвҲ’iвҖӢО»вҖӢx+iвҖӢkвҶ’вҹӮвӢ…bвҶ’вҹӮрқ‘“рқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-to12superscriptрқ‘ғрқ‘‘рқңҶ2рқңӢsuperscriptрқ‘‘2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tosuperscript2рқңӢ2superscriptрқ‘’рқ‘–рқңҶрқ‘ҘвӢ…рқ‘–subscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-tosubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-todisplaystyle f(x,veckperp)=frac12P+intfracdlambda2pifracd2vecbperp(2pi)2e-ilambda x+iveckperpcdotvecbperp Г—вҹЁP|ПҲВҜвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn/2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢОі+вҖӢрқ’ІnвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn/2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(вҲ’О»вҖӢn/2)|Pвҹ©,absentquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғВҜрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tosuperscriptрқӣҫsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңҶрқ‘ӣ2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2рқ‘ғdisplaystyletimes P|barpsi(lambda n/2+vecbperp)gamma+cal Wn(lambda n/2+vecbperp)psi(-lambda n/2)|P , where рқ’ІnвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn+bвҶ’вҹӮ)subscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңҶрқ‘ӣsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tocal Wn(lambda n+vecbperp) is the staple-shaped gauge-link of the form рқ’ІnвҖӢ(Оҫ)=WnвҖ вҖӢ(Оҫ)вҖӢWвҹӮвҖӢWnвҖӢ(вҲ’ОҫвӢ…pвҖӢn),subscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңүsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҠвҖ рқ‘ӣрқңүsubscriptрқ‘Ҡperpendicular-tosubscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣвӢ…рқңүрқ‘қрқ‘ӣdisplaystylecal Wn(xi)=Wdaggern(xi)WperpWn(-xicdot pn) , WnвҖӢ(Оҫ)=рқ’«вҖӢexpвҒЎ[вҲ’iвҖӢgвҖӢвҲ«0вҲ’вҲһрқ‘‘О»вҖӢnвӢ…AвҖӢ(Оҫ+О»вҖӢn)],subscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣрқңүрқ’«рқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptsubscript0вӢ…differential-dрқңҶрқ‘ӣрқҗҙрқңүрқңҶрқ‘ӣdisplaystyle Wn(xi)=cal Pexpleft[-igint0-inftydlambda ncdot A(xi+lambda n)right] , along the light-cone direction nОјsuperscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮnmu, as shown in Fig. . The WвҹӮsubscriptрқ‘Ҡperpendicular-toWperp is a transverse gauge-link at light-cone infinity to maintain gauge-invariance. . If one uses LFQ and ignores the transverse gauge-link, the above distribution is just вҹЁP|bвҖ вҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢbвҖӢ(x,kвҶ’вҹӮ)|Pвҹ©quantum-operator-productрқ‘ғsuperscriptрқ‘ҸвҖ рқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toрқ‘Ҹрқ‘ҘsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toрқ‘ғ P|bdagger(x,veckperp)b(x,veckperp)|P for x>0рқ‘Ҙ0x>0, as expected. However, there are a number of qualifications in the above definition. First, the light-like gauge-links рқ’Іnsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣcal Wn are chosen to be past-pointing in accordance with the DY kinematics, but for SIDIS they should be chosen as future-pointing, as shown in Fig. . For unpolarized TMDPDFs there is no distinction between the two choices, but for spin-dependent TMDPDFs there are physical consequences associated with the direction of gauge-links. Second, there exists a new type of divergence associated with the infinitely-long light-like gauge-links. These divergences are due to radiation of gluons collinear to the light-like gauge-link and cannot be regularized by the standard UV regulators. An example is the following integral in dimensional regularization (DR)citebib.bib177, I=вҲ«рқ‘‘k+вҖӢрқ‘‘kвҲ’вҖӢfвҖӢ(k+вҖӢkвҲ’)(k+вҖӢkвҲ’)1+Пө=12вҖӢвҲ«dвҖӢyyвҖӢвҲ«рқ‘‘m2вҖӢfвҖӢ(m2)m2+2вҖӢПө,рқҗјdifferential-dsuperscriptрқ‘ҳdifferential-dsuperscriptрқ‘ҳрқ‘“superscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳ1italic-Пө12рқ‘‘рқ‘Ұрқ‘Ұdifferential-dsuperscriptрқ‘ҡ2рқ‘“superscriptрқ‘ҡ2superscriptрқ‘ҡ22italic-ПөI=int dk+dk-fracf(k+k-)(k+k-)1+epsilon=frac12intfracdyyint dm2fracf(m2)m2+2epsilon,, where m2=k+вҖӢkвҲ’superscriptрқ‘ҡ2superscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳm2=k+k- and y=k+/kвҲ’рқ‘Ұsuperscriptрқ‘ҳsuperscriptрқ‘ҳy=k+/k- is the rapidity-related variable. The divergences in yрқ‘Ұy arise from large and small yрқ‘Ұy where the integral is unregulated. The contribution from k+=0superscriptрқ‘ҳ0k+=0 is called the light-zero mode in LFQ, where it is also called light-cone divergence which causes considerable problems. To regulate the light-cone or rapidity divergences, a number of methods have been introduced in the literature (for a review seecitebib.bib177). They can be put into two classes: on-light-cone regulators and off-light-cone regulators. In the former case, the gauge-links are kept along the light-cone direction nОјsuperscriptрқ‘ӣрқңҮnmu after regularization. For example, the so-called Оҙрқӣҝdelta regulatorcitebib.bib182, bib.bib183 regularizes the gauge-link as: WnвҖӢ(Оҫ)вҶ’WnвҖӢ(Оҫ)|ОҙвҲ’вҶ’subscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣрқңүevaluated-atsubscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘ӣрқңүsuperscriptрқӣҝdisplaystyle Wn(xi)rightarrow Wn(xi)|delta- =рқ’«вҖӢexpвҖӢ[вҲ’iвҖӢgвҖӢвҲ«0вҲ’вҲһрқ‘‘О»вҖӢA+вҖӢ(Оҫ+О»вҖӢn)вҖӢeвҲ’ОҙвҲ’2вҖӢp+вҖӢ|О»|],absentрқ’«expdelimited-рқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptsubscript0differential-dрқңҶsuperscriptрқҗҙрқңүрқңҶрқ‘ӣsuperscriptрқ‘’superscriptрқӣҝ2superscriptрқ‘қрқңҶdisplaystyle=cal Prm expleft[-igint0-inftydlambda A+(xi+lambda n)e-fracdelta-2p+|lambda|right],, and similarly for the conjugate direction. The Оҙрқӣҝdelta regulator breaks gauge-invariance, but preserves the boost invariance ОҙВұвҶ’eВұYвҖӢОҙВұвҶ’superscriptрқӣҝplus-or-minussuperscriptрқ‘’plus-or-minusрқ‘Ңsuperscriptрқӣҝplus-or-minusdeltapmrightarrow epm Ydeltapm where Yрқ‘ҢY is the rapidity of the Lorentz boost. Other on-light-cone regulators include the exponential regulatorcitebib.bib323, О·рқңӮeta regulatorcitebib.bib125, analytical regulatorcitebib.bib51, etc. In the remainder of this section, we will use the Оҙрқӣҝdelta regulator as a representative whenever we need an on-light-cone regulator. The off-light-cone regulator was introduced in citebib.bib141, bib.bib264, bib.bib263, bib.bib132, and also used in citebib.bib264. This type of regulator chooses off-light-cone directions to avoid the rapidity divergence. One can choose, for instance, to deform the gauge-links into the space-like region: nвҶ’nY=nвҲ’eвҲ’2вҖӢYвҖӢp(p+)2. вҶ’рқ‘ӣsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘Ңрқ‘ӣsuperscriptрқ‘’2рқ‘Ңрқ‘қsuperscriptsuperscriptрқ‘қ2displaystyle nrightarrow nY=n-e-2Yfracp(p+)2,. Here Yрқ‘ҢY plays the role of a rapidity regulator, as when YвҶ’вҲһвҶ’рқ‘ҢYrightarrowinfty, nYвҶ’nвҶ’subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘Ңрқ‘ӣnYrightarrow n. In certain cases one can also deform nYsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘ҢnY into time-like regioncitebib.bib138. The on-light-cone regulators are consistent with the spirit of parton physics, and therefore are useful to define COM-momentum-independent parton densities. The off-light-cone regulators, on the other hand, follow a similar spirit as LaMET, and therefore can be exploited for practical lattice QCD calculations, as we shall see in the next subsection. To avoid light-cone divergences, from now on we include the rapidity regulator in the definition of the light-cone TMDPDFs. Using the same label fрқ‘“f for the TMDPDFs in both momentum and coordinate spaces, we have fвҖӢ(О»,bвҹӮ,Ој,ОҙвҲ’/P+)рқ‘“рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮsuperscriptрқӣҝsuperscriptрқ‘ғdisplaystyle f(lambda,bperp,mu,delta-/P+) =вҹЁP|вҖӢПҲВҜвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn/2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢnМёвҖӢрқ’ІnвҖӢ(О»вҖӢn/2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)|ОҙвҲ’вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(вҲ’О»вҖӢn/2)вҖӢ|Pвҹ©,absentevaluated-atbraрқ‘ғВҜрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toitalic-nМёsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣрқңҶрқ‘ӣ2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tosuperscriptрқӣҝрқң“рқңҶрқ‘ӣ2ketрқ‘ғdisplaystyle= P|barpsi(lambda n/2+vecbperp)notncal Wn(lambda n/2+vecbperp)|delta-psi(-lambda n/2)|P,, where ОјрқңҮmu is the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS scale for UV renormalization. To define the physical TMDPDF, we need to remove all divergences and rapidity regularization scheme dependencies in fрқ‘“f, in a way similar to removing UV divergences in physical quantities. The rapidity divergence for TMDPDFs can be removed by the soft function, which also plays an important role in TMD factorization. Intuitively, the soft function represents a cross section for fast-moving charged particles emitting soft gluons into final states. It has rapidity divergence associated with the light-cone direction, which is ultimately related to the mass singularity. The О¶рқңҒzeta-dependence comes from the initial-state quark radiation and is intrinsically nonperturbative for large bвҹӮsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tobperp. fTMDвҖӢ(x,bвҹӮ,Ој,О¶)superscriptрқ‘“TMDрқ‘Ҙsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮрқңҒfrm TMD(x,bperp,mu,zeta) is the standard object to be matched to in LaMET. We should emphasize that although fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD is free from rapidity divergences, it does contain soft radiation from the charged particles in the initial state. This can be seen clearly by considering Feynman diagrams for the unsubtracted fрқ‘“f and applying soft approximation to gluons. вҖңOne-halfвҖқ of the soft contribution in fрқ‘“f is subtracted to define the physical fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD due to the requirement of factorization of physical processes. The remaining soft radiation also has a natural rapidity cut-off associated with lnвҒЎ(xвҖӢP+)рқ‘Ҙsuperscriptрқ‘ғln(xP+), reflected in the О¶рқңҒzeta-dependence. What is remarkable, however, is that fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD is rapidity-regulator independent. Although a general proof to all orders in perturbation theory is beyond the scope of this review, it is due to factorization and exponentiation of the soft physics in fрқ‘“f and thus the scheme cancellation can be done systematically in the exponent. It worth mentioning that in old-fashioned or SCET-like approaches, one can define the вҖңsubtractedвҖқ TMDPDF or вҖңbeams functionsвҖқ that contains only collinear physics. However, they are generically scheme dependent and must be combined with an extra soft functions in factorization theorems. content_sections.V Transverse-Momentum Dependent PDFs V.2: Before LaMET, there had been efforts to access TMD physics from lattice QCD by calculating the ratios of the xрқ‘Ҙx-moments of TMDPDFscitebib.bib222, bib.bib375, bib.bib374, bib.bib191, bib.bib449, which are free from complications associated with the soft function and can be compared to certain experimental observables. In LaMET, we are more interested in obtaining the full xрқ‘Ҙx and kвҶ’вҹӮsubscriptвҶ’рқ‘ҳperpendicular-toveckperp dependence of the TMDPDFscitebib.bib279, bib.bib271, bib.bib176, bib.bib177, bib.bib273, bib.bib272. Therefore, a proper treatment of the soft function subtraction and matching is essential. The earliest suggestion of a bent soft function incitebib.bib279 and the follow-up workcitebib.bib177 has the correct IR logarithms at one-loop order, but this is expected to break down at higher-loop orderscitebib.bib273, thus not allowing for a perturbative matching. Another suggestion which uses a naive rectangle-shaped Wilson loopcitebib.bib271, bib.bib177 does not possess the correct IR physics, either. Nevertheless, incitebib.bib176 important progress was made for calculating the nonperturbative Collins-Soper kernel KвҖӢ(bвҹӮ,Ој)рқҗҫsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮK(bperp,mu) from the ratio of quasi-TMDPDFs at two different large momenta. Recently, some of the authors showedcitebib.bib273, bib.bib272 that the quasi-TMDPDF combined with a reduced soft function capture the correct IR physics to all-orders and thus allow for a perturbative matching to the physical TMDPDF. To construct such quasi-TMDPDFs, the collinear part can be treated in a way similar to the collinear PDFs, while the soft piece is more challenging. Our starting point is that the physical fTMDsuperscriptрқ‘“TMDfrm TMD is independent of the rapidity regulator, so one can use a scheme in which the gauge-links in both fрқ‘“f and Sрқ‘ҶS are off the light-cone, such as that used incitebib.bib132. In this case, one can use Lorentz symmetry to boost the staple-shaped gauge-link рқ’Іnsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣcal Wn in fрқ‘“f to a purely space-like staple with no time dependence. However, one can only use this trick for one of the staples in Sрқ‘ҶS, say рқ’Іnsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘ӣcal Wn, whereas the other one рқ’Іpsubscriptрқ’Ірқ‘қcal Wp is still time-dependent. In other words, there is no way to get rid of the time dependence in Sрқ‘ҶS entirely with Lorentz boost alone. This is natural because Sрқ‘ҶS in fact represents the square of an Sрқ‘ҶS-matrix, which appears to be intrinsically Minkowskian. However, using the LaMET principle that time dependence of an operator can be simulated through external physical states at large momentum, we find that Sрқ‘ҶS can indeed be calculated on the lattice in the off-light-cone scheme as a form factor. A detailed discussion will be given in the next subsection. Here we assume that this is true, and discuss the matching between quasi- and physical TMDPDFs. First, we define the quasi-TMDPDF with a stapleвҖ“shaped gauge-link along the zрқ‘§z directioncitebib.bib279, bib.bib271, bib.bib177, bib.bib272 as fвҖӢ(О»,bвҹӮ,Ој,О¶z)рқ‘“рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮsubscriptрқңҒрқ‘§displaystyletildef(lambda,bperp,mu,zetaz) =limLвҶ’вҲһвҹЁP|ПҲВҜвҖӢ(О»вҖӢnz2+bвҶ’вҹӮ)вҖӢОіzвҖӢрқ’ІzвҖӢ(О»вҖӢnz2+bвҶ’вҹӮ;L)вҖӢПҲвҖӢ(вҲ’О»вҖӢnz2)|Pвҹ©ZEвҖӢ(2вҖӢL,bвҹӮ,Ој),absentsubscriptвҶ’рқҗҝquantum-operator-productрқ‘ғВҜрқң“рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-tosuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§subscriptрқ’Ірқ‘§рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§2subscriptвҶ’рқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқҗҝрқң“рқңҶsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§2рқ‘ғsubscriptрқ‘Қрқҗё2рқҗҝsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮdisplaystyle=!limLrightarrowinftyfrac P|barpsibig(fraclambda nz2!+!vecbperpbig)gammazcal Wz(fraclambda nz2!+!vecbperp;L)psibig(!-!fraclambda nz2big)|PsqrtZE(2L,bperp,mu) , where the MSВҜВҜMSoverlinerm MS renormalization is implied, and рқ’ІzвҖӢ(Оҫ;L)=WzвҖ вҖӢ(Оҫ;L)вҖӢWвҹӮвҖӢWzвҖӢ(вҲ’ОҫzвҖӢnz;L),subscriptрқ’Ірқ‘§рқңүрқҗҝsubscriptsuperscriptрқ‘ҠвҖ рқ‘§рқңүрқҗҝsubscriptрқ‘Ҡperpendicular-tosubscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘§superscriptрқңүрқ‘§subscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§рқҗҝdisplaystylecal Wz(xi;L)=Wdaggerz(xi;L)WperpWz(-xiznz;L) , WzвҖӢ(Оҫ;L)=рқ’«вҖӢexpвҖӢ[вҲ’iвҖӢgвҖӢвҲ«ОҫzLрқ‘‘О»вҖӢnzвӢ…AвҖӢ(ОҫвҶ’вҹӮ+nzвҖӢО»)]. subscriptрқ‘Ҡрқ‘§рқңүрқҗҝрқ’«expdelimited-рқ‘–рқ‘”superscriptsubscriptsuperscriptрқңүрқ‘§рқҗҝвӢ…differential-dрқңҶsubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§рқҗҙsubscriptвҶ’рқңүperpendicular-tosubscriptрқ‘ӣрқ‘§рқңҶdisplaystyle Wz(xi;L)=cal Prm expBig[-igintxizLdlambda nzcdot A(vecxiperp!+!nzlambda)Big] . Again, Оіzsuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘§gammaz can be replaced by Оіtsuperscriptрқӣҫрқ‘Ўgammat as in the collinear quasi-PDF. For a depiction of fрқ‘“tildef and ZEsubscriptрқ‘ҚрқҗёZE see Fig. . The purpose of the factor ZEsubscriptрқ‘ҚрқҗёZE is as follows. At large LрқҗҝL, the naive quasi-TMD correlator in the numerator of Eq. contains divergences that go as eвҲ’LвҖӢEвҖӢ(bвҹӮ,Ој)superscriptрқ‘’рқҗҝрқҗёsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮe-LE(bperp,mu) where EвҖӢ(bвҹӮ)рқҗёsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toE(bperp) is the ground state energy of a pair of static heavy-quarks. EвҖӢ(bвҹӮ,Ој)=2вҖӢОҙвҖӢm+VвҖӢ(bвҹӮ,Ој)рқҗёsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮ2рқӣҝрқ‘ҡрқ‘үsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮE(bperp,mu)=2delta m+V(bperp,mu) contains both the linear divergent mass corrections 2вҖӢОҙвҖӢm2рқӣҝрқ‘ҡ2delta m and the heavy-quark potential VвҖӢ(bвҹӮ,Ој)рқ‘үsubscriptрқ‘Ҹperpendicular-toрқңҮV(bperp,mu) due to mutual interactions. The introduction of ZEsubscriptрқ‘ҚрқҗёsqrtZE also removes additional contributions from the transverse gauge link. An alternative approach to avoid the pinch-pole singularity was proposed incitebib.bib321. We should mention that although the ZEsubscriptрқ‘ҚрқҗёsqrtZE subtraction removes all the linear divergences, the logarithmic UV divergences are still present.
